title: 12 Million Queries, 2,500 Users: How Siemens Energy Scales AI with Alteryx
abstract: Siemens Energy needed to scale analytics across a global enterprise — not just access but governance and performance across more than 2,500 users and 12 million queries a year. Learn how it built an architecture where Alteryx pushes analytics directly into Snowflake, eliminating extraction and keeping compute where the data lives. The centerpiece: custom Alteryx workflows that call Snowflake Cortex AI natively, embedding generative AI into production pipelines without rebuilding workflows or bypassing governance. Walk away with a concrete model for scaling self-service analytics, integrating Cortex AI through Alteryx, and giving business teams a path from data to AI-driven decisions.

speakers.job_title: Data Science & ML Expert | Alteryx ACE
speakers.bio: Alex started his career as a consultant to help customer build BI solutions and implement self-service analytics solutions. He switched to Siemens Energy in 2023 and is an expert in topics around Alteryx, Snowflake and the Siemens Energy BI landscape. He has built an scaled solutions to thousands of users and is a frontrunner in solutions that utilise cortex.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: 2026 Data Driver Awards
abstract: The Data Drivers Awards is an annual awards program celebrating Snowflake customers’ (individuals and organizations) that are pushing the boundaries, driving innovation and delivering impact within their business and industry — using the AI Data Cloud.

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

title: 20-Minute Threat Hunt: Building and Deploying a Cybersecurity Agent with Cortex
abstract:
Stop building custom data pipelines for AI. Start solving threats in minutes. This session covers the end-to-end process to rapidly deploy a production-ready, investigative generative AI agent for cybersecurity using Snowflake Intelligence:

  1. Unifying security logs (access, audit, vulns)
  2. Using Semantic Views to apply governance and context with Snowflake Horizon Catalog
  3. Configuring the agent with specialized tools like Cortex Analyst (for structured data queries) and Cortex Search (for unstructured threat intelligence)

See a live demo of the agent performing a complex threat hunt, all running securely inside your security perimeter.

speakers.bio:
Vince Luongo is a cybersecurity architect at Snowflake, specializing in building and scaling cybersecurity use cases on modern data platforms. He holds a degree in Cybersecurity from Penn State and brings over seven years of experience as a security consultant, working across both public sector and commercial organizations.

With a background in security engineering and governance, risk, and compliance (GRC), Vince has helped organizations strengthen their security posture and operationalize risk management. Today, he focuses on helping teams leverage data to enhance threat detection, streamline compliance, and modernize security operations on the Snowflake platform.

Jake is Snowflake's Cybersecurity Field CTO and leads Snowflake's Cybersecurity Data Cloud. At Snowflake, Jake's mission is to evangelize and enable the implementation of modern security analytics and engineering. Prior to joining Snowflake, Jake has had a diverse background of technical and leadership roles having most recently served as Co-Founder and CTO of a Cloud Consulting and Data Intelligence company. He regularly maintains his experience and interests in the areas of cloud, devops and development and is an active outdoorsman and nature enthusiast.

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: 3 Essentials for Building Winning AI Projects
abstract:
Many AI projects fail not because of models but because the data lacks context. AI success relies on knowing where data lives, what it means and whether it is fresh. Without enterprise context, applications and agents fail. Join Cloudera and Sanjeev Mohan, Analyst at SanjMo, to explore three essentials to give AI the context it needs:

  • Connected data across your ecosystem
  • Trusted governance for secure access
  • Real-time pipelines for fresh knowledge

Learn how Cloudera and Snowflake provide the data foundation to discover and securely access the right data. Move from isolated experiments to AI projects that deliver measurable, business-ready outcomes.

speakers.bio:
Sanjeev Mohan is a recognized thought leader in cloud technologies, data architectures, analytics, and artificial intelligence. With a keen focus on emerging trends and technologies, Sanjeev hosts It Depends podcast and authors regular Medium blogs. He is also the author of Data Product for Dummies.

Formerly a Vice President at Gartner, Sanjeev was renowned for his in-depth research and strategic insights, shaping the research agenda for data and analytics globally. He now leads SanjMo, a consultancy specializing in technical advisory services that elevate category and brand awareness for clients.

speakers.job_title: Director Product Marketing, Data and AI
speakers.bio:
Navita Sood is a seasoned Data and AI leader dedicated to helping global enterprises build a strong data foundation to power their analytics and AI innovations. As the Director of Product Marketing at Cloudera, Navita guides customers on their data strategy and the adoption of emerging technologies.

With over 15 years of experience across the enterprise software landscape—including leadership roles at Oracle, IBM, Micro Focus, and Avaya—her expertise spans the entire data lifecycle, cloud services, Generative AI and autonomous Agents. She is a trusted voice in bridging the gap between complex technology and business value. She holds an MBA from HEC Paris and a B.E. in Computer Science.

title: 80x Faster Data Engineering: From Prompts to Pipelines with Maia
abstract: What happens when complex data engineering work can be delegated to AI agents? In this session, Jason Mulvin, Director of Enterprise Data & Analytics at Sophos, shares how his team designs pipelines through prompting — accelerating development, testing and deployment. Sophos uses agents to generate pipelines, convert legacy workflows and analyze existing ones. In one example, a pipeline that would take five days was generated and validated in under 30 minutes, achieving 95-99% completion and producing automated tests. Sophos is now reviewing 1,400+ pipelines to identify optimizations and inconsistencies. This session shows how prompts translate into production pipelines.

speakers.bio: Joe Herbert is a Principal Solution Architect at Maia, at the forefront of the go-to-market strategy for their new agentic AI and virtual data engineers. With a rich background in applying data science to solve real-world challenges, including optimizing operations as a data scientist at Edinburgh Airport, Joe brings a unique perspective on the evolution of data engineering. He is passionate about empowering organizations to leverage autonomous AI agents to drive business value and innovation.

title: Accelerate and Automate: Mastering Snowflake ML with Cortex Code
abstract: Experience the next level of MLE productivity. This session provides a deep dive into Cortex Code, a massive accelerator designed to automate Snowflake ML pipelines from ingestion to production. We will demonstrate how Cortex Code meets machine learning engineers in their preferred environments to simplify the full lifecycle of enterprise models. Whether you are looking to reduce technical debt or scale your ML operations, join us to see how to build faster, smarter and more resilient pipelines at scale.

speakers.job_title: Sr Manager, Applied AI Field Engineering
speakers.bio: Caleb is a Sr Manager in the AI/ML Applied Field Engineering office at Snowflake, and formerly a Principal Architect on the team. He is the global field lead for the Snowflake ML ecosystem and manages Snowflake's team of ML Applied Field Engineers. His background is in theoretical mathematics and philosophy. Prior to Snowflake, he was a practicing data scientist and machine learning engineer primarily in the defense and intelligence contracting industry. Caleb is based out of Boulder, CO.

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

title: Accelerating AI Readiness: Data to Real-Time Answers with Snowflake Intelligence
abstract: This session shows how we accelerated AI readiness in manufacturing by placing Snowflake Intelligence at the center of our data platform. We ingest real-time data from enterprise and mainframe systems using Snowpipe Streaming and Kafka to enable low-latency operational visibility. Pipelines are orchestrated with dbt, with Dynamic Tables automating CDC and delivering near real-time datasets. As streaming scaled, we applied Dynamic Table best practices and Gen2 warehouses to control costs. We conclude by demonstrating how Snowflake Intelligence, powered by semantic models and Cortex Analyst, enables teams to ask real-time operational questions and move beyond dashboards.

speakers.bio:
Senior Solutions Architect | Data Strategist at Snowflake with 20+ years in the data world. One question drives everything I do: which data creates value, and which is just noise?

I partner with customers to design AI, data, and digital transformation roadmaps — turning silos into actionable intelligence, embedding analytics and GenAI where they matter, and aligning data strategy with business goals to deliver measurable value.

What gets me up in the morning is seeing customers move from drowning in data to making confident, data-driven decisions.

Data Strategy · Solutions Architecture · AI & Digital Transformation · Customer-Centric Design

Venu Mashetty is a Data & AI Engineering Lead at Daimler Truck North America with over 10 years of experience building enterprise data platforms and AI solutions. He leads a development team delivering scalable data and AI architectures across multiple business domains. Venu has extensive experience with SAP and modern cloud data platforms and has supported Snowflake implementations enabling real-time data pipelines, governed data platforms, and enterprise analytics. His team is also developing Generative AI solutions, including RAG-based AI assistants powered by Snowflake Cortex and the Snowflake AI Data Cloud.

title: Accelerating AI-Ready Data with Open Semantic Interchange
abstract: As data, BI and AI converge, enterprises need a shared semantic foundation that works across tools, models, and workloads. Explore how the Open Semantic Interchange (OSI) creates a vendor-neutral semantic standard that lets BI, analytics and AI tools speak the same language for reliable, consistent data interpretation. This session will explore how OSI supports dimensional BI models, enabling a durable way to define semantics once and use them everywhere. Learn how this standard insulates your models from schema drift and accelerates cross-system integration in Snowflake, ensuring your traditional BI dashboards and new AI applications always agree on the numbers.

speakers.bio: Josh Klahr is the Head of Analytics Product Management at Snowflake, where he is responsible for driving the core database product roadmap and strategy. Josh has over 25 years of experience working with data and analytics. Prior to joining Snowflake, Josh was VP of product management for Splunk, where he was responsible for the Splunk Enterprise and Splunk Cloud product lines. He has also spent time in product management roles at Yahoo!, Pivotal Software, Greenplum, and AtScale.
Yogitha, a Senior Product Manager at Snowflake drives the strategy and implementation of Snowflake SQL and Advanced Analytic features, like time-series analytics.

title: Accelerating T.Rowe Price’s Data Product Strategy with Modern MDM on Snowflake
abstract: As T.Rowe Price grew its $1.78 trillion asset management business with new solutions, partners and enhanced digital + AI capabilities, the legacy master data management (MDM) became a bottleneck. The “trust and latency” gap eroded confidence from business and executives while it increased TCO due to complex ETL pipelines, manual rework and unmanageable technical debt. In this session, T.Rowe Price will share their data product strategy vision and journey of migrating legacy MDM into a cloud-native “Party Master 360” using Semarchy on Snowflake.

speakers.job_title: Head of Enterprise Data Solution - Cheif Data Office
speakers.bio: Sri is responsible for formulation, innovation, and execution of AI & Data solutions for the Enterprise in enabling data as a key business value driver at T Rowe Price.  Sri built modern Data Lakehouse platform at T Rowe Price providing scale and agility to the business. Sri previously worked at Toyota Motors, Experian, CoreLogic, and Credit Sesame, focusing on Automotive, Consumer, Business Credit, and Real Estate markets. Sri's expertise is in Innovation, Transformation, and M&A in the data & technology space. He owns six US patents.  Sri has led several large transformation initiatives leveraging Data and AI/ML as a key differentiator, yielding top-line business growth.

title: Achieving Agentic AI ROI with Snowflake: Is Your Data Ready?
abstract: As organizations build AI agents and autonomous workflows on Snowflake, many struggle to turn AI investment into real ROI. The challenge isn’t the AI — it’s the data. A growing gap in data trust limits what autonomous systems can reliably deliver. In this session, we break down the six data challenges that hold agentic AI back — and discuss what it takes to prepare your Snowflake data for agent-driven outcomes. You’ll also see a demonstration of how Precisely is applying AI-driven automation and AI agents — delivered through interoperable services, APIs and data — to help accelerate the path to agentic-ready data for AI powered by Snowflake.

speakers.bio:
Trey Wood helps enterprises unlock the value of trusted data through comprehensive, multi-product solutioning and storytelling. With a degree in Management Information Systems, he began his career in consulting, implementing Master Data Automation workflows for core business processes and supporting the development and maturation of enterprise Data Governance programs. 
 
Drawn to the creative side of solutioning, Trey shifted into presales to blend technical depth with narrative clarity. He now brings a consultative, outcomes-first approach to every engagement—focusing on finding the right hammers for the right nails, rather than fitting every challenge to a single tool.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

title: A Computation-First Architecture for Real-Time ML Feature Stores
abstract: Snowflake has become the backbone for model development and analytical workloads. As production ML systems increasingly depend on signals that change continuously and live outside warehouse tables, traditional feature store architectures built around prematerialized pipelines introduce latency, duplication and training inference drift. In this talk, Chalk's Co-Founder, Elliot Marx, unveils a computation-first feature store architecture where Chalk extends Snowflake into real-time decision systems. By executing Python feature definitions directly against warehouse and live data sources, this ensures that consistency and freshness are properties of the system rather than downstream optimizations.

speakers.bio: Before founding Chalk, the data platform for AI and ML, Elliot previously built the data engineering platform for fraud at Affirm and later sold the company he founded to Credit Karma, where he worked on building out their ML infrastructure systems. He’s spent the last decade working on production ML systems that operate under real latency, scale, and correctness constraints.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

title: Activating Enterprise Audience Intelligence with Snowflake Intelligence
abstract: Acxiom receives inbound customer feeds that are transformed into a trusted, activation-ready Gold layer in Snowflake, enriched and identity-resolved into a governed customer/entity view, and protected with encrypted sensitive data controls. The business challenge isn’t whether we store and govern the data, it’s how fast leaders can turn data into decisions that improve campaign ROI while reducing risk. In this session, we show how Snowflake Intelligence enables marketers, analytics leads and executives to interact with governed Gold data in plain language to get board-ready answers and recommended actions without waiting on backlogs or writing SQL.

speakers.bio: Technical architect expert in implementing scalable solutions leveraging Snowflake.

Jain is responsible for Acxiom’s product and data engineering transformation. Bringing over 20 years of leadership expertise in big data, analytics, AI, machine learning, and cloud computing, Jain joined Acxiom from Merkle, where he led the company’s cloud-first strategy as its Global Cloud Practice Lead.

Note: This session is currently open to Marketer's Forum registrants. Registration will open to the general event audience closer to the event.

title: Agentic Data Access: Booking.com’s Agentic Travel Experience, Secured by Immuta
abstract: AI agents are becoming top-level data consumers, and they need governed access at machine speed. In this session, Booking.com and Immuta share how Booking is extending Snowflake to support both humans and agents across structured and unstructured data in Snowflake and S3. You’ll learn how policy-driven provisioning, request-based workflows and an on-behalf-of access model help scale secure, auditable access without slowing down analytics or AI innovation.

speakers.bio: I am the CTO of Immuta where I get to fulfill my love for data and its power to solve our customer’s greatest challenges.  Data has been the foundation of my career since the beginning, be it creating algorithms for Special Operations Command (SOCOM) to writing some of the very first MapReduce analytics at NSA, to building a secure application engine for customers with some of the most complex data policies on the planet.  Prior to co-founding Immuta I served as CTO for 42six Solutions, a services company focused on data-intensive custom application development, acquired by Computer Sciences Corp (CSC).  I hold a BS from the University of Maryland

speakers.job_title: Group Product Manager - Data & AI Platform
speakers.bio: Abhro currently leads product development for multiple foundational platform products powering Booking.com's next generation Data & AI platform. Abhro and his teams leads the distributed compute services powering ETL / ELT batch and streaming workloads on the platform, distributed open storage, IAM,  data & AI prodduct discovery marketplace and Privacy. With a background in engineering, over the last 15 years Abhro has had the opportunity to essay multiple IC and leadership roles with AWS, the BBVA Group and with global players in the capital markets, BFSI, manufacturing and now travel space, building distributed data & ML products addressing real world challenges.

title: Agentic Procurement: Accelerating Drug Development with Autonomous Procurement
abstract: We’re accelerating time to market for new medicines by removing enterprise friction. Learn how we’re transforming procurement by combining Elementum AI workflows and agent orchestration with Snowflake to reinvent source‑to‑pay, freeing scientists and clinical teams to focus on life‑saving research. We’ll show how agentic intelligence moves beyond rules‑based bots to interpret policy, resolve exceptions and use near real‑time signals to drive faster cycle times, fewer touchpoints, smarter buying and aligned metrics. We’ll cover the journey, design principles, operating model changes and ways to scale responsibly toward autonomous enterprise operations.

speakers.bio:
AI & Data leader at Deloitte specializing in large-scale, AI-enabled transformations for B2C organizations across Life Sciences, e-Commerce, Telecom, and Banking.
With 15 years in consulting, he has led both strategic programs for global enterprises and complex multi-year implementations delivering measurable business value. Passionate about applying Agentic AI capabilities to drive the next generation of enterprise innovation.

title: Agent Monday: Automated, Personalized Shopper Insights at Scale
abstract: What if every stakeholder received curated, actionable shopper insights — automatically? Meet Agent Monday, an AI-powered agent that synthesizes behavioral data and delivers tailored insights directly to inboxes. Explore the architecture, personalization logic and governance guardrails that turn raw retail data into decision-ready intelligence at scale.

speakers.bio:
Emily Gibbons is the Head of Product for Kroger Precision Marketing, where she leads the development of a $1B+ portfolio across retail media, insights, and loyalty. She focuses on bringing data, analytics, and activation together into unified products that help brands drive measurable growth.

With a background spanning both product and data science, Emily has spent her career building and scaling data-driven platforms and leading high-performing, cross-functional teams. She’s particularly passionate about turning complex analytics into practical, real-world solutions—and about creating products that actually get used.

Note: This session is currently open to Marketer's Forum registrants. Registration will open to the general event audience closer to the event.

title: Agent-to-Agent Data Supply Chains: Delivering Data at Machine Scale
abstract: Snowflake Intelligence provides answers quickly, but data availability latency is the limiting factor. Traditional platforms analyze existing data, they don't respond when new data is needed. When an analyst or AI needs data not in the warehouse, manual source integration, pipeline building and transformation adaptation introduce delays AI can't bypass. This session reveals agent-to-agent coordination: Snowflake Intelligence spots data gaps, and Maia operates upstream to discover sources, auto-generate pipelines, reconcile schemas and deliver queryable datasets in minutes, without manual work.

speakers.bio: Maia's not just following the AI trend—we’re defining it. I’m incredibly proud to be part of the team building the future of AI Data Automation. As the data stack evolves, #Maia is stepping up as the de facto leader to help enterprises build data products without limits

Arawan Gajajiva is a Principal Architect at Matillion with over 20 years of experience in the data and cloud technology sector. A seasoned technical leader, Arawan has spent two decades helping organizations across finance, healthcare, and retail navigate the complexities of modern data architecture. At Matillion, he specializes in developing scalable ETL solutions and cloud-native data patterns that empower businesses to turn raw data into actionable insights. A frequent contributor to technical thought leadership, Arawan is passionate about simplifying complex data workflows and leveraging AI for real-world business intelligence.

title: AI Agents and Forward Deployed Engineering for Commercial Decision Intelligence
abstract: Learn about Jarvis, Sanofi's AI sales adviser, bridging the gap between leadership strategy and field execution to drive a $1.5 billion annual revenue uplift. Powered by Snowflake Cortex Code, Cortex Search, Cortex Agents and SPCS, this Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) engagement enables Sanofi to scale their commercial field force effectiveness agent across the globe for more than 10,000 employees. The system uses a hub-and-spoke model for global scale for deterministic compliance, ensuring data-driven clinical influence with zero human-in-the-loop.

speakers.bio: Dimitrije is responsible for Sanofi's stance on digital transformation and ecosystems in the field of digital, data and AI, as well as overseeing strategy and transformation. He manages the digital portfolio, strategy and all AI Workflows at Sanofi.

Note: This session is currently open to Marketer's Forum registrants. Registration will open to the general event audience closer to the event.

title: AI Agents, Snowflake and Domo: Running a Product Team of One
abstract: In this session, we show how a single developer built a full production application by combining AI agents, Snowflake and Domo. Snowflake powers the intelligence layer — ML scoring, Cortex AI Functions and data pipelines — while Domo provides the application framework and user experience for the business. Using AI agents as engineering teammates, the project followed a structured development process with shaping, sprints and iteration to deliver a deal intelligence platform in just weeks. Learn the operating model, architecture and development pattern that enables developers to turn Snowflake data into real AI-powered business applications.

speakers.bio: Dan Wentworth is a technology leader serving as the Manager of Solutions Engineering for Technology Partnerships at Domo. He specializes in data management, predictive analytics, and architecting innovative solutions across the data lifecycle. With a strong background in AI and machine learning from his time at Pluralsight and Big Squid (acquired by Qlik), Dan has a proven record of developing models to solve key business challenges.

title: AI Applied Mastery: Skill-Based AI Engineering Curriculum in the Agentic World
abstract: From knowledge to measurable skills and impact, this session explores how Snowflake Academia and Northeastern University are redefining AI education for the agentic era. We start with imparting AI knowledge and then move on to project-driven mastery, where students solve realistic problems by building knowledge graphs and agentic AI and data engineering workflows on Snowflake’s free-to-academic AI platform. Enhanced by industry guest lecture, mentorship and iterative innovation, this model bridges the gap between classroom and career. Learn how to empower students to architect real-world solutions using contextual engineering and the latest AI skills. The future is skills based; let’s build it.

speakers.bio:
Kishore Aradhya is a senior technology leader with over twenty-five years of experience developing and scaling technology organizations, from startups to Fortune 500 companies. He currently leads Data and AI initiatives at Frontdoor Inc., a tech-enabled leader in home warranty services. He has held senior Data/Analytics leadership positions at Stanley Black & Decker, Bose, Adobe, edX/MIT, Staples, and various other startups.
He also serves as adjunct faculty at Northeastern University, teaching graduate classes in Data Engineering and Knowledge Graphs, serving on the CDO Magazine Editorial Board, and held Industry/Product Advisory roles at early-stage startups.

Seeling  is an experienced software professional specializing in distributed data management systems and big data technologies. As Head of Snowflake's Academia program, she leads efforts to democratize AI education for university students worldwide. With deep expertise in educating and enabling users to leverage technology effectively, Seeling is also passionate about mentoring and contributing to non-profit education initiatives, empowering youth and women with hands-on technical career development skills. She holds a Bachelor's and Master's degree in Computer Science from Florida Atlantic University.

title: AI-Guided Migration: From Manual Rewrites to Automation
abstract: Manual migration rewrites consume valuable engineering time. SnowConvert AI shifts the focus from labor-intensive code conversion to intelligent automation and orchestration. This session highlights innovations including Semantic Conversion (AST + Cortex AI) for high-fidelity SQL translation, AI-driven verification to resolve complex issues, automated data validation for schema and metrics parity, ETL conversion to dbt on Snowflake, and ecosystem repointing for tools like Power BI. Learn how AI-guided automation accelerates modernization and frees engineers to build, not fix.

speakers.bio:
Pallavi Sharma is a Senior Data Cloud Architect at Snowflake with over 15 years of experience in the data domain.She brings deep expertise in AI driven data platform migrations, modernization, and data engineering on Snowflake.
Having previously worked as a Snowflake customer and now a partner solution engineer, Pallavi brings a unique perspective across the ecosystem. She currently works closely with global system integrators and solution partners, helping them design migration strategies, build demos, and accelerate modernization programs using initiatives such as SnowConvert AI , Cortex code ,Make your Data AI ready and the LiftOff migration program.

title: AI in Action: Real-World FSI Production Showcases
abstract: Leading financial institutions are already deploying AI to drive measurable ROI. This curated collection showcases the most impactful AI stories currently in production across the industry. Explore how your peers are moving from pilot to scale, leveraging the Snowflake AI Data Cloud to solve complex challenges in risk, customer experience and employee efficiency.

speakers.job_title: Head of Wealth and Asset Management
speakers.bio: Jonathan is the Head of Wealth and Asset Management at Snowflake, an advisor to ZettaQuant AI and a Research Affiliate at Georgia Tech's Financial Services Innovation Lab. He is also the author of the forthcoming book "Large Language Models: The Hard Parts" (O'Reilly 2026). Prior to joining Snowflake, he was the Executive Director of Quantitative Research at Sylvan Road Capital and Head of Quantamental Research at Truist Securities.

title: AI-Powered Analytics and Data Discovery Using Snowflake at Booking.com
abstract: Commercial, product and platform teams — from partnerships, trips and accommodations – are using Snowflake’s AI capabilities to power self-service analytics and data discovery. Built on a multienvironment infrastructure, analytical semantics from multiple business units are integrated with Booking’s data discovery and low-code AI application, enabling teams to ask product, performance and data management contextual questions using natural language in a variety of contexts. Snowflake Intelligence, semantic views and agents are reducing our dependence on dashboards and data tribal knowledge — freeing up analysts’ time for complex experimentation, causal inference and strategic analysis.

speakers.bio: Kiran Kodandoor is a Principal Software Engineer at Booking.com in the Trips organization, where they design and evolve large-scale, data-intensive data platforms that power analytics and AI across the enterprise. Kiran leads initiatives that bring AI-powered self-service analytics into everyday decision-making, combining Snowflake’s cloud data platform with modern engineering practices to unlock faster, trusted, more reliable insights. At Snowflake Summit, Kiran will share how Booking.com is building an AI-driven analytics layer on Snowflake—covering architecture choices, governance, and real-world lessons from driving data democratization at scale.

Paul Neustadt is a Senior Technical Product Manager at Booking.com on a mission to retire the "where is that dataset?" Slack message. As the lead for Asset Discovery, he builds the Knowledge Graphs and AI-powered search tools that help over 2,000 analysts and engineers find what they need without the headache.

Paul’s path into data foundations included building serverless pipelines at trivago and an MBA in South Korea, where he graduated top of his class in Seoul. He untangles the puzzle of fragmented data to make things simpler for the people building with it.

title: AI-Powered Conversations with Your Data Using Amazon Quick and Snowflake via MCP
abstract: The enterprise data landscape is scattered. Teams are trapped in disconnected tools, business users wait days for insights, and engineers build fragile one-off integrations. This hands-on workshop shows how to integrate Amazon Quick MCP actions with Snowflake Cortex AI's MCP server to build AI agents that transform how teams interact with enterprise data. Business users can ask questions such as "What are our top-performing products this quarter?" without writing SQL, while IT maintains security and governance controls. Walk out with working code and production-ready patterns that support self-service analytics across your organization.

speakers.bio: Matt is a Partner Engineer at Snowflake specializing in integrating the Snowflake platform with Microsoft for AI use cases. He has 11 years of experience in Data Science and Machine Learning roles both in consulting and with industry organizations. Matt has worked for companies such as Deloitte and Slalom as a Data Science Consultant and as an AI Architect and Engineer with CSPs and Snowflake. Matt has experience developing and deploying AI and ML models across many different organizations in areas such as marketing, sales, operations, clinical and finance as well as advising in consultative roles.

Nithyashree Alwarsamy is a Partner Solutions Architect at Amazon Web Services, specializing in data and analytics solutions with a focus on Snowflake. With the strong background in cloud architecture and data engineering, Nithyashree works closely with Snowflake and joint customers to design scalable, secure and cost-effective data platforms on AWS. Leveraging deep expertise in modern data architectures, Nithyashree helps organizations unlock the full potential of their data by integrating Snowflake's cloud-native data platform with the breadth of AWS services.

title: AI-Powered FinOps Transformation at Ally Financial
abstract: Ally Financial revolutionized its financial operations (FinOps) strategy by integrating advanced AI capabilities through Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Analyst. This session details how the company moved beyond traditional cost management to implement a proactive, AI-driven optimization framework within the Snowflake platform. We will showcase specific use cases and methodologies where Snowflake Cortex AI Analyst provided actionable insights, leading to improved resource utilization and significant cost savings. Attendees will learn how to leverage these native Snowflake AI features to modernize their own FinOps practices and drive better business outcomes.

speakers.bio: Loren Roberson is a FinOps lead at Ally Bank in Charlotte, NC, overseeing Snowflake cost governance across 13 lines of business, influencing ~$600K/month in compute spend and driving $250K+ in quarterly savings. I partner with engineering teams and executive leadership to optimize performance, build cost allocation models, and evaluate new data technologies. Previously, led end-to-end data ingestion and large-scale migration projects into Snowflake as an Associate Software Engineer at Ally. I hold a Master’s and dual Bachelor’s degrees in MIS and Operations & Supply Chain Management from the University of Arizona. Driven by turning technical depth into measureable business impact.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

title: AI-Powered Modernization: Oregon's Multnomah County Leverages SnowConvert AI
abstract: Public sector organizations are under pressure to modernize legacy systems, control costs and deliver better services for their communities. Multnomah County — home to Portland, Oregon — faced these same challenges as it sought to move away from expensive, aging SQL Server infrastructure. Together with Snowflake, the county leveraged SnowConvert AI to accelerate code conversion and migration, transforming its data operations onto the Snowflake Data Cloud. Join us to hear how this partnership reduced costs, improved scalability and positioned the county to deliver smarter, data-driven services to citizens through Snowflake Intelligence.

speakers.bio: Federico Zoufaly helps Snowflake customers accelerate their adoption of the platform from his position as Director of Migrations and Modernizations @Snowflake. Federico previously served as Co-Founder  of Mobilize.Net. Federico has been a leader in modernizing software applications for almost 30 years.  With a technical background (Ph.D.c. from the University of Florida with a Fulbright scholarship and M.Sc. from the Technology Institute of Costa Rica) and hands-on management in real world technology projects, Federico is passionate about the intersection of the technical challenges and the business need for modernization. Federico is a fellow of the Aspen Global Leadership Network.

Michael is a Portland, Oregon-based Data Architect with more than 30 years of experience in IT, specializing in data warehousing, analytics, and enterprise data modernization. He currently serves as a Snowflake Administrator for Multnomah County, where he leads a large-scale migration to Snowflake and builds out a modern medallion data architecture. He is passionate about using data and AI to drive smarter decisions in both the public sector and beyond. On his personal time he enjoys vibe coding and the beautiful outdoors of the Pacific Northwest.

title: AI Readiness at Siemens: From Governed Semantics to Scalable Agents on Snowflake
abstract: Discover how Siemens built a governed, enterprise-scale AI data stack on Snowflake to securely connect automated agents with core business data. This technical session dives into our version-controlled semantic layer that ensures data consistency, as well as our architecture for extending user-based access control to the AI agents themselves. We'll demonstrate a productive agent that combines "talk to data" with research tasks to automate complex business processes, providing a blueprint for building a scalable and secure foundation for enterprise AI.

speakers.bio: Data architect with focus on data sharing and data agents. Migrating the data setup of a large international company to a cloud based data mesh, making data findable, shareable and reusable through data products. Currently working on scaling data agents in Siemens.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: AI-Ready Data: Quality, Lineage and Root Cause at Scale
abstract: As organizations expand from traditional analytics into generative AI and LLMs, the cost of poor data quality has shifted — from misleading reports to hallucinating models. AI-ready data demands rigorous consistency, freshness and integrity. But as data flows from upstream sources through dbt transformations into downstream BI tools, visibility breaks at every border. In this session, we'll show you how to build a native data quality framework in Snowflake and pair it with cross-platform lineage, so root-cause analysis and impact assessment take minutes, not days.

speakers.bio: Pino Suliman is a Senior Product Manager on Governance team at Snowflake, focusing on data and AI observability.

title: AI Techniques That Boost Social, Marketing and Live Event Performance
abstract: Media and entertainment, marketing and social teams need deeper insight into how audiences engage with video. Snowflake Cortex AI Video Processing enables hook-strength prediction, live-event retention analysis and ad-break optimization, helping organizations reduce drop-off, improve message impact and make smarter content decisions directly in the Snowflake AI Data Cloud.

speakers.bio: Jessie Felix builds AI-powered data products at scale. As a Product Manager on Snowflake’s Cortex AI team, he works on embedding advanced AI capabilities directly into the data cloud, helping enterprises getting value from all data. Previously at AWS, He has led various product management initiatives, driving cost and performance features that powered large-scale data lake workloads. His work has helped organizations optimize petabyte-scale environments while accelerating analytics and AI adoption.

I'm a developer advocate at Snowflake who gets excited about helping developers do cool things with AI and data in the cloud. After a decade of building developer communities at Hume AI, Bill.com, and Clover Network, I found my happy place bridging the gap between complex tech and the developers who want to use it. Think of me as a technical matchmaker – connecting developers with the right tools and watching the magic happen. When I'm not geeking out about AI, you'll find me spending time with my son and collecting new hobbies (because why stick to just one?).

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: This session is currently open to Marketer's Forum registrants. Registration will open to the general event audience closer to the event.

title: Analytics to AI Apps: Democratizing Agentic Workflows with Sigma and Snowflake
abstract:
Stop looking at data and start acting on it. Top organizations empower employees across all functions and levels to build intelligent AI apps that interpret data, automate workflows and drive immediate action.

Join this hands-on lab to see how Sigma connects business users, live analytics and Snowflake Cortex Agents. Using a real-world retail loss prevention scenario, you'll replace legacy reporting with a custom AI app that runs Python machine learning and embeds Snowflake Cortex AI chat agents to investigate fraud in real time.

Walk away with a replicable blueprint for deploying code-free AI apps across your enterprise, no specialized engineering resources required.

speakers.bio: Raj is a Partner SE at Sigma with over 8 years of experience in analytics and solutions engineering, helping organizations unlock the value of their data. He specializes in integrating Sigma with Snowflake to deliver intuitive, business-ready analytics solutions. Rajesh is passionate about bringing agentic capabilities to every business user by leveraging Snowflake Cortex agents within Sigma, enabling teams to move from dashboards to intelligent, AI-driven decision-making. In this session, he’ll guide you through real-world use cases and hands-on exercises that highlight how Cortex agents bring AI directly into the everyday workflows of business users.

title: Apache Iceberg™ v3 Tables in Snowflake: A Practical Guide
abstract: The release of Apache Iceberg™ v3 tables is a major step for open data architectures. But beyond a version number, what does v3 mean when managing an open lakehouse in Snowflake? This session introduces the Iceberg table format, its evolution and the key breakthroughs of v3. We’ll highlight practical benefits — better CDC support, more efficient handling of semi-structured data and improved metadata management — and show how these features help solve real-world lakehouse challenges. Through a series of live demos, you’ll learn how to get started with v3 features in Snowflake so you can hit the ground running with this new format version.

speakers.bio: Danica began her career as a software engineer in financial services and pivoted to developer relations, where she focussed primarily on open source technologies under the Apache Software Foundation umbrella such as Apache Kafka® and Apache Flink®. She now leads the open source advocacy efforts at Snowflake and is involved in the Apache Iceberg™ and Apache Polaris (incubating) communities. She can be found on X (Bluesky and Mastodon), talking about tech, plants, and baking @TheDanicaFine.

With over a decade of experience as a technical developer educator, Gilberto has made complex concepts accessible to over 100,000 students and engineers through both online learning platforms and in-person experiences.


He's worn multiple hats as both product manager and developer educator at developer-loved tech companies, including MongoDB, Domino Data Lab, Plaid, and Snowflake.


Gilberto is passionate about crafting exceptional developer experiences and educational resources. He frequently writes about data engineering, AI, and application development.


Connect with him on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/gilberto-hernandez/

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Apache Polaris in Practice: Open Catalogs, Open Formats, Open Community
abstract: Learn how Apache Polaris is becoming the open catalog layer for every engine and format. Engineers from Snowflake and other organizations will walk through new capabilities including S3-compatible on-prem storage, generic tables, a built-in policy store, audit logs and events, catalog federation and the evolving Polaris REST catalog spec. A customer and a partner will demonstrate how they use and contribute to Polaris, including Delta, Lance format support beyond Iceberg and concrete ways you can get involved.

speakers.bio: Vishwa Lakkundi leads the Open Analytics, Catalog Platform and Catalog Integration Platform teams at Snowflake, driving the strategy for  open data standards. Before joining Snowflake, Vishwa spent over thirteen years at Apple leading engineering for Apple’s Data Lakehouse and Data Orchestration platforms. He has extensive experience building elastic, fully managed Lakehouse platforms, AI infrastructures and large-scale distributed systems.

Lei Xu is the co-founder and CTO of LanceDB.He co-invented the Lance columnar format and LanceDB, a multimodal lakehouse. Prior to LanceDB, he led various data infrastructure and machine learning infrastructure initiatives at Cruise Automation. He also spent several years at Cloudera as a Hadoop HDFS Project Management Committee (PMC) member. Lei holds a PhD in computer engineering, specializing in distributed storage systems.

title: A Practical Guide to Building Marketplace Data Products
abstract: Sharing data is harder than it should be. Permissions tell you who gets access, but they don't help people use the data effectively. For data to be truly valuable, you need to package it right. This session explores practical strategies for transforming raw data sets into compelling marketplace products. You'll learn how to bundle tables with context, create dashboards that tell a story and build assistants that understand your data. We'll cover publishing workflows for Snowflake Marketplace, real-world examples and ways to maintain control without bottlenecks. Whether sharing internally or building revenue streams via external sharing, learn to make your data valuable, not just available.

speakers.bio:
Kris Jenkins is a Lead Developer Advocate for Snowflake, the host of the Developer Voices podcast, and a lifelong programmer. Over the course of his career he's been the CTO of a gold-trading company, a contractor specialising in functional programming, and a regular hackathon organiser.

He loves good software architecture, innovative new programming languages, and most of all, building stuff.

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Architecting Semantic Memory: Scaling Domain Wisdom with Snowflake Intelligence
abstract: In dynamic SaaS environments, domain context is the ultimate bottleneck. Our team found that data scientists were trapped in request queues as consumers struggled to keep pace with shifting product schemas and reporting nuances. This session shares our journey of using Snowflake Intelligence to scale domain wisdom through building “semantic memory.” We share the practical lessons learned from extracting scientist-level nuance into reusable metadata to create AI "digital twins" in Snowflake Intelligence. We share practical tips and learnings in architecting a "memory" layer and our ongoing efforts to ensure agent reliability as our product and its data evolve.

speakers.bio: Marcus Poh is the Head of Analytics Engineering (Product) at Xero, bringing an adaptive leadership style to a career that spans the entire analytics lifecycle—from engineering and science to delivery and enablement. He specialises in architecting transformations that scale, helping complex organisations move past the 'hype' to find pragmatic, value-driven paths for their data investments. Above all, Marcus believes that the most impactful data work happens when great people collaborate, innovate, and have fun along the way.

Dave Ramsden is a Staff Analytics Engineer who has spent his career decoding data across the spectrum—from Digital Forensics investigator to Principal Data Scientist and Manager of Product Insights. Today, he focuses on leveraging the Snowflake Data Cloud to build high-performance analytics workflows. Dave is passionate about the intersection of data reliability and business impact, helping teams move from "collecting data" to "finding answers".

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: AT&T’s Interoperable Iceberg Journey
abstract: AT&T is transitioning its data ecosystem to the Iceberg open-table format, aiming to achieve a single version of truth and seamless data interoperability across platforms including Snowflake, which reduces data duplication and operational costs. This architecture separates data, compute and metadata planes, allowing different compute engines to efficiently access and manage Iceberg tables while maintaining strict access controls and supporting both human and AI data consumers. This migration, supported by automation and platform partnerships, will cover 75% of AT&T’s analytic data by the end of 2026, simplifying data access, improving governance and accelerating business growth.

speakers.bio: Mark Holcomb is a Distinguished Solution Architect in the Chief Data Office with over 25 years of experience designing and implementing solutions for AT&T.  Mark is responsible for AT&T’s analytic data platform target architecture.

title: Automated Data Classification and Pseudonymization for Securely Sharing Data
abstract: Struggling with manual data governance? Learn how to build an enterprise-grade data pseudonymization framework leveraging Snowflake native capabilities. Join Snowflake Data Superhero Chris Hastie as he demonstrates automated data classification and tag-based dynamic data masking for policy-driven protection and secure sharing of pseudonymized data. Walk away with production-ready patterns for automated PII detection across your estate, reusable masking policies that adapt to tag values and real-time secure sharing with context-aware access control. Transform weeks of manual work into automated, scalable solutions that maintain analytical utility while ensuring privacy compliance.

speakers.bio:
My passion is not only to personally provide elegant and simple solutions to complex problems but also to provide others with the knowledge and tools to do this themselves.

I have been engineering data since 2014 and using Snowflake since 2018 to solve customer challenges and drive value. I was appointed as an inaugural Snowflake Data Superhero in 2019 for my industry thought leadership and knowledge-sharing; through public speaking engagements and blogs, sharing information whenever I can and often mumbling about Snowflake in my sleep.

I have also been a SnowPro Subject Matter Expert since 2020, as an inaugural external expert supporting the development of most current SnowPro exams.

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Automating Governed Analytics with Qlik MCP and Snowflake Cortex Code
abstract: Learn how to deliver governed, explainable AI-assisted analytics on Snowflake by pairing Qlik MCP with Snowflake Cortex Code. We’ll show how to encode analytics standards as reusable “skills” that guide LLMs to generate consistent master dimensions and measures, then automatically assemble analytics app assets — KPIs, tables and filters — all driven by AI. You’ll see robust load-script patterns (naming, date/time handling, verification) and a metadata-first approach that keeps KPIs stable with set analysis.

speakers.bio: Dalton Ruer is a Data Cathedral Architect who has worked in the IT field for 40 years, 35 of which have surrounded database related activities. He has traveled the world as a consultant and trainer, worked for startup companies as well as large corporations and authored a book on client/server development. He is a seasoned author, speaker, blogger and YouTube video creator who is best known for dynamically sharing inconvenient truths and observations in a humorous manner. The passion which Dalton shares thru all mediums moves and motivates others to action.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Automating Pipelines and with Snowflake Openflow and Cortex Code
abstract: bpx Energy is an Upstream Oil and Gas company that has used Snowflake Openflow to reduce the costs of their overall tech stack. They started by migrating current pipelines from various data engineering tools to Snowflake Openflow. Once comfortable with the tool, they were excited to be early adopters of Snowflake Openflow with Cortex Code. By using Cortex Code, they built pipelines quickly and migrated off other providers much faster than planned. Snowflake Openflow + Cortex Code saved the bpx team time and money compared to their current stack, and they are already fielding additional requests from the business to use Snowflake Openflow.

speakers.bio:
Data Platform Owner - Outdoor Enthusiast

Over 12 years of data experience, passionate about adaptable architecture, accelerating data to the speed of business,  and empowering developers. 

Virginia native, currently located in Denver, CO.

Sunilkumar Nandargi is a Senior Data Engineer and Subject Matter Expert (SME) at BP America Production Company, based in Houston. He specializes in building and scaling cloud‑native, enterprise‑grade data platforms with a strong focus on Snowflake, data integration, and platform reliability. Sunil is passionate about standardization, automation, and AI‑assisted engineering, helping teams accelerate delivery while improving data quality and trust. A key contributor to evolving Snowflake Openflow into a scalable, governed ingestion framework, he is known for translating complex architectures into practical, high‑impact solutions that deliver real business value

title: Autonomous Data Pipelines for Explainable AI: From Insight to Source
abstract: When AI-driven decisions are made, you must prove the data's source. AI analytics tools like Snowflake Intelligence operate on the right side of the data stack (BI/warehouse data), but trust is built on the left side, where operational data moves through pipelines. As AI scales, schema drift and evolving logic make explaining metrics difficult. Maia closes this gap by managing the data system feeding Snowflake. It builds, executes and adapts pipelines, capturing context and lineage by default, ensuring an end-to-end view from insight to source.

speakers.bio: Mia McMillan is a Senior Product Manager at Matillion specializing in DataOps including Git, CI/CD, and automation for Maia Foundation. A product thinker and tinkerer who builds her own tools, including PM Copilot and ProductOS, AI-powered systems that 10x PM productivity. She's taken products from 0 to 1 and led strategy for agentic analytics. Before joining Matillion, she worked in smart cities and electric vehicle policy and earned an MBA with Distinction from Durham University.

Cyril Sonnefraud is the Principal Product Manager steering the AI strategy and roadmap at Matillion. A seasoned data professional with over a decade of experience across Data Governance, Quality, and Cloud software, Cyril now focuses on the frontier of Agentic AI and automated data pipelines. He specializes in turning complex data ecosystems into high-velocity engines for generative AI.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Aviva's Internal Marketplace Playbook: From 29 Silos to One Source of Truth
abstract: With 29 business units and hundreds of disparate data products, Aviva faced a fragmented landscape that hindered cross-organizational agility. While an initial migration to Snowflake delivered 60% cost savings, the true challenge remained: scaling a consistent operating model. This session reveals how Aviva is using Snowflake Internal Marketplace to consolidate these silos into a single source of truth. By industrializing data products, Aviva has enabled rapid prototyping and high-velocity collaboration across the group. Learn how this shift from fragmentation to a unified marketplace is pivotal to reaching Aviva's £2 billion profit target and fostering a culture of continuous innovation.

speakers.bio: Libby Hickey is Head of Data Platforms & Enablement at Aviva, one of the UK’s leading insurance, wealth and retirement providers. She leads the transformation of how data is built, accessed and used across the organisation, focusing on scalable, self‑serve platforms that enable teams to deliver high‑quality data products faster without compromising governance or trust. She is passionate about simplifying data platform complexity, designing clear “golden paths” that make it easy to do the right thing, and ensuring every data investment is tied to real business value.

Andy McPhee is Aviva’s Director of Data Engineering, with over 25 years experience in technology, data and AI domains bringing deep experience spanning data engineering, information architecture, and enterprise data platforms across financial services and life sciences. He has led major transformations, introducing modern data, AI, and integration capabilities that power intelligent, scalable enterprise solutions.

title: Banking on Uptime: Morgan Stanley Achieves Resilience at Scale with Snowflake
abstract: In an always-on economy, resilience is nonnegotiable for mission-critical data platforms. This session explores how Snowflake delivers enterprise-grade disaster recovery and business continuity — from failover groups to account replication, client redirect, and immutable backups that safeguard against cloud outages and ransomware attacks while meeting compliance mandates. Snowflake experts will share the latest innovations in resilience and best practices, followed by a deep dive from Morgan Stanley on how they architect resilience at global scale to meet stringent regulatory, RTO and RPO requirements.

speakers.bio:
Boris Serebrinskiy is a Data and AI leader focused on enterprise data architecture, strategy, and modernization. He has led building and integrating cloud and on-prem data platforms, streaming, and NoSQL solutions, and helped standardize modern analytics patterns and scalable data processing. He serves as a technical leader and mentor, partners with stakeholders across Morgan Stanley.

Boris holds a M.S. in Nuclear Physics and Engineering. He enjoys volunteering that focus on helping non-profit organizations and schools to improve their technology platforms, especially data and analytics related. He is a huge fan of all things related to aerospace and renewable energy.

title: Batch Data Extraction at Scale with Cortex AI Functions
abstract: Unlock insights trapped in unstructured data using Cortex AI Functions. In this hands-on lab, you'll build batch extraction pipelines that process documents, images and audio — all with SQL. Load files to Snowflake stages, then use AI_PARSE_DOCUMENT to extract text from PDFs, AI_EXTRACT to pull structured fields from text and images, AI_CLASSIFY to categorize content, and AI_TRANSCRIBE to convert audio to text. Build automated pipelines with Dynamic Tables that process new data as it arrives. By the end, you'll transform unstructured files into queryable tables — extracting entities, classifying documents, and structuring data for analytics or downstream AI.

speakers.bio: I'm a developer advocate at Snowflake who gets excited about helping developers do cool things with AI and data in the cloud. After a decade of building developer communities at Hume AI, Bill.com, and Clover Network, I found my happy place bridging the gap between complex tech and the developers who want to use it. Think of me as a technical matchmaker – connecting developers with the right tools and watching the magic happen. When I'm not geeking out about AI, you'll find me spending time with my son and collecting new hobbies (because why stick to just one?).

As a Developer Advocate at Snowflake, Sho is responsible for writing blog posts and developing demos utilizing Snowflake and AI/ML/Data. Sho is also the co-founder of the MLOps community in Japan, where he spearheaded the community's launch, growing it into a leading hub for MLOps, LLMOps, and AgentOps experts. Google Developer Expert in AI/ML.

title: Best Practices for Building Real-Time AI Experiences with Snowflake Streaming
abstract: Agentic AI applications demand continuous and up-to-date data to make autonomous decisions and deliver personalized experiences. But building reliable real-time pipelines remains one of the hardest problems in data engineering. In this session, learn how Snowflake's streaming capabilities including Snowpipe Streaming, Dynamic Tables and native Kafka integration provide a simple, scalable and cost-effective foundation for real-time AI workloads. We'll walk through common architecture patterns and real customer examples so you can start building real-time AI applications today.

speakers.bio: Software Engineer working on exciting streaming related projects such as Snowpipe Streaming.

title: Best Practices for Enterprise-Ready MLOps with Snowflake
abstract: In this session we'll dive deep on best practices for managing enterprise-ready machine learning pipelines at scale in Snowflake. We'll cover the orchestration of training and inference workflows, model lifecycle management, administrative best practices, RBAC models, CI/CD pipelines and more. January Technologies will share how it used these tools to take its machine learning projects from experimentation to deployment.

speakers.bio: As an AI/ML Architect at Snowflake, Allie partners with customers to optimize their machine learning implementations. Drawing on firsthand experience as a data scientist at a Snowflake customer organization, she guides teams through migrating and modernizing their ML development and deployment workflows. She's passionate about transforming experimental models into production-ready pipelines and staying at the forefront of emerging capabilities.

Thomas is a Senior Data Scientist at January. He focuses on ML modeling and research to drive personalized outcomes for people in financial distress and expand access to credit at better rates. His work spans research to production, building ML systems that are both performant and responsible. Thomas holds a Master's degree in Computational Mathematics (Stanford University) and a Master's and Bachelor's in Econometrics (Erasmus University Rotterdam). He is particularly interested in applied ML, convex optimization, and responsible AI.

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Best Practices for Protecting and Auditing Sensitive Data in an AI-First World
abstract: In the AI-first world, privacy and compliance are nonnegotiable necessities, not optional add-ons. The rapid proliferation of sensitive data, coupled with the increasing reliance on AI and machine learning models, makes it critical to proactively address regulations like HIPAA, PCI-DSS, CCPA and GDPR. Failure to do so not only risks massive regulatory fines but also erodes customer trust. This makes privacy and compliance a strategic priority powered by intelligent automation. In this session, learn about how Snowflake addresses this challenge with Horizon Catalog's Sensitive Data Classification and new AI-driven capabilities for data governance and audit.

speakers.bio: Ankit Gupta works as a Product Manager on the Data Governance team. Prior to joining Snowflake, Ankit held a product management role at Microsoft, where he worked on various products ranging from Azure, IoT, and Data Governance & Compliance.

Anupriti has spent over a decade working across the data ecosystem, focusing on enterprise infrastructure products ranging from high-scale storage and lake analytics to data and AI governance. She is passionate about helping customers innovate and building solutions that solve problems at scale. She is based out of Seattle, Washington, and enjoys spending time with family and friends, cooking, and creating DIY crafts.

title: Best Practices for Snowflake-Managed MCP Setup and Integration
abstract: The introduction of the Snowflake-managed MCP server has generated significant customer interest and practical deployment questions. This session directly addresses the most common challenges we've observed across numerous customer engagements, specifically focusing on server setup, secure authentication, tool integration and establishing external client connectivity. Attendees will gain practical guidance and validated best practices derived from real-world deployments to ensure a seamless and successful MCP implementation.

speakers.bio: Okhtay Azarmanesh is a Principal AI Architect at Snowflake, where he leads the architectural shift toward agentic workloads and autonomous AI systems. Operating as a tech lead Okhtay bridges the gap between high-level strategy and hands-on execution, mentoring engineering teams to build AI that doesn't just talk, but acts. His career is built on a foundation of scaling complex systems, including roles as Staff ML Engineer at Lacework and Senior ML Engineer at Kinship.

As a Product Manager on the Snowflake Cortex AI team, Harshal Pimpalkhute leads initiatives across LLM inference and Agentic AI.  With a deep understanding of cloud services and software engineering he is passionate about bringing AI innovations to customers.

title: Better Together: When AI Data Agents Collaborate with AI Coding Agents
abstract:
Watch autonomous agents build a production data pipeline — live. Watch as a Snowflake customer shows how Cortex Code and Genesis Data Agents operate the entire data operations as one unified stack to ingest, map, code, test, deploy, monitor and self-heal data pipelines — autonomously. Multiple concurrently working agents build and utilize context graphs while collaborating in real time end to end. This is the "better together" story — with proof.

Walk away with:

  • A replicable blueprint for agentic AI on Snowflake
  • Real enterprise impact, with enterprise agentic AI building and use of context graphs to perform end-to-end data engineering

speakers.bio: An industry leader in enterprise data platforms, Matt led the development of business-critical analytics at Goldman Sachs for over 20 years. He later joined Snowflake to run Product Management and launched the Snowflake Data Marketplace. Now, he is bringing AI workers to enterprises to power the new AI economy.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

title: Beyond Chat: Activating Snowflake Cortex Agents for Enterprise Productivity
abstract: Is your AI stuck in a chat box? To drive real ROI, Snowflake Cortex Agents must be governed and integrated into your broader business processes. This session introduces the Boomi Agent Control Tower, the central hub for managing agents from Snowflake, Bedrock and beyond. Learn how to fuel agents with real-time data and business context for maximum accuracy; move from "passive assistants" to "active workflows" that trigger tasks in Salesforce, SAP and others; and sync Cortex Agents into a multiprovider governance plane for agent trust. Ready to move from isolated bots to a high-performing, governed agentic workforce that turns Snowflake data into enterprise impact? Join us.

speakers.job_title: SVP Solution Engineering and Data Management GTM
speakers.bio: Amy McNee is currently the SVP of Solution Engineering and Data Management GTM at Boomi, where she leads a global team of solution engineers and experts focused on helping customers build scalable data and AI foundations. Previously, Amy worked at AWS, where she led global data strategy and transformation initiatives. Her background includes leadership roles at Informatica, SAP, DataStax, and Workday. Amy is a proven expert in facilitating organizational growth and navigating the cultural shifts required for modern technology evolution. She was recognized as part of the 2024 Global Data Power Women list by CDO Magazine and is a regular speaker at industry events.

title: Beyond Customer 360: Extending Identity for Clean Room Collaboration
abstract: Join us in exploring this unprecedented session on how Kargo leverages Snowflake Data Clean Rooms and TransUnion’s identity resolution to deliver high-quality, privacy-centric ad segments for publishers and retail media networks. By bringing together publisher IP/MAID data and TransUnion identity with marketing attributes inside Snowflake’s multiparty clean room, Kargo runs more secure, policy-enforced three-way joins that minimize the risk of PII exposure. Attendees will learn how multiparty clean rooms with data access policies and identity enrichment drive improved match rates and benefit from minimal data movement and more efficient ad targeting across the ecosystem.

speakers.bio: Diana Koshy leads Data Engineering at Kargo, where she focuses on building scalable data platforms and identity-driven solutions that power audience targeting, measurement, and activation. She brings deep experience in data infrastructure, warehousing, and real-time systems, with prior leadership experience at 1010data. Diana combines a strong technical foundation with a practical product mindset, helping turn complex data capabilities into solutions that deliver business value. At Snowflake Summit, she will speak about Kargo’s clean room solution with TransUnion and how privacy-safe collaboration can unlock more effective data activation.

Don Black is Senior Director of Consulting Services at TransUnion, leading TruAudience Identity Solutions Consulting. He helps organizations use identity to enable data‑driven marketing and deliver meaningful consumer experiences. Don brings extensive product management and leadership experience from Resonate, TARGUSinfo, and Acterna, and is passionate about partnering with customers to solve complex identity challenges and drive measurable business impact.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)
