title: Beyond Ingestion: How Zendesk Scales Global Insights with Fivetran and Snowflake
abstract: To power AI-driven customer insights, Zendesk needed to unify product telemetry and enterprise data. Legacy pipelines meant a single connector could take months to deliver value. By replacing a homegrown Airflow and custom connector stack with a Snowflake-powered data platform, Zendesk dramatically accelerated delivery. Insights that once took months now take weeks. In this session, learn how Zendesk achieved a 4x increase in connector output and scaled to more than 30 data sources with a lean team. We’ll explore the shift from siloed systems to a unified semantic layer — and how to prepare your data stack for custom SDK connectors, automated transformations and unstructured data.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Beyond Passwords: Strong Authentication for Snowflake Workloads
abstract: While strong authentication is essential for accessing Snowflake resources, implementing it across all users and services can be complex. This session will detail real-world customer transitions from traditional methods (passwords, keys) to robust, modern authentication solutions like Workload Identity Federation (secretless authentication). We will explore effective rollout strategies and migration procedures, enabling you to learn the latest techniques for modernizing authentication without disrupting critical workloads.

speakers.bio: Eric Woroshow is a Principal Software Engineer in Snowflake's Database Security group. In his 15 years of writing software professionally he has had the privilege of working at multiple leading organizations, including NVIDIA, Google and Stripe.
Principal Data Engineer at Gitlab

title: Beyond the Landing Zone: From Raw Streams and Files to Governed Iceberg Tables
abstract: Apache Iceberg is the standard for data sovereignty and interoperability, but building a high-performance lakehouse is often slowed by complex engineering. Modern data is fragmented across real-time streams, CDC and cloud files. Manually transforming these into a performant Iceberg architecture requires massive effort to manage maintenance, metadata and partitioning. This session shows how Snowflake acts as a universal engine for Iceberg. Learn how Snowflake automates the data lifecycle — from ingestion to continuous optimization — without manual intervention. See how to transform any raw source into governed, production-ready Iceberg tables that stay open and easy to manage at scale.

speakers.bio: Michael is a Senior Product Manager at Snowflake, focusing on data ingestion and integration with Snowflake Openflow. He joined Snowflake following a tenure as Senior Director of Product at Cloudera, where he headed the streaming and data-in-motion portfolio, including Apache NiFi, Kafka, and Flink. With a foundational background in Sales Engineering, he focuses on transforming complex customer problems into scalable, enterprise-grade products.

Scott Teal is a Product Manager at Snowflake focused on data lake interoperability, including Apache Iceberg and legacy data lake integrations. He works with customers to modernize their data lakes and unlock zero-copy access to open table formats across platforms.

title: Beyond the Silo: AI-Driven Manufacturing with Snowflake, SAP and AWS
abstract: Manufacturing leaders are trapped in data silos, unable to connect SAP's business context with the speed AI-powered operations demand. This session shows how leading manufacturers are unifying SAP S/4HANA, Snowflake and AWS into an AI-ready data foundation. Walk through the architecture for zero-copy data sharing and multi-source harmonization, eliminating ETL pipelines while preserving SAP's business semantics. See how static ERP records become dynamic, closed-loop outcomes: real-time scenario modeling, automated SAP write-backs and AI-optimized make-to-order workflows. Leave with a practical blueprint that turns enterprise data into a competitive advantage.

speakers.bio: Sunny Krishnan is currently leading Snowflake Industry GTM on AWS, Sunny helps Build, Market, Sell and Scale motion that transforms partner engagement and accelerats market adoption. His strategic framework for industry solutions, particularly in manufacturing, automotive sectors driving substantial growth and established new benchmarks for solving real workd customer problems. Sunny is instrumental in co-create AWS, Snowflake, SAP GTM helping unlock the SAP BDC and enrich with diverse source systems driving LOB outcomes.  Prior to Amazon, Sunny led strategic roles with Rockwell Automation and Siemens.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: BI to Generative BI
abstract: Learn how we built a data-to-AI pipeline using Snowflake Intelligence and Semantic Views, resulting in 10x faster data modeling and instantaneous insights for both our traditional BI dashboards and generative AI agents. We will detail the technical journey, how we solved the challenges of governance using Semantic Views to ground our AI, and the blueprints for building a data-driven organization where every decision is powered by trusted analytics and explainable generative AI.

speakers.bio: Abby Kai Liu is a hands-on analytics leader who builds enterprise-scale data solutions to drive actionable insights. She has led multiple cross-functional teams of data engineers, scientists, analysts, and visualization specialists to develop scalable, trustworthy analytics products that power business decisions.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Blue Yonder: Accelerating Business Transformation with Agentic AI
abstract: Blue Yonder embarked on a business transformation journey two years ago with a goal to complete the journey by Q1 2027. The goal is to change from a traditional ISV software company that has been in business for over 30 years into a best-of-breed SaaS provider, modeled after the Snowflake operating model. Chris Burchett, SVP of Generative AI at Blue Yonder, speak's about the company's journey and focuses on how it utilizes agentic technology — from Snowflake Cortex AI, to value-sell Blue Yonder's legacy on-premises customers to move, to its AI-enabled SaaS AI-driven supply chain platform.

speakers.bio: Chris Burchett is the Senior Vice President, Generative AI at Blue Yonder where he is responsible for developing and executing the generative AI strategy. As part of this role, he and his team work with Blue Yonder customers to develop AI agents that meet their needs and work in complement with their Blue Yonder solutions. Prior to this, Chris led Blue Yonder’s platform engineering initiatives, driving the modernization of SaaS applications and fostering a DevOps culture. He supports the company's digital transformation by leveraging Microsoft Azure and Snowflake to improve and expand Blue Yonder's native SaaS platform and solutions.

title: Booking.com's Data Travels: Platform Foundations to Agentic Analytics
abstract: Booking.com is on a mission to remove friction from travel and now from data. In this session, discover how a robust Snowflake data platform is laying the foundations for scalable, trusted analytics across the business. Learn why enabling true self-service insights has become critical to unlocking faster decisions and operational efficiency. Hear how early pioneers in customer service are bringing this vision to life, going live with ThoughtSpot’s agentic analytics to empower frontline teams with AI-driven answers in the flow of work. See how this new analytics operating model is turning trusted data into better employee experiences and more seamless journeys for travelers.

speakers.bio: Currently operating at the strategic forefront, Chris is the Manager of Data Engineering at Booking.com. He leads the Customer Service Data Engineering team responsible for designing and flawlessly maintaining the robust data warehouse that serves as the single source of truth for all critical stakeholders. With over four years dedicated to revolutionizing data infrastructure in this role, Chris leverages cutting-edge data technologies, including Kafka, S3, Snowflake, and AWS, to transform raw data into a powerful business assets. This expertise culminates in delivering actionable, high-impact insights accessible through advanced visualization and analysis tools like ThoughtSpot.

Jay Stricks has worked on data analytics platforms across multiple industries -- from news and media, advertising and marketing, e-commerce, and now travel. He is currently a group product manager for Booking.com's insights platforms, which encompasses strategy and delivery for a range of analytical capabilities, including AI analytics and enterprise semantic layer, data warehousing, analytical data modeling, data development tooling,  reporting/BI, data sharing, and workload cost management tooling.

title: Bridging the Gap: How BNC Migrated Legacy PySpark to Snowpark Unified Compute
abstract: As data volumes scale, the friction between external Apache Spark clusters and cloud warehouses often creates latency and governance challenges. BNC addressed this by migrating PySpark pipelines directly into Snowflake’s unified compute platform using Snowpark Connect. By eliminating external infrastructure management while maintaining Python-based flexibility, BNC streamlined its operations and simplified data oversight. This session explores the technical architecture of the migration, strategies for handling complex dependency management, and benchmark results comparing their legacy Spark environment to the new, optimized Snowflake implementation.

speakers.job_title: AI Lead at National Bank of Canada
speakers.bio: Renan Waroux is an AI Lead at National Bank of Canada, where he drives high‑impact AI use cases for Personal and Commercial Banking. With 7+ years at the bank, he has led applied ML initiatives across risk, customer experience, and cloud analytics. Renan co‑piloted the evolution of the enterprise analytics platform and played a key role in migrating ML workloads from Databricks to Snowflake, enabling scalable MLOps, stronger governance, and faster time‑to‑value. He focuses on responsible AI and GenAI adoption aligned with business outcomes.

Nagesh Cherukuri is based in the San Francisco-Bay Area and is the Principal Architect in the Applied Field Engineering at Snowflake, responsible for driving application workloads on Snowflake and helping customers design solutions for their data applications. He has held several leadership roles in a career spanning 20 years, with his most recent experience as Principal Architect at AWS, where he help devise data strategies to develop modern data solutions on public cloud for strategic accounts. Nagesh is an expert in leading enterprise data and analytics, holds an MS degree in Computer Information Science with a specialization in Security from Boston University.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Bridging the Gap: Powering Real-Time Apps with Snowflake and Postgres
abstract: Managing a 6 TB install base in Snowflake is ideal for analytics, but how do you serve that same data to high-performance web applications? Ericsson faced this challenge: needing to move from massive analytical queries to sub-second, small-result lookups for UI users. Enter Snowflake Postgres. This session explores how we mirror 6 TB of data using advanced data movement capabilities to bridge the gap between the Data Cloud and the application layer. Learn how we achieve rapid UI responsiveness while ensuring all application changes flow seamlessly back into Snowflake for a unified, bidirectional data ecosystem.

speakers.bio: Dean is a software developer based in Montreal with deep experience in Java/Spring Boot, Python, cloud infrastructure, and data engineering across Azure and AWS. He thrives on hard problems — from multi-tenant networking at scale to petabyte-scale data platforms. Currently, he's exploring how AI-powered tooling can reshape the way teams build and ship software.

Brian is a Applied Field Engineer at Snowflake with over three decades of experience in enterprise database engineering. He specializes in cross-platform data architecture, with deep expertise in PostgreSQL, Oracle, and distributed data systems. Brian is the creator of pgCompare, a high-speed, cross-platform data comparison tool, and actively develops solutions that enhance PostgreSQL monitoring and platform migrations. Throughout his career, he has led enterprise-scale database transformations across the financial sector and other major industries. He brings deep technical insight and a practical mindset to modern data architecture challenges.

title: Build an AI Agent with Streamlit and Postgres
abstract: In this hands-on lab, we put you in the driver’s seat of the modern AI application stack. Together we will build a powerful, data-friendly Python application using just two core technologies: Streamlit’s “pure Python" approach for a rapid, beautiful frontend, and Postgres for robust data handling and semantic search. We will build a functional AI agent that allows users to query data using Snowflake Cortex AI. You will learn how to build a Streamlit interface, connect to an agent, and add interactive, data-driven elements like charts, graphs and maps. You’ll leave this session with the tools to unlock value by turning existing data and static text into an interactive AI agent.

speakers.bio: Chanin Nantasenamat, PhD is a Lead Developer Advocate at Snowflake where he creates educational content on building data apps with Streamlit and Snowflake. Previously a full Professor of Bioinformatics, he conducted research on computational drug discovery and authored over 160 research articles and book chapters. He runs the Data Professor YouTube channel with 220,000 subscribers and over 400 videos that have received more than 10 million views. He has also written 100+ tutorial blogs on data science and AI. Recently, he launched a Coursera course on "Fast Prototyping of GenAI Apps with Streamlit" as part of a DeepLearning.AI × Snowflake collaboration.

Elizabeth is PostgreSQL contributor and developer advocate at Snowflake. Elizabeth is passionate about open source advocacy and education. She is on the board of the United States PostgreSQL Association (PgUS), hosts monthly Postgres Meetup for All, and co-hosts annual PostGIS Day. She is also active in local tech communities in Lawrence, Kansas and Kansas City.

title: Build an Iceberg Lakehouse with Snowflake and Microsoft Fabric OneLake
abstract: As data volumes increase and AI workloads proliferate, copying and ETL-ing data across systems is no longer sustainable. This hands-on workshop walks through building a fully interoperable, open lakehouse by integrating Snowflake and Microsoft OneLake. Learn how to leverage Apache Iceberg™ to create an open, governed data foundation that allows both Snowflake and Microsoft Fabric engines to operate on the same datasets without data duplication or complex pipelines. You will use sample data in Fabric, create an external volume and set up Microsoft OneLake as a catalog-linked database in Snowflake, create Iceberg tables in OneLake and query them both from Snowflake and Fabric.

speakers.bio: Principal Cloud Solution Architect - Microsoft

Eric is a Partner Solution Engineer at Snowflake, where he specializes in all things Microsoft and Azure. With a strong background in data engineering and data architecture, Eric has built his career across diverse industries such as healthcare, finance, and manufacturing. His primary focus involved leveraging the Microsoft stack, designing a robust cloud architecture, and orchestrating seamless migrations of data from on-premises into Snowflake on Azure.

title: Build a Unified Metadata Catalog with Iceberg in DoorDash's Lakehouse Platform
abstract: Join us as DoorDash shares their battle-tested blueprint for turning Iceberg interoperability into a repeatable, production-grade lakehouse platform. Get insights from their real-world journey adopting Apache Iceberg as the lakehouse table format. Learn about query performance, catalog latency and data optimization, including VARIANT, deletion vectors, CDF/CDC patterns, operationalization via Snowflake catalog-linked databases and Iceberg table replication. We'll cover the “gotchas” of multi-engine architecture patterns, including feature parity gaps, type/schema mismatches and SQL behavior differences.

speakers.bio: Worked on exabytes and small startup projects  with constant emphasis on advanced, emerging technologies with high ROI
I'm a software engineer from Doordash data platform team

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Build a Universal Semantic Layer for BI and AI: Hands-On Lab
abstract: This 90-minute hands-on lab demonstrates how to create a semantic layer in Snowflake using semantic views. Attendees will learn to leverage features such as Semantic View Autopilot and Cortex Code to accelerate the creation of a governed data model. You will build a single source of truth that can be accessed by traditional BI tools and used in tandem with Snowflake Cortex AI to enable the fast deployment of complex, robust chatbots and AI tools.

speakers.bio: Abhinav Vadrevu is a seasoned product manager with expertise in AI and data infrastructure. As the product manager for Snowflake's Cortex Analyst, he focuses on creating AI-driven solutions for enterprises to get insights from their data. With a strong track record working on GitHub Copilot and Grammarly AI, Abhinav has launched enterprise AI features and scalable products, driving significant user adoption.

Fawad is a strategic technology leader with 20+ years of experience across 50 countries. As Field CTO at Snowflake, he advises C-suite executives on data strategy, AI-driven analytics, and cloud architecture. His expertise spans the full tech lifecycle, from engineering to business development. Fawad also serves as a Data Advisor to the World Economic Forum, contributing to global initiatives on responsible AI and ethical data management. A LinkedIn Learning Instructor and sustainability advocate, he focuses on leveraging AI and "Data for Good" to drive measurable impact while shaping the next generation of data leaders.

title: Build Better Semantic Views Using Cortex Code and Glean
abstract: This session shows how Glean, the Work AI platform, connects to Cortex Code via MCP to bring enterprise context directly into Snowflake builders’ dev workflows. We cover the value of Glean and Snowflake for conversational analytics, then demo Glean’s integration with Cortex Code using a finance schema spanning subscriptions, customers, invoices and MRR snapshots. We’ll show how a semantic view optimizer in Cortex Code inspects DDL, searches finance SOPs and Slack via Glean, refines metrics such as active customers and churn, and then compares the original and optimized views in Snowflake Cortex Analyst.

speakers.job_title: Head of Partner Solutions, ISVs Glean
speakers.bio: Jess Eurich is a Partner Solutions Engineer at Glean, leading ISV partnerships that bring Work AI to Snowflake customers. They work hands-on with data and AI teams to design and implement agentic AI solutions that unify Snowflake data with enterprise context from docs, chats, and tickets. At Snowflake Summit, Jess will share field-tested patterns for making analytics self-serve, productionizing agents securely, and turning AI experiments into measurable business impact.

Randy Pettus is a Principal Partner Solution Engineer at Snowflake, who works with Snowflake's AI & ML ISV partners. Randy has 15+ years leading analytics and data science teams and delivering patented data-driven solutions. He specializes in turning complex data into strategic insights and scalable systems, with a focus on operationalizing generative AI applications to AI agents tied directly to business impact. Combining business consulting, GTM strategy, and deep technical expertise, he accelerates AI adoption and measurable outcomes.

title: Builders Keynote
abstract: AI is transforming what (and how) you build on Snowflake. From designing self-serve intelligence and architecting AI-ready data infrastructure to shipping production agents, builders are at the center of the biggest shift in data since the cloud. Through demos and customer stories, we'll learn how data practitioners are adapting and delivering more impact than ever before.

speakers.bio: Danica began her career as a software engineer in financial services and pivoted to developer relations, where she focussed primarily on open source technologies under the Apache Software Foundation umbrella such as Apache Kafka® and Apache Flink®. She now leads the open source advocacy efforts at Snowflake and is involved in the Apache Iceberg™ and Apache Polaris (incubating) communities. She can be found on X (Bluesky and Mastodon), talking about tech, plants, and baking @TheDanicaFine.

title: Building a Cross-Cloud Data Platform with Iceberg, Horizon Catalog and Pulsar
abstract: Toyota Motor Europe (TME) set out to unify vehicle profile data across multiple clouds without duplicating it. This session shows how TME built a cross-cloud data strategy using Snowflake Open Catalog and Iceberg tables, integrating managed Apache Pulsar as a streaming backbone. Learn how TME connected cloud environments through external Iceberg tables for a zero-copy approach, and how separate maintenance jobs complemented Open Catalog by compacting metadata for consistency and governance. We also explain why migrating to Horizon Catalog improved interoperability and scalability, and the steps TME followed to combine Pulsar, Iceberg and Open Catalog to power the vehicle profile.

speakers.bio: Tomer Steinberg is a Principal Solutions Engineer at Snowflake with experience in the data industry since 2005. He partners with automotive and manufacturing organizations to unlock the value of their data—bringing together information across the enterprise and its ecosystem. Tomer helps design smart, trusted, and governed data and AI solutions using scalable architectures to accelerate productivity and innovation.

speakers.job_title: Data & AI Engg, Platform Technical Owner

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Building an End-to-End AI Application on Snowflake: From Data to Intelligence
abstract: Build a complete AI retail app entirely in Snowflake. Start by using Cortex Code to generate SQL and dbt models for metrics such as CLV. Next, train a GPU-accelerated XGBoost model in Snowflake Notebooks, register it, and deploy it as a service. To handle OLTP, use Snowflake Postgres and sync data to Apache Iceberg on S3, demonstrating lakehouse interoperability with pg_lake. Finally, unify the stack with Snowflake Intelligence, a conversational interface orchestrating Snowflake Cortex Analyst, Snowflake Cortex Search and your ML models for a seamless analytical experience.

speakers.bio:
With experience in big data, data science, and machine learning Dash Desai is able to apply 18+ years of full-stack, hands-on software and data engineering skills to help build solutions that solve business problems and surface trends that shape markets in new ways than imagined before. As a Senior Lead Developer Advocate at Snowflake, he is passionate about evaluating new ideas, trends, and helping articulate how technology can address a given business problem.

Dash Desai has worked for global enterprises and in agile environments–for tech startups in the Bay Area in varying verticals, such as VoIP, Social Gaming, Digital Health, NoSQL database, and Data Cloud platforms.

Arun Agarwal is currently leading the AI product marketing initiatives at Snowflake. With prior experience at Salesforce, where he contributed to their product marketing team with a focus on data and integration, Arun brings over 15 years of diverse expertise. Holding a PhD in STEM, Arun’s achievements include co-authoring several patents and international scientific research articles. Passionate about cutting-edge technology, he is committed to driving innovation and shaping the future of the industry.

title: Building an Interoperable Lakehouse with Snowflake Horizon and Cortex Code
abstract: Multiple engines and catalogs make governance hard and AI‑ready data even harder. In this session, we will show how to use Snowflake Horizon and Cortex Code to build an interoperable lakehouse that is secure, well-governed and easy to extend. We will start by designing Iceberg-based lakehouse, then use Horizon to centralize discovery and policy enforcement across engines. On top of this governed foundation, we will introduce Cortex Code as the developer copilot that generates and refactors the data lake objects. Attendees will leave with patterns and code templates to operationalize an interoperable data lake on Snowflake.

speakers.bio: Piotr works with large enterprises to design and scale Snowflake AI Data Cloud. He is an active thought leader contributing to architectural best practices with focus on emerging technologies.

Frans leads Data Modernization and Cloud Transformation initiatives at Snowflake. A specialist in AI/ML and Data Strategy, he has a proven track record of helping global enterprises unlock the full potential of the AI Data Cloud. From architectural design to full-scale implementation, Frans is dedicated to helping organizations build intelligent, data-driven ecosystems that drive the future of business.

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Building an Operational Data Store Using Snowflake PostGres
abstract: We present a scalable architecture for a near real-time operational data store (ODS) on Snowflake using PostgreSQL as the source. By leveraging change data capture (CDC) via the PostgreSQL Write-Ahead Log (WAL), the system captures row-level changes with minimal source impact. The framework uses Snowflake Dynamic Tables and Streams to automate incremental ingestion and materialization. This creates a high-concurrency, low-latency environment for operational reporting and real-time apps, eliminating the overhead of traditional batch ETL.

speakers.bio: Sam is a Sr. Data Cloud Architect with deep expertise in strategic advisory, platform design and architecture, data engineering and GenAI, working with customers and partners across industries.

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Building a Trusted AI Data Foundation with Salesforce and Snowflake
abstract: Discover how the strategic partnership between Salesforce and Snowflake creates a unified architecture to unlock the full potential of the agentic enterprise. Attendees will learn how current integrations across Tableau, Data 360 and Agentforce enable humans and agents to drive success together.

speakers.bio: Conor works on the Salesforce Strategic Tech Partner Marketing team, leading partner marketing for Snowflake and is a former Tableau Solution Engineer. When he isn't talking about Zero Copy with Data 360 or LOD calculations in Tableau, you can find him watching Formula 1 or hunting down the world's best drip coffee.

Martin Ko is a Partner Management Senior Principal at Salesforce, where he leads strategic technology partnerships at the intersection of data, AI, and cloud innovation. With a focus on building and scaling high-impact alliances, Martin serves as the primary partnership owner for Salesforce's most significant data ecosystem relationships.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Building Context-Aware Agents with Snowflake and Google Cloud Vertex AI
abstract: In the race to deploy generative AI, the biggest hurdle isn't the model — it’s the context. Accessing data in the Snowflake AI Data Cloud is often blocked by security silos and complex pipelines, but the seamless integration between Snowflake and Google Cloud eliminates this barrier. Join our session to explore the synergy of Snowflake’s governed data and Google’s Gemini models on Vertex AI. We will use our Quickstart integration to demonstrate secure data flows for context-aware AI agents capable of nuanced reasoning over your enterprise data. This session delivers a proven blueprint to advance from AI experimentation to production-grade intelligence, without the architectural overhead.

speakers.bio:
At Google Cloud, Don spearheads the strategy, business development and global GTM scale-out for the AI, Data and Analytics partner ecosystem.  

Before Google Cloud, Don spent 14+ years at Informatica in a variety of technical product and partnerships roles, finishing as Vice President of Business Development, OEM and Alliances. 

Before Informatica, Don spent 14 years at Sun Microsystems, Oracle and other startups in different roles in Product,  Alliances, and PM.  

Don’s education includes degrees in Computer Science and Math from Cal State East Bay and enjoys hiking, biking, golf, traveling, volunteering and spending time with his family.

title: Building Enterprise AI Agents Using Snowflake Intelligence
abstract: In mid 2025, we launched the Snowflake GTM AI Assistant to 6,000 users across Snowflake sales and marketing organizations — our first full-scale enterprise deployment of Snowflake Intelligence. By year-end, the assistant had already answered more than 330,000 questions, helping users work faster and make better decisions. Since launch, we got so many questions about how to successfully develop, launch and scale reliable enterprise AI assistants for thousands of users. This session is about sharing our best practices so others can easily build similar enterprise agents that users love.

speakers.bio:
Sait Izmit is a Principal Product Manager at Snowflake, leading AI products that empower sales and Go-to-Market teams. His work spans GTM assistants, intelligent workflow automation, and AI-driven revenue acceleration solutions built on Snowflake’s data platform.

Previously at Google, Sait drove GenAI and platform initiatives across Google Ads and Google Play, delivering large-scale solutions that enhanced sales workflows and Android user experiences worldwide. An Engineering Director turned product leader, he specializes in turning ambiguous problem spaces into scalable, high-impact AI products at the intersection of data platforms, consumer-grade UX, and revenue execution.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: This session is currently open to Marketer's Forum registrants. Registration will open to the general event audience closer to the event.

title: Building Multiparty Intelligence with AI-Native Data Clean Rooms
abstract: The data you need lives in someone else's warehouse. This session provides the practical playbook for the fix. See how advertisers, publishers, healthcare networks and financial institutions are collaborating today: multiple parties, one neutral room, no exposed raw data. From setup to first result takes one business day, with partners already on Snowflake. We demo an overlap analysis with machine learning, then show how to get results out of the secure environment so you can actually use them, without leaving Snowflake's governance layer. No SQL required. Snowflake's agentic interface lets anyone configure a collaboration and run analysis in plain English.

speakers.bio: Spencer Janyk is an expert in privacy-preserving infrastructure and secure data collaboration and leads interoperability and privacy foundations for Data Clean Rooms at Snowflake, developinbg trusted infrastructure for secure, multi-party data collaboration. A seasoned leader in the privacy-enhancing technology (PET) space, Spencer previously served as the CEO and Co-Founder of Checksum Media, a machine-vision platform protecting digital identities and content for creators and public figures. Previously Spencer launched Confidential Matching at Google Ads Google, and also held roles at Robinhood and AWS Key Management Service (KMS).

Shivangi Tandon is a Product Manager at Snowflake, where she works on Data Clean Rooms to enable secure, privacy-first data collaboration. Her work focuses on building core collaboration fundamentals that help organizations operationalize and scale their cleanrooms while maintaining strong governance and compliance. Previously, Shivangi was a Product Manager at LiveRamp, where she led the Data Catalog product, driving data discovery and helping enterprises understand and activate their data. She brings a strong technical foundation and deep expertise in data governance, identity, and collaboration, with a focus on translating complex data systems into intuitive, scalable products.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Building Open Source Native Apps: Johnson & Johnson's Drug Discovery Solution
abstract: Join this session to learn how research teams at Johnson & Johnson (J&J) are implementing an RDKit chemical library on Snowflake — unlocking the power of high-speed cheminformatics to accelerate downstream drug discovery. Hear how J&J's IT teams are reducing operational complexity with zero data movement by leveraging advanced programming techniques through Snowpark for Python stored procedures and UDFs. Discover how J&J and Snowflake are partnering to make J&J's solution available to the broader healthcare and life sciences community as an open source Snowflake Native App on Snowflake Marketplace.

title: Building Powerful AI Data Tools with AI Functions and Agentic Search
abstract:
Building AI applications in the age of agents demands a platform that turns vast enterprise data into
real business outcomes. Snowflake Cortex AI Functions deliver the core primitives — classify, extract,
summarize and analyze — to build powerful AI pipelines directly in Snowflake. AI Function Studio
accelerates time to value with managed quality optimizations, multimodal support and built-in cost
controls. Add agentic search, and intelligent systems can now orchestrate analytical workflows across
thousands of documents that traditional RAG can't handle. One platform. Enterprise-grade AI, end to
end.

speakers.bio: Jessie Felix builds AI-powered data products at scale. As a Product Manager on Snowflake’s Cortex AI team, he works on embedding advanced AI capabilities directly into the data cloud, helping enterprises getting value from all data. Previously at AWS, He has led various product management initiatives, driving cost and performance features that powered large-scale data lake workloads. His work has helped organizations optimize petabyte-scale environments while accelerating analytics and AI adoption.

title: Building Real-Time Fraud Detection with Agentic ML in Snowflake
abstract: Moving ML from experimentation to production shouldn't require rebuilding from scratch or navigating lengthy development cycles. In this hands-on session, you'll build an end-to-end fraud detection model using an agentic approach in Snowflake. Starting with natural language prompts in Cortex Code from Snowflake Notebooks, you'll train a model, register it to the Snowflake Model Registry and deploy it for real-time inference, all without leaving Snowflake. Walk away with practical techniques for creating production-ready prediction endpoints and scaling online inference workloads for fraud detection and beyond.

speakers.bio: Chanin Nantasenamat, PhD is a Lead Developer Advocate at Snowflake where he creates educational content on building data apps with Streamlit and Snowflake. Previously a full Professor of Bioinformatics, he conducted research on computational drug discovery and authored over 160 research articles and book chapters. He runs the Data Professor YouTube channel with 220,000 subscribers and over 400 videos that have received more than 10 million views. He has also written 100+ tutorial blogs on data science and AI. Recently, he launched a Coursera course on "Fast Prototyping of GenAI Apps with Streamlit" as part of a DeepLearning.AI × Snowflake collaboration.

As a Developer Advocate at Snowflake, Sho is responsible for writing blog posts and developing demos utilizing Snowflake and AI/ML/Data. Sho is also the co-founder of the MLOps community in Japan, where he spearheaded the community's launch, growing it into a leading hub for MLOps, LLMOps, and AgentOps experts. Google Developer Expert in AI/ML.

title: Building Real-Time, Multimodal AI Agents with Snowflake
abstract: Generative AI often sits isolated in experimental notebooks, disconnected from the high-velocity data of the real world. In this session, we introduce a Snowflake architecture (Snowflake, NiFi, Apache Iceberg, Cortex, Kafka) that includes a blueprint for operationalizing AI on data in motion. We will demonstrate how to move beyond static RAG by building a live "vision agent" that ingests real-time video streams, performs autonomous image analysis using multimodal LLMs (Cortex) and triggers downstream actions instantly. Attendees will see a live blueprint where data ingestion, vectorization and inference happen in a unified, serverless loop, turning raw chaos into structured intelligence.

speakers.bio: Tim Spann is a Senior Solutions Engineer @ Snowflake. He works with Generative AI, LLM, Snowflake, SQL,, Python, Java, Apache NiFi and Apache Kafka. Tim has over fifteen years of experience with the IoT, distributed computing, messaging, streaming technologies, and Java programming. Previously, he was a Principal Developer Advocate at Zilliz, Principal Developer Advocate at Cloudera, Developer Advocate at StreamNative, Principal DataFlow Field Engineer at Cloudera, a Senior Solutions Engineer at Hortonworks, a Senior Solutions Architect at AirisData, a Senior Field Engineer at Pivotal and a Senior Team Leader at HPE. https://www.snowghostbreakers.com/

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Building the Block Data MCP: A Blueprint for Gen AI Insights and Analytics
abstract: Your data warehouse holds the answers to your most important business questions. But without an AI-ready data architecture, the LLM can’t access or use them. We solved this problem at Block by building the Block Data MCP, combining a governed semantic layer, a Snowflake client and AI agents (Goose), to map business questions to trusted metrics, layered with generative AI insights. In this session we share how we designed and implemented the underlying architecture, eliminated AI hallucinations through deterministic data flows, and scaled our Block Data MCP to support ~2,000 internal data users and various business use cases.

speakers.bio: Alyssa Ransbury leads foundational, cross-functional initiatives as a Staff Software Engineer and builder at Block.

Zach is a Staff AI Data Engineer at Block (Square/Cash App) where he architects and builds agentic data solutions. He’s passionate about building systems that democratize data and empower teams to make better business decisions. At Snowflake Summit 2026, he will share how Block overhauled its data strategy to enable agentic AI for analytics.

title: Build Real-Time User Analytics with Snowpipe Streaming and Cortex Agents
abstract: Stop relying on stale dashboards and build your own live user analytics engine. In this session, we will architect a scalable user analytics platform end-to-end using Snowpipe Streaming. Discover how to use Snowflake CLI and Cortex AI to automate pipeline code generation, deploying elastic channels for effortless auto-scaling and error tables for native dead-letter queue handling. We will demonstrate how to go from raw clickstream events to AI-driven insights in minutes, removing the operational complexity of traditional streaming stacks.

speakers.bio:
Chase Thomas is a Technical Product Manager at Snowflake, focused on real-time data streaming and Kafka-based architectures. He brings deep experience designing scalable, event-driven systems that enable fast, reliable insights.

Before joining Snowflake, Chase worked on scaling cloud native Kafka services and building real-time dam safety monitoring systems, experience that sparked his passion for streaming data and time-critical infrastructure. Today, he helps Snowflake customers unlock the full potential of streaming technologies for modern analytics and operations.
From store to retrieve and everything in between. Lead a Data Engineering team at Cboe Global Markets streaming terabytes of activity data daily from U.S. equities, options, and futures exchanges.

title: Build REST APIs for your Data Product with Snowpark Container Services
abstract: Data products evolve, and so must their APIs. This session focuses on how Snowflake SPCS empowers developers to host and run data applications, including custom services, directly alongside their data. The core theme is offering your data applications as seamless REST APIs to consumers. We'll show a practical example of wrapping a data product in an API and allowing partners or customers to consume the service instantly via standard HTTP requests, completely bypassing the need for installing connectors and drivers. This approach drastically simplifies consumption and opens new avenues for data monetization and Secure Data Sharing.

speakers.bio: Ovi is a Solutions Architect dedicated to transforming how organizations leverage data to make smarter, more sustainable decisions. He specializes in designing and implementing robust data-driven applications and efficient data pipelines, with a strong focus on collaborating with customers to develop actionable data strategies that enable AI/ML-driven use cases. Outside of technology, he enjoys exploring the complexities of human psychology through reading and debating, maintains his resilience through endurance sports, and is committed to raising the next generation of children to value critical thinking and deep, focused work.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Built for Humans, Ready for Agents: A Data Stack That Serves Everyone
abstract: The next wave of self-serve analytics won't be driven by better dashboards. It'll be driven by agents that find, interpret and act on data autonomously. But most data stacks weren't built for this. In this session, you'll learn how agentic workloads differ from analytical ones and what that means for your tooling, models, freshness SLAs and access patterns. We'll cover the decisions that create optionality versus lock-in — storage formats, compute separation, semantic layers and metadata richness — and see how open source changes the calculus. Drawing on real trade-offs from Airbyte, you'll leave with a practical framework to assess your own stack.

speakers.bio:
Jim Kutz is Head of Data at Airbyte, the open-source data movement platform, where he leads the internal analytics infrastructure powering the company's go-to-market, product, and revenue analytics. With over 20 years of experience across analytics, data engineering, data science, and product. Jim has built and scaled data organizations at Capital One, Nike, New Relic, Grafana Labs, and Airbyte.

Jim is based in Oakland, California.

title: Built with Care: Powering the Next Wave of Healthcare Technology on Snowflake
abstract: Healthcare and life sciences organizations rely on their technology partners to operationalize so many of their business-critical workflows. From clinical trial management to electronic health records to claims management and billing, healthcare runs on technology. Join this panel discussion to hear from healthcare and life sciences technology innovators on how they are leveraging the power of data, AI and the Snowflake platform to build solutions that accelerate customer success. You’ll hear examples and see demonstrations of real applications powered by Snowflake AI Data Cloud for Healthcare and Life Sciences that drive efficiency in healthcare.

speakers.bio: As CEO of Improzo, an award-winning AI-native firm honored as AI Innovation of the Year, he is transforming how life sciences companies operate by deploying intelligence that executes across a tech-agnostic pharma ecosystem. Improzo builds AI agents, accelerators, and solutions to solve complex commercial, medical, and operational challenges in global pharma and biotech. Grounded in the belief that innovation must evolve within the ecosystem, not in a black box, he combines medical training (DDS, MS, MBA) with leadership experience across major pharma-tech innovators to position Improzo at the forefront of intelligent automation in life sciences.

Prior to joining Snowflake in 2018, Todd spent 30 years in the Healthcare and Life Sciences industry, from roles in Pharmacy (PDX, now a division of Optum), Health Systems (Novant Health), and Payers (Prime Therapeutics) culminating with leading/serving software development teams and implementing cloud transformations as CIO at PDX which included being an early adopter of Snowflake.

Todd works with Snowflake’s Sales, Ventures, Marketing, and Product teams, as well as external Partners to engage with current and potential customers to fully realize the potential of their data.

Fawad Butt is the CEO of Penguin AI, a healthcare AI company serving payors, providers, and revenue cycle management organizations. He previously served as Chief Data & Analytics Officer at UnitedHealthcare and Chief Data Officer at Optum, where he led one of the largest data organizations in healthcare, supporting platforms and analytics for over 60 million members and managing a multi-hundred-million-dollar P&L. Earlier, he held Chief Data and Governance roles at Kaiser Permanente, building enterprise data, privacy, and analytics capabilities. He also served as an Executive-in-Residence at Canvas Ventures, advising on healthcare AI investments and portfolio companies.

title: Causality Data for Marketing: Deploy Valuable AI Personalization on Snowflake
abstract: Marketing teams run thousands of experiments, but the learnings don’t always translate into better results. Why? We’re scaling our campaigns on the wrong data. This session introduces the agentic context graph built on the Snowflake AI Data Cloud. It’s a warehouse-native learning layer that continuously captures customer context, actions and outcomes to power AI decision-making. Walk away with a blueprint for turning results from thousands of experiments into compounding intelligence, helping you scale smarter campaigns and guarantee growth.

speakers.bio: Anthony is the Chief Data Strategy Officer at GrowthLoop, where he leads AI research and development, following his tenure as Chief Customer Officer. Prior to GrowthLoop, Anthony was head of the Owned Retail Business Unit at Anheuser-Busch; following stints as a marketing director, digital product manager, and sales strategy manager. Anthony invented and founded Pizza Shelf (a consumer product) and led Amazon Services and Data Analysis at Crimson Advantage (a marketing strategy consultancy). He has his B.A. in Computer Science from Harvard, where he also played varsity football.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Connect InfluxDB to Snowflake: How Capella Space Uses Snowpark Data Source APIs
abstract: Learn how Capella Space used Snowpark DB-API to build a Python-based connector to InfluxDB and break the data silos between operational telemetry and analytics in Snowflake. We’ll walk through the architecture, show how developers used a standard DB-API pattern to move key time-series data into Snowflake, and share lessons learned that you can apply to your own data sources.

speakers.bio: Qinyi Ding is a Product Manager at Snowflake, where he leads product efforts for Snowpark, focusing on improving the developer experience for DataFrame-based data engineering workflows. With a strong foundation in data engineering and data warehousing, his goal is to empower data engineers to build scalable, performant pipelines on Snowflake.

Travis is Senior Data Analytics Architect at Capella Space, a satellite manufacturing and imagery company, where he has spent the past three years building their data infrastructure. He architected and engineered the company's data warehouse platform from conception to
deployment and currently leads the DataOps team, developing a self-serve data platform with
Snowflake that empowers users across the organization, from business analysts to data
scientists to engineers.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Consistency Before Intelligence: The Hidden Work Behind AI Analytics
abstract: We’re at a moment in the industry where every data professional is trying to reach the AI-powered future of analytics: where reliable insights are generated instantly on demand, freeing our analysts from ad hoc data requests to pursue more strategic work. However, many of us have found that simply plugging an AI to our data warehouse won’t magically get us there. At Canva, we’ve identified three big things that need to happen first: an AI-ready data warehouse architecture, a semantic layer to assist and govern our LLM assistants, and a solution to metric consistency. In this session, we dive into each of those three major aspects of our journey toward the future of analytics.

speakers.bio:
Previously a data scientist in marketing technology. Once upon a time a mathematician in probability theory. I'm currently an analytics engineer building the future of analytics at Canva as a part of the Analytics Platform team.

My current work exists in the intersection between AI and infrastructure. How do we build the AI-powered analytics engines of the future in a reliable and scalable way?

title: Context, Governance, Infrastructure: The Stack AI Data Agents Don't Have
abstract: AI agents can build pipelines, generate dbt models, evolve Snowflake schemas and write quality tests. But, they lack three pieces essential for production-grade data engineering demands: 1) context about your data stack and business, 2) governance to enforce access controls, policies and cost constraints, and 3) infrastructure to validate changes before they hit your warehouse. Pradnesh Patil, Co-Founder of Altimate.ai, shares the missing stack for agentic data engineering. Watch agents catch schema drift, self-correct hallucinated models and enforce guardrails in Snowflake and across the entire data platform.

speakers.job_title: Senior Director, Enterprise Data & AI Platform
speakers.bio: Ra serves as the Head of Advanced Data Engineering and Analytics at Zscaler, leading the architecture, strategy, and execution of the Enterprise Data & AI Platform. With a focus on scalability, security, and business alignment, he drives the development of robust data pipelines, transformation processes, and seamless integration of diverse data sources to empower innovation across the organization. As a forward thinking leader, he also champions the operationalization of machine learning workflows and the adoption of Generative AI to unlock advanced analytics, automate processes, and drive strategic impact

Pradnesh Patil is the Co-founder and CEO of Altimate AI, a platform that brings agentic AI to data engineering — helping teams automate Snowflake platform management, optimize warehouse performance, build cross-platform data pipelines, and accelerate workload migrations to Snowflake. Altimate AI's open-source tools are used by thousands of data engineers daily, and the platform is trusted by Fortune 500s. Prior to Altimate AI, Pradnesh spent over a decade in product leadership roles at Palo Alto Networks, Cisco, and VMware, building enterprise data, security, and AI products at scale.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Context Is the New Code: AI Engineering with Cortex
abstract: As AI becomes capable of writing SQL and building data pipelines, the real bottleneck is no longer code — it’s context. Many enterprise AI initiatives struggle not because the models are weak, but because they lack the business logic, definitions and operational nuance that reside within human expertise. In this session, we’ll explore a practical approach to encoding both business and technical context so Snowflake Cortex AI can generate reliable, enterprise-grade data workflows. The result: AI that doesn’t just write code, but operates like a true enterprise data engineer.

speakers.bio: Karthik S. Raman is the Head of Snowflake Practice at LTM, where he leads initiatives to help organizations unlock the full potential of the Snowflake AI Data Cloud. With over 23 years of experience in data architecture, artificial intelligence, and cloud data solutions, he brings deep expertise in driving enterprise-scale data transformations.His core strengths include Cloud Data Modernization, Data Engineering, Architecture Consulting, and Data Governance, enabling organizations to build secure, scalable, and high-performance data platforms across leading cloud ecosystems. Karthik is passionate about translating complex data challenges into impactful, business-driven solutions.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Cortex AI Functions: Unstructured Data in Manufacturing and Customer Analytics
abstract: Unlock the power of your unstructured data by turning manual bottlenecks into high-speed engines for growth. In this session, we explore how Snowflake Cortex AI revolutionized two critical workflows: manufacturing verification and customer sentiment analysis. By leveraging Cortex AI Functions and Streamlit, we slashed document verification times from 340 minutes to just 10 (a 97% reduction) and accelerated feedback parsing by 90%. Join us to learn how to transform complex PDFs and subjective comments into objective, AI-ready assets that drive faster, more strategic decision-making.

speakers.bio:
Yuta Watanabe leads business transformation through data at Panasonic Connect. He believes that data-driven success depends not simply on which platform is chosen, but on how organizational culture evolves.

With this mindset, he has positioned Snowflake as Panasonic Connect’s standard data platform, advancing a Single Source of Truth across the organization.

Guided by the principle of "teaching teams how to fish rather than giving them fish," he focuses on building self-driven data capabilities. He is passionate about making data and AI real drivers of business change.

Kotone Yoshioka works in the Customer Success Promotion team within the Gemba Solutions Company at Panasonic Connect, where she leads initiatives to improve customer satisfaction through data-driven insights. She believes that effective action comes from data-driven decision making. With this mindset, she uses Snowflake as a central data platform and Tableau for analytics to connect distributed enterprise data with customer satisfaction survey results, enabling deeper insights and stronger internal feedback loops. She is also the first Tableau DATA Saber certified professional in her department and promotes frontline-driven DX and a data-driven culture across the organization.

title: Cortex Code for Full Lifecycle Development
abstract:
Cortex Code is superb for "help me build a Snowflake object."

In this session, we prove that Cortex Code enables data engineers to achieve a true 50% or more improvement every day; show how it works with DataOps.live's Metis capabilities to extend the ask to "help me deliver something into production"; and demonstrate how, with the right tooling and workflow around Cortex Code, productivity can improve by 400%. We demonstrate building and deploying complete applications and data products onto Snowflake, all the way into production, covering development, QA and production. We'll also show how debugging, troubleshooting and bug fixing become trivial with Cortex Code and DataOps.live.

speakers.bio: Guy Adams is the CTO & co-founder of DataOps.live as well as a Snowflake Global #1 Data SuperHero. An experienced CTO & VP who is passionate about DataOps. He's spent 20+ years running software development organisations and now his focus is bringing the principles and business value from DevOps and CI/CD to data. Co-founder of the truedataops.org movement.  Also Dad, technologist, (over) engineer, amateur inventor, skier, and mildly eccentric.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: DaaS on Snowflake: Designing for Scale, Governance and Decision Intelligence
abstract: As analytics adoption within Comcast Supply Chain scaled from hundreds to more than 1,000 users, traditional data products revealed limitations in reuse, consistency and governance. We implemented data as a service (DaaS) on Snowflake, enabling governed, scalable data consumption across a diverse user base. We walk through the architecture, operating model and lessons learned, showing how DaaS provides a resilient foundation for intelligent decision workflows and future agent-based capabilities — while maintaining flexibility, trust and strong governance.

speakers.bio:
Bonny Joy is the Director of BI & Analytics for the Comcast Supply Chain Organization—covering Residential, Xfinity Mobile, and Networks—where he leads the data modernization toolkit focused on AI and scaling intelligence. He shapes the team’s data strategy and the evolution of Data Products and Services that enable self‑service analytics, operational reporting, and Decision Intelligence.

He has advanced the team from fragmented reporting to a strategic, AI‑enriched environment supported by strong governance, observability, and data product best practices. He works across engineering, field, and business teams to strengthen the partnerships that drive a data‑first culture.

title: Data Discovery and Semantic Context for Agentic AI with Horizon Catalog
abstract: Agentic AI can turn data into action, but only if it can find the right data and understand it. That's a challenge since most enterprise data isn’t sufficiently self-describing. In this session, we’ll define what AI-ready data looks like and show how Horizon Catalog provides the critical context layer that helps AI know your data. Horizon Catalog delivers quality-aware context directly to AI agents — so they don’t just access data, they understand it. It also catalogs data from external systems, captures end-to-end lineage and usage patterns, and automates documentation and semantic modeling. Join us to see how a strong metadata foundation enables AI that’s more accurate and trustworthy.

speakers.bio: Riccardo Muti is a Principal Product Manager at Snowflake focusing on search & discovery capabilities in Horizon Catalog and Snowflake Marketplace. He has over 15 years’ experience building business intelligence and database platforms. Prior to Snowflake, he held product management roles at Google working on Looker Studio and BigQuery, and at Microsoft working on Power BI and SQL Server Reporting Services.

title: Data Engineering for AI: dbt Projects, Semantic Views and Cortex Code
abstract: Make your data AI-ready with the powerful combination of dbt Projects and Snowflake Semantic Views. This session demonstrates how to leverage both SQL and Python DataFrame models in dbt for flexible data engineering that appeals to Spark developers and SQL practitioners alike. Explore semantic layers, the Open Semantic Initiative (OSI) and best practices for managing semantic view lifecycles in Snowflake. Watch Cortex Code's AI coding agent accelerate dbt Project development in real time, and learn to automate deployments with Snowflake CLI. Walk away with a complete, modern approach to building AI-ready data pipelines that empower both AI models and analytics tools.

speakers.bio: Jeremiah is a Principal Architect in the Field CTO office at Snowflake, leading the Data Engineering workload there. He has an extensive background in data engineering and DevOps, having spent the past 20 years focused on it as a developer, architect, dev manager, consultant leader, sales engineer, and currently Field CTO. He is a frequent speaker and blogger on data architecture and engineering.

title: Data Exfiltration and Ransomware Protection: Guardrails for Regulated Industries
abstract: Implement a robust zero trust architecture to safeguard highly regulated data from contemporary threats. This session focuses on establishing advanced guardrails, network and identity boundaries to prevent data exfiltration at its origin. Attain superior cyber resilience and proactive ransomware protection by neutralizing unauthorized access. We will demonstrate the integration of DSPM for comprehensive visibility, leveraging insights to inform the new data movement policies and restrict high-risk information to approved zones. Conclude by exploring how the trust center serves as your unified security analytics hub for real-time risk monitoring and an audit-ready security posture.

speakers.bio: Prasoon is a staff product manager at Snowflake, owning various aspects of database security. Before that, he led products at Uber, Balbix, Splunk, and Palo Alto Networks spanning domains from risk posture management, enterprise IAM, behavioral threat detection, security analytics, cryptography, encryption, etc. In the last 14 years, Prasoon has explored cybersecurity through the roles of a security practitioner, product guy, marketer, and above all a student of the field.

Seth has over 20 years experience in the field of Cybersecurity data governance and data privacy. He covered both the public sector, and highly regulated industry globally. In August 2020, Seth is currently working as global Field CTO, focussing on the security, security data lake, data governance, and data privacy

title: Data Governance to AI Innovation: Camden Bank ROI with Qlik Cortex in FS AI
abstract: Camden National Bank is launching Snowflake Cortex AI to cut insight cycles from weeks to minutes and deliver real-time customer growth insights for executives and frontline teams. But the bank started out on Snowflake with a data team of two. Learn how Camden National Bank modernized its platform using Snowflake on AWS and Qlik, using Passerelle’s Data Rocket to migrate over 600 million records. Hear how it built success, including a 250% increase in analytics output, certified data marts validating 99% of enterprise data and efficiencies that freed resources and funded new analytics roles. Then, discover how Camden National Bank is operationalizing Snowflake Intelligence across the organization.

speakers.bio: Joshua M. Nash is an SVP and Director of Technology & Analytics at Camden National Bank, where he leads enterprise data, analytics, and technology strategy in a highly regulated financial services environment. With over a decade of leadership experience, he has driven digital transformation initiatives including cloud migration and the implementation of a modern BI stack using Snowflake, Talend, and Tableau to enable real‑time insights and data‑driven decision‑making. Joshua is an established presenter at business intelligence and Snowflake conferences across New England and brings a practical, governance‑focused perspective on building scalable and trusted data platforms.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Data Orchestration to Activation for Snowflake with Enterprise MCP & Agentic AI
abstract: Snowflake is where your most trusted data lives. But maximizing its value means solving two things: getting data in efficiently and getting intelligence back out in real time. In this session, Workato's Field CTO for Data, Avinash Deshpande, walks through end-to-end Snowflake orchestration, from high-performance ingestion and native push-down transformations to reverse ETL and enterprise MCP servers that let AI agents query and act on trusted Snowflake data instantly.

speakers.bio: Avinash Deshpande is a technology leader specializing in enterprise automation, data orchestration, and AI-driven operations. As Field CDO at Workato, he helps organizations transform fragmented systems into connected, insight-driven ecosystems. With expertise in MCP and agentic AI, he enables secure, governed, real-time workflows that power AI-ready enterprises. Avinash partners with Fortune 500 leaders and collaborates across product and engineering to advance intelligent, context-rich automation at scale.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: dbt Fusion Engine: What's New and How to Upgrade on Snowflake
abstract: The dbt Fusion engine is here, and if you're running dbt on Snowflake, this is the upgrade you've been waiting for. With native Snowflake ADBC/Arrow integration, Fusion delivers faster query previews, snappier CI runs, and IDE-quality autocomplete and inline errors, all while generating rich semantic metadata that makes your dbt project understandable to both humans and LLMs. In this session, we'll walk through enabling Fusion in dbt on Snowflake and show you exactly what changes in your development experience — including the gotchas — to know before you flip the switch in production.

speakers.bio: Connie Carey is a staff product manager at dbt Labs with 10+ years of experience building data products across MarTech, Product Analytics, and Data Infrastructure. She's currently focused on helping dbt users onboard to the new dbt Fusion engine with confidence on platforms like Snowflake. She's passionate about improving the developer experience for data engineering workflows for both humans and agents.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Deliver Reliable, Explainable, Traceable Agents with dbt
abstract: Agents can generate outputs in seconds, but without visibility into their reasoning, data sources and logic, teams can't trust them in production. This session shows how dbt powers trusted Cortex Agents through a governed context layer and high-precision retrieval — ensuring agents start from the right models and metrics, cite their sources, align to approved definitions and fail safely when context is unavailable. Using dbt agent workflows, you can deploy autonomous agents with traceable, reviewable outputs. Leave with a practical framework for moving from agent demos to production-ready deployments you can trust.

speakers.bio: Chakshu Mehta is a product marketing manager at dbt Labs focused on AI products.  She works closely with data and AI teams to define how the space is evolving and leads go-to-market for dbt's AI capabilities. By telling practitioner stories, she brings to life one of the harder problems in data right now: how data teams move from experimenting with AI to actually deploying it.

Sam Ferguson is a Staff Product Designer at dbt Labs, designing interfaces and workflows to help power AI-first analytics engineering. Her work focuses on how modern data teams interact with, trust, and scale AI-assisted data development across cloud data platforms.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Deploying Geospatial Solutions Using Cortex Code Skills
abstract: Discover how Cortex Code skills can transform complex geospatial deployments into guided, repeatable workflows. This session demonstrates a real-world Fleet Intelligence solutions for retail, manufacturing, and travel and hospitality that perform sophisticated geospatial analytics, all deployed through an AI-assisted skill. Attendees will learn how to package domain expertise into reusable skills that handle template variables, validate prerequisites, execute idempotent SQL and provide self-healing error recovery, making advanced geospatial solutions accessible to any Snowflake user.

speakers.bio: After working in 18 years as a Solution Engineer in a variety of companies including Snowflake, Becky has since moved to the enablement team, where she produces hands on experiences  using Snowflake. Her Special interest is end to end advanced analytics.
Piotr works with large enterprises to design and scale Snowflake AI Data Cloud. He is an active thought leader contributing to architectural best practices with focus on emerging technologies.

title: Deploying Geospatial Solutions via Natural Language with Cortex Code
abstract: This session demonstrates how to transform natural language requests into deployment-ready geospatial applications using Cortex Code. You will learn how to parameterize solution templates and extend logic to support organization-specific KPIs. Discover how Snowflake enables businesses to optimize delivery routes, increase fleet utilization, and reduce both idle time and operational costs. Attendees will gain practical knowledge on using Cortex Code to deploy and customize geospatial solutions within the Snowflake native stack. All session materials, including code and templates, will be made publicly available for immediate use.

speakers.bio: After working in 18 years as a Solution Engineer in a variety of companies including Snowflake, Becky has since moved to the enablement team, where she produces hands on experiences  using Snowflake. Her Special interest is end to end advanced analytics.
Piotr works with large enterprises to design and scale Snowflake AI Data Cloud. He is an active thought leader contributing to architectural best practices with focus on emerging technologies.

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Deploy, Version, Test and Manage Snowflake Openflow Connectors Using Cortex Code
abstract: Join us to learn and see recommended ways for addressing DevOps requirements when it comes to Snowflake Openflow Connectors. A demo will show how it is possible to deploy an Snowflake Openflow Connector using a Cortex Code agent, make changes in the connector based on specific business requirements, version control the changes, configure GitHub automated testing and automate deployment of the new version of the connector in a production environment. See the entire process from a flow problem statement, to development, all the way up to production using the Cortex Code agent.

speakers.bio: Pierre Villard has been contributing to Apache NiFi for over 10 years and serves as a member of the project's PMC. His career has been rooted in data flow, cloud integration, and large-scale data movement. Pierre joined Snowflake through the acquisition of Datavolo in 2024 and is now part of the Openflow engineering team, bringing his extensive open-source experience to Snowflake's data platform.

speakers.job_title: Principal Architect, Data Engineering, Applied Field Engineering Office

title: Designing a Low-Latency Warehouse for 1.5M Daily Queries
abstract: Serving 1.5 million daily queries demands architectural precision. This session breaks down workload isolation, clustering strategy, caching optimization and data modeling patterns used to achieve consistently low latency at scale. Learn the trade-offs and engineering decisions that enabled sub-second performance under heavy concurrency.

speakers.bio: Avinash Enugu is a Senior Development Manager with 12+ years of experience building enterprise data warehouses and high‑impact data products. He focuses on modern data platforms, scalable analytics, and leading teams to turn complex data into actionable business insights.

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Ditch DIY, Build with AI: Leverage Agents and SDK for Custom Connectors
abstract: Building custom data connectors often requires weeks of engineering effort, ongoing maintenance and complex API integrations. In this demo, see how Fivetran’s AI Connector Agent and Connector SDK use AI to generate production-ready connectors for REST API-based sources — automatically handling authentication, pagination and schema mapping — and deliver data into Snowflake in minutes. Learn how data teams can quickly connect unsupported or custom sources without writing integration code or managing fragile pipelines. Discover how Fivetran's AI Connector Agent provides a faster, more reliable alternative to DIY builds, reducing engineering overhead so teams can focus on unlocking value from data.

speakers.job_title: Sr. Director Global Partner Sales Eng
speakers.bio: Kelly Kohlleffel leads Fivetran’s Global Partner Sales Engineering organization, working with technology and consulting partners to deliver modern data and AI solutions. He also hosts the Fivetran Data Podcast, where industry leaders share their data journeys, visions, and challenges. Prior to Fivetran, Kelly held leadership and sales roles at NTT Data, Hashmap, Hortonworks, and Oracle. Connect with Kelly on LinkedIn for insights on data, AI, and partnerships.

A software and data engineering expert who was building big data architectures before the term was coined, David Hrncir is on a mission to automate the "boring stuff." Currently driving technical partnerships at Fivetran, David is a fierce advocate for Open Data Infrastructure (ODI) and AI-driven automation. He focuses on removing the friction of manual data integration, allowing companies to pivot from "managing pipelines" to "delivering outcomes." A philosopher at heart with a "no challenge is too big" attitude, David is a frequent speaker and panelist dedicated to making the world of data integration more efficient—and a lot more fun.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Driving Continuous Price-Performance Optimization at T-Mobile with Snowflake
abstract: Optimization isn’t a one-time event — it’s a continuous discipline. T-Mobile shares how they embedded telemetry-driven decision-making, automated cost controls and workload segmentation to sustain price-performance improvements over time. Learn how to operationalize optimization as an ongoing capability.

speakers.bio: Chris James, T-Mobile's Principal Architect, brings 22 years of expertise in Data Warehouse strategy, Architecture, Administration, and Performance optimization. He led the transition to Snowflake, enhancing analytics and cost-efficiency. His role was pivotal in steering T-Mobile's data infrastructure towards efficiency, reliability, and scalability, ensuring that the company's data management systems are not just robust but also future-proof. As a respected thought leader, Chris has shared insights at various conferences, showcasing his dedication and leadership in modern data management, benefiting both T-Mobile and the wider tech community.

Nicolae Vasu is a data platform engineer with over 20 years of experience in Oracle and large-scale data systems. He began his career as an Oracle developer and DBA, later joining Oracle Advanced Customer Services, where he supported critical production environments and high-severity incidents. In the U.S., he worked on large Oracle RAC clusters before joining T-Mobile in 2016. At T-Mobile, he has built and supported data platforms including Oracle, Hadoop (OneData), and Snowflake, focusing on data replication, monitoring, reliability, and cost optimization. He currently focuses on Snowflake performance and storage efficiency.

title: Driving Efficiency and Cost Savings with Snowflake Native to Iceberg
abstract: Enterprises often run Snowflake native tables alongside open formats, driving duplicate storage, complex governance and higher costs. Capital One partnered with Slalom and Snowflake to migrate Collaboration and Sandbox workloads from Snowflake native to Iceberg tables in OneLake (S3). This session covers the target architecture, migration and automation patterns, and how defaulting table creation to Iceberg simplified adoption. Attendees will learn how this approach reduced vendor lock-in, eliminated redundant storage and delivered measurable cost savings at scale.

speakers.bio:
Temesgen (Teme) Meheret is a Director of Engineering at Capital One, leading the team responsible for the optimal usage and risk management of Snowflake.

Previously the VP of Engineering for Data & Intelligence at Amobee/Videology, Teme is a specialist in large-scale data efficiency. He is a vocal advocate for the lakehouse strategy, focusing on giving customers total control of their data and the flexibility to bring specialized compute engines directly to their data lake.

Karpagam Balakrishnan is a Senior Architect specializing in modern data platforms, cloud data engineering, and enterprise migrations. She leads large-scale transformations, modernizing legacy data warehouses into scalable, cloud‑native platforms using Snowflake and open table formats. Her work focuses on resilient architectures for ingestion, transformation, governance, and analytics. Partnering with engineering and platform teams, she delivers automated, cost‑efficient solutions that improve reliability, simplify operations, and accelerate analytics at enterprise scale.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Driving Fan Engagement with the NBA’s Fan Data Platform on Snowflake
abstract: The NBA's Fan Data Platform (FDP) is the central hub for collecting, mastering and activating fan interactions across the entire NBA ecosystem. In this session, we'll showcase how Snowflake powers the FDP using a modified medallion architecture, Dynamic Tables and a configuration-driven approach to manage diverse ingests and processing pipelines, minimizing custom work and maximizing flexibility. We’ll share lessons from our migration from Databricks to Snowflake. Attendees will see how the FDP enables downstream reporting, integrations and activations that deliver personalized experiences and drive fan engagement across a global sports ecosystem.

speakers.bio: Colin Bowers is a Senior Staff Data Engineer at the NBA, where he leads engineering for the league’s Fan Data Platform.
