He oversees platform strategy, architecture, and development for systems that collect, unify, and activate fan data across the global NBA ecosystem. Colin has led the evolution of the NBA’s fan data platform, including guiding its transition from on-premise infrastructure to Snowflake. He specializes in data platform architecture, customer data systems, and warehouse migrations, and enjoys solving hard technical problems that help grow the game of basketball. Based in Atlanta, he enjoys spending time with his family, running, watching sports, and smoking meats.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: This session is currently open to Marketer's Forum registrants. Registration will open to the general event audience closer to the event.

title: Driving Innovation in Global Clinical Operations Through AI
abstract:
The mandate for clinical operations is clear: Deliver medicines with unprecedented speed. Through Program EAGLE, GSK is transforming clinical trials by integrating AI and autonomous agents into its core. In this session, Colin Weller shares how GSK achieves measurable business outcomes on Snowflake. We explore how Program EAGLE:

  • Shortens drug lifecycles: Accelerating workflows with AI agents to automate complex data mapping
  • Drives patient outcomes: Using predictive insights for precision recruitment
  • Scales efficiency: Shifting to intelligent, automated trial management

Learn how GSK turns data into a strategic asset to get ahead of disease together.

speakers.bio: Barry Moore is a technology leader in the pharmaceutical industry with over 20 years of experience delivering clinical trial and supply chain systems. At GSK, he leads initiatives to ensure medicines reach patients safely and efficiently at global scale. His work spans RTSM, EDC, CTMS, and ERP platforms, with a growing focus on AI and data-driven transformation. Barry specializes in applying AI to improve forecasting, optimize supply, and enhance data integrity, bringing a practical, execution-focused approach to deploying AI in regulated clinical environments.

title: Dynamic Tables Deep Dive
abstract: Dynamic Tables is the new default for change data and incremental processing. Join us to learn about the latest features and innovations powering Snowflake’s declarative data pipelines — breaking the boundaries between batch and streaming pipelines. This session will provide an overview of Dynamic Tables and explore its key benefits, top use cases, latest improvements and newest capabilities. It will also feature a customer sharing their experience deploying Dynamic Tables and exploring the latest innovations.

speakers.bio: PD Dutta is a product manager at Snowflake responsible for Dynamic Tables. Dynamic Tables let teams use simple SQL statements to declaratively define the result of your data pipelines. Dynamic Tables also automatically refresh as the data changes, only operating on new changes since the last refresh. The scheduling and orchestration needed to achieve this are also transparently managed by Snowflake.  Prior to joining Snowflake, PD held product management roles at Microsoft Azure, AWS and NetApp.

title: Enabling Enterprise AI at Scale: Mitsui’s Federated AI Mesh
abstract: Mitsui & Co. is tackling one of the biggest barriers to enterprise AI: fragmented data spread across independently operated businesses. In this session, Mitsui and NTT DATA will demonstrate how they are leveraging Snowflake to build a "Federated AI Mesh" — a solution that makes data AI-ready without the need for forced centralization. Attendees will walk away with a practical blueprint for scaling AI, enabling use-case sharing across 25+ group companies globally, and accelerating agentic AI and BI using Secure Data Sharing, Snowflake Openflow and Cortex AI.

speakers.bio: I spearhead a global data initiative at Mitsui & Co., one of Japan’s largest conglomerates, democratizing AI and data-driven decision-making across our worldwide group. From the ground up, I built strategic partnerships with over five leading U.S. tech firms—including Snowflake—and scaled the initiative globally. Leading a cross-functional team, I drive innovation and operational transformation. With 15+ years at the company, my leadership spans diverse sectors: tech investments, retail business development, shale oil & gas, and renewable energy. I am passionate about applying data-driven management to traditional industries, unlocking new value through data and AI.

speakers.job_title: Director, Data & AI | Snowflake Data Superhero
speakers.bio: Ryo Shibuya is a highly accomplished Data &AI Architect with a wealth of experience in the field. With a career spanning over a decade, Ryo has been instrumental in implementing data analytics and AI platform solutions for various organizations. Notably, Ryo played a key role in the early adoption of Snowflake technology in the Japanese market, spearheading transformative change. Ryo's expertise extends to providing technical consulting for numerous Snowflake implementation projects in the U.S. and global. For his supreme Snowflake expertise, Snowflake awarded Ryo the title of Snowflake Data Superhero, and he is considered a true thought leader in the industry.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: End‑to‑End Data Management for Snowflake: From Design to Delivered Value
abstract: Most organizations don’t struggle with data — they struggle with the upstream work required to make data usable. This session shows how to close the gaps between design-before-ingest, modeling, pipeline construction, governance and productization so what enters Snowflake is already clean, structured and aligned to business meaning. You’ll learn how to streamline the flow from upstream definition to downstream consumption, automate delivery of governed data products, and gain visibility into performance, cost and usage so Snowflake runs as cleanly as the pipelines feeding it.

speakers.bio: Mark Gowdy is the Chief Partner Technologist at Quest where he helps shape strategy and drive co-innovation for key technology partners across all solution areas for Quest, including Data, Cybersecurity and Platform Modernization. With over 25 years in data management, observability, replication, and cloud optimization he helps global enterprises build trusted data, security, and platform foundations for AI by aligning modern data architectures and identity centric security with real world outcomes.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Enforcing Data Governance at the Orchestration Layer
abstract:
Financial institutions don't struggle with governance frameworks. They struggle with enforceability. When regulators ask how an AI decision was produced, most teams cannot prove which data ran, which controls were applied or who approved exceptions. Governance on paper is not governance at runtime.

This session shows how leading institutions use orchestration to codify policy, enforce controls, capture audit trails and gate high-risk decisions before they reach agents. We'll ground this in real-world patterns from financial institutions using Airflow and Astro to bring governance and control to their most complex data pipelines.

speakers.bio: Carter Page is the EVP of R&D at Astronomer, the home of the modern data orchestration platform. A 30-year veteran of enterprise data infrastructure, Carter previously led data engineering at Two Sigma, overseeing the firm's mission-critical global data pipelines. Prior to Two Sigma, Carter spent a decade at Google, where he led the development of many of the early products and technologies of GCP, including Cloud Bigtable.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Enterprise Data Agents for Public Safety: Insights from Virginia State Police
abstract: Public safety agencies must navigate a complex landscape of legacy systems, evolving threats, and increasing demands for transparency and efficiency. This session will explore the real-world challenges and strategic decisions involved in transforming data infrastructure within a law enforcement environment. Attendees will gain practical insights into balancing innovation with compliance, improving data accessibility without compromising security, and aligning IT initiatives with the broader goals of public service. Whether you're in the public or private sector, this talk will offer valuable takeaways on leading data transformation in high-integrity environments.

speakers.bio:
Steven Maclauchlan is the Chief Data Officer at the Virginia State Police, where he leads strategic data initiatives to modernize and secure the agency’s information systems. With over 15 years of experience in data architecture, analytics, and digital transformation, Steven is passionate about using data to drive meaningful change in organizations.
Prior to joining VSP, Steven held leadership roles in consulting and data strategy, including serving as Head of Data at Ippon Technologies. He has worked across industries and continents helping organizations unlock the value of their data through modern platforms, governance frameworks, and AI-driven insights.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Eval-Driven Agent Optimization with Cortex Code CLI
abstract: Transform agent debugging from guesswork into a measurable process. Using Cortex Code's agent-optimization skill and native Snowflake Cortex Agent Evaluations, you'll curate evaluation data sets from production logs, run benchmarks with metrics such as answer_correctness and tool_selection_accuracy, analyze failure patterns and iterate on agent instructions — all from the CLI. You'll practice the closed-loop workflow: benchmark → analyze failures → improve → validate. Walk away with a repeatable methodology for taking any Cortex Agents from prototype to production readiness.

speakers.bio: Elliott is a Principal AI/ML Architect on the Applied Field Engineering team at Snowflake. He joined Snowflake in May of 2024 as part of the TruEra acquisition. Elliott has spent much of his career focusing on explainability and observability for ML and GenAI applications. Outside of work you can find Elliott enjoying the mountains in his home state of Colorado!

I'm a developer advocate at Snowflake who gets excited about helping developers do cool things with AI and data in the cloud. After a decade of building developer communities at Hume AI, Bill.com, and Clover Network, I found my happy place bridging the gap between complex tech and the developers who want to use it. Think of me as a technical matchmaker – connecting developers with the right tools and watching the magic happen. When I'm not geeking out about AI, you'll find me spending time with my son and collecting new hobbies (because why stick to just one?).

title: Exploding the BOM: Advanced Analytics for Manufacturing Decision-Making
abstract: Are you ready to unlock the full potential of your Bill of Materials (BOM) data? Join us to discover how L3Harris Technologies revolutionized BOM management for its core product portfolio using Snowflake’s cutting-edge features. We’ll explore how recursive SQL, Dynamic Tables and elastic compute scaling can transform static BOMs into a dynamic, time-aware, fully exploded data asset — delivering insights that were previously out of reach. Learn how this approach empowers manufacturing teams to spot engineering change patterns, track component obsolescence and proactively address supply chain risks.

speakers.bio: Luke Bamburoski serves as Segment Data Architect at L3Harris, where he leads analytics architecture for the Communications & Spectrum Dominance segment. Holding an MS in Business Analytics from the University of Rochester's Simon Business School, he has built advanced analytics capabilities within his segment while sharing implementation guidance across the organization. Luke brings practical insights on accelerating data access, strengthening governance, and generating actionable insights at enterprise scale.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: ExxonMobil's Enterprise Data Transformation with Marketplace Capacity Drawdown
abstract: Discover how ExxonMobil transformed its data landscape from 7,000 fragmented data products scattered across 20 legacy platforms into a single, enterprisewide central data hub. Learn how it leveraged the Snowflake Marketplace Capacity Drawdown (MCD) program to consolidate 350 governed data products powering more than 250 use cases while providing flexible payment options across multiple lines of business. This session covers practical strategies for large-scale data modernization, including managing complex budget structures across business units, derisking multiyear platform investments and using MCD to accelerate partner tool adoption.

speakers.bio: Dylan is a seasoned data professional with 7 years of experience spanning full stack development, data science, and data warehousing. Currently serving as a System Architect within Data Warehousing focusing on Snowflake.

speakers.job_title: Director of Global Operation Data Warehouse & Big Data (Product Manager)
speakers.bio:
Wayne Frawner is an accomplished Information Technology Product Manager with extensive experience in the oil and energy sector. With a strong foundation in both business and technology, Wayne brings a strategic mindset and hands-on technical expertise to every project. He is highly skilled in data warehousing and engineering, infrastructure automation, and troubleshooting across both Windows and Linux environments.

 He holds an MBA from The University of Texas at Austin and a BA from Mississippi State University, combining technical acumen with business insight to bridge the gap between IT and enterprise goals.

title: From 12 to 200: Scaling U.S. Bank’s Data Product Strategy with Snowflake
abstract: U.S. Bank is transforming data from a siloed asset into a scalable product, growing its internal marketplace from 12 to 200 AI-ready data products by 2027. This session explores how it's empowering domain teams to curate data products using Snowflake Internal Marketplace and Dynamic Tables, while aligning them with business outcomes such as revenue modeling and forecasting. Learn how the CDAO office uses "data ambassadors" to drive enterprise-wide adoption, and discover their roadmap for decentralizing data ownership while maintaining central governance.

speakers.job_title: VP of Data Platform Engineering Lead
speakers.bio: Disciplined, self-driven, highly effective technology leader & lifelong learner with a strong result leadership and demonstrated history of successes working in IT and banking/financial services industry with emphasis on data & analytics, automation and AI.

title: From Chaos to Consistency: Using Snowflake Cortex to Standardize Fraud Labels
abstract: At BioCatch, confirmed fraud labels provided by customers underpin our reporting, research and machine learning models. With over 200 customers across multiple regions and languages, these labels arrive in highly varied and inconsistent forms, creating significant operational challenges. In this session, we demonstrate how we use Snowflake Cortex AI in a multistage pipeline to automatically reclassify customer-provided fraud labels into standardized taxonomies. This approach enables scalable automation, improves downstream model training and introduces objective measures of label quality to help identify and address weaknesses in customer fraud processes.

speakers.bio: I am a leader of a global team of fraud fighters. Our North Star is to fight the good fight against cybercriminals and ultimately protect your every day person from suffering at the hands of these groups. With a PhD in Forensic Science, I have spent the last 15 years in the analytics space, the most recent 9 years specifically in Fraud.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: From Customer to Co‑Creator: Aviv’s Role in Shaping dbt Projects on Snowflake
abstract: When a KKR‑backed European classifieds group committed to rebuilding its data platform in under six months, it also reframed Snowflake as a co‑builder rather than a vendor. Aviv and Snowflake formed a joint squad of product managers, engineers and data practitioners focused on dbt Projects on Snowflake. Aviv gained faster, cleaner delivery of five revenue‑critical data products and a single governed way of working. Attendees will see how Snowflake gained a demanding early adopter whose real‑world requirements and design reviews directly shaped the maturity, usability and roadmap of dbt Projects.

speakers.bio: I am the Product Manager of dbt Projects on Snowflake. I spend each day obsessing over the details of how I can make the critical workflows of Data Engineers easier on Snowflake and building scalable, world-class technologies.

speakers.job_title: Director of Data Engineering (reporting into Chief Data and AI Officer)
speakers.bio: António Costa is a Partner at NewData.Tech with 20+ years of experience in data and technology, focused on turning complexity into durable business value. He works with organizations to shape data and AI strategy, build strong foundations, and deliver scalable, reusable data products with measurable impact. Through one engagement, António became Director of Data Engineering at AVIV, where he is leading the rebuild of the Snowflake-based data platform powering some of Europe’s largest real-estate classifieds. Previously, he led high-performing data teams at companies like OLX Group and is an active voice in the Portuguese and European tech and data ecosystems.

title: From Data Chaos to Clarity: The Intelligent Data Marketplace Blueprint
abstract: Every enterprise is racing to turn data into intelligence, yet most struggle with fragmented tools and inconsistent governance. This session shares how we built an Intelligent Internal Marketplace powered by Snowflake Horizon Catalog for trust and governance and Cortex Code for AI-driven discovery. Learn how automated tagging, lineage and intelligent search make data instantly findable, reliable and ready for action. Attendees will explore a practical framework and real-world lessons to unify governance, AI and discovery — driving adoption, data literacy and faster, trusted business outcomes.

speakers.bio: Rafique Awan is a Strategic Data and Solution Architect with deep expertise in modern data platforms, AI integration, and enterprise data strategy. He leads initiatives that unify governance, data products, and AI to accelerate business value. At his firm, he’s driving the adoption of Snowflake Horizon, Cortex, and internal data marketplaces to transform data discovery, trust, and collaboration across domains. Rafique is passionate about simplifying complexity and enabling data-driven innovation.

Youssef is a Solutions Engineer at Snowflake, where he partners with some of the company’s largest enterprise customers to design scalable data architectures and build modern platforms on the Snowflake Data Cloud. With experience supporting organizations across multiple industries, including financial services, he works closely with technical and business leaders to modernize data foundations and prepare them for the next era of AI-driven innovation.

title: From Data to Dialogue: Making AI Useful in the Business
abstract: As AI capabilities become native within Snowflake, the challenge shifts from experimentation to real-world application. In this session, Under Armour shares how it is enabling conversational access to data across sales, product and consumer domains. Learn how users interact with both structured and unstructured data through natural language powered by Snowflake Cortex AI. The session focuses on the patterns, data foundations and governance approaches supporting these solutions today, along with practical considerations for teams looking to apply Snowflake’s AI capabilities to real business use cases.

speakers.bio: Jeff leads Enterprise Data Platform Engineering at Under Armour, where he builds and operates the data platform powering analytics and AI across a global organization. He focuses on enabling self-service, scaling AI use cases, and balancing speed with governance across diverse data domains. With experience spanning analytics, architecture, and data management, Jeff brings a pragmatic approach to simplifying complexity and driving measurable adoption of modern data and AI capabilities.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: From Data to Outcomes: How Ecolab Uses Snowflake ML to Deliver Customer Value
abstract: Imagine every customer location performing at its best — every shift, every day. In this session, hear how Ecolab turned raw operational data into ML models that show exactly what each site needs to reach peak performance. We'll take you inside the journey: where the spark began, how teams pushed through complexity to find signal in the noise, and the moment explainable AI began revealing actionable growth opportunities hiding in plain sight. See how predictive modeling, fast iteration and a shared obsession with customer outcomes translated into stronger relationships, measurable revenue gains and a new blueprint for AI-driven value creation.

speakers.bio: Maddie Thies is Director of Enterprise Growth Operations at Ecolab, where she leads Best‑in‑Class and value‑based growth initiatives for strategic enterprise customers. Her work focuses on translating customer priorities into scalable tools, KPIs, and activation models that drive measurable business outcomes through close partnership with sales, marketing, and technology teams.
Jesse Luebbert partners with Snowflake customers to advise and deliver on AI/ML projects.

title: From Evaluation to Co-Innovation: How SoFi Built a Semantic Layer with Snowflake
abstract: In 2024, SoFi chose to co-develop a semantic layer directly with Snowflake rather than buying a third-party tool. Join us to learn how this strategic partnership transformed our data strategy. This session goes beyond the standard implementation story. We’ll share how we deployed the semantic layer within the SoFi data platform to establish a "single source of truth" and create the backbone for our gen AI and self-serve capabilities. We will unpack our real-world architecture, the challenges we faced and tactical lessons learned so you can accelerate your own journey.

speakers.bio: Sreekanth is a Senior Manager at SoFi Data Platform, leading the Data Insights charter to build central Data Products for diverse business units. He oversees the Experimentation Platform for A/B testing and leads the development of AI-driven foundational capabilities, including Semantic Layers and conversational analytics, to unlock insights across the firm. Prior to SoFi, Sreekanth owned and scaled the Data Platform for Alexa Shopping at Amazon. His background includes engineering leadership at Fannie Mae, where he operationalized ML models, and Freddie Mac, where he launched B2B service gateways. His expertise spans scaling high-impact systems in fintech, telecom, and transportation.

title: From Legacy to AI-Ready: Public Sector Data Modernization Panel
abstract: In the era of AI, modernizing data is now a mission-critical imperative. While moving off legacy systems is challenging, it is a necessity to improve efficiency, reduce costs and accelerate mission outcomes. In this session, public sector leaders share how they are building a cloud-native, organizational data strategy with a secure and governed foundation. Discover how these modern architectures enable better decision-making and cross-agency collaboration. We will move beyond the "why" to the "how," providing a roadmap to transform technical debt into a scalable runway for future generative AI and agentic initiatives.

speakers.bio:
Dr. Erica Reuter is the Director of US Public Sector Sales Engineering at Snowflake, leading teams that support federal, state, and local agencies. As a data scientist and former law enforcement leader with 20+ years of experience, she brings a mission-first approach to technology.

She holds a PhD in Criminal Justice and previously led crime analysis units, developing algorithms to reduce victimization. Beyond her corporate role, she uses her OSINT and digital forensics skills to volunteer with a national counter-trafficking task force. Dr. Reuter is a dedicated advocate for using ethical AI and advanced data solutions to drive societal good in the public sector.

title: From Legacy to Lift-Off: Fifth Third & TransUnion Reimagine Data for the AI Era
abstract: Imagine a world where your team isn’t held back by legacy systems or fragmented data. You operate with complete control, and clean, connected, AI-ready data flows instantly where needed. Models refresh and confidently improve. Decisions that took weeks now take minutes. Data teams no longer maintain the past — they build the future. The best part? It’s not a far-fetched dream. Hear how Fifth Third and TransUnion are reimagining data architectures, moving from legacy to cloud-powered foundations, and how Fifth Third leverages TransUnion’s TruIQ Data Enrichment to take more control over how critical data is accessed and linked and to power future-ready analytic and marketing processes.

speakers.bio: Michael leads the Analytics Consulting practice in TU’s Global Data & Analytics organization, which applies a wealth of analytics and custom modeling services to our data assets, collaborating with clients to solve their most critical business needs across credit risk, fraud, and marketing. His team of data scientists and analysts work across the enterprise to cultivate and commercialize capabilities based on emerging ML and AI methods. Michael holds a BA in Economics from the University of Chicago and an MS in Operations Research from the UC Berkeley.  He serves on Advisory Boards for Northwestern University’s ML and Data Science Program and the Board of VantageScore Solutions, LLC.

Jay is SVP, AI/ML Model Development and Ops at Fifth Third Bank, where he oversees model development for consumer credit and back-office operations and plays a key role in shaping the consumer data strategy, including modernizing analytics platforms, credit modeling and decisioning. Prior, Jay was CTO at Zest AI, where he led data science, engineering, and product teams that pioneered the use of AI in consumer loan underwriting, serving clients like Freddie Mac, Citibank, Truist, Discover, and + 100 regional banks and credit unions. He holds +40 patents in applied AI, earned a Ph.D. in Computer Science (CS) from Northwestern University, and has a B.S. in CS from The University of Chicago.

title: From Tables to Predictions: Zero-Shot ML Insights in Snowflake
abstract: Building predictive ML on enterprise data remains slow and complex, driven by heavy feature engineering, domain expertise and manual iteration. This session shows how Snowflake and Kumo enable teams to generate accurate predictions directly from Snowflake tables, without complex prompts or handcrafted features while maintaining strong explainability. Teams can run explainable ML directly where their data lives and operationalize predictions without leaving Snowflake.

speakers.bio: Doris Lee leads ML and data science experience across Snowflake's products. Previously, Doris was CEO and co-founder of Ponder (acquired by Snowflake in 2023), the company behind the open-source project Modin. Doris received her Ph.D. from the UC Berkeley RISE Lab and School of Information in 2021, where she developed open-source tools that help data scientists explore and understand their data. She received Forbes 30 under 30 for Enterprise Technology in 2023.

title: From Tankers to Tickers: Spot Market Stress Earlier with Connected Data
abstract: Geopolitical disruption does not stay contained. It ripples through trade routes, commodity flows, supplier networks and market expectations, often creating signals before the full impact is obvious. In this session, we show how connected S&P Global data on Snowflake, queryable with AI, helps teams trace those effects from the physical world to financial markets more quickly. See how connected shipping and cargo data can reveal stress building across critical trade routes, and how supplier momentum can help anticipate downstream pressure on companies and markets. We also demo how Snowflake enables efficient processing of large supply chain data sets at scale.

speakers.job_title: Global Head of New Product Development
speakers.bio: Liam Hynes is the Global Head of New Product Development at S&P Global Market Intelligence, leading strategic innovation and advancing research across S&P's data and analytics. He oversees the full solution life cycle, from proof-of-concept to commercialization. Previously, he was Head of ESG & Quant Specialists, EMEA, advising clients on quantitative modeling and  risk. Before S&P, he co-founded Monreith Capital, managing a Global Financials Hedge Fund, and held senior roles at Liontrust Asset Management and Occam Asset Management. Liam holds a PhD from Kemmy Business School, University of Limerick, a BSc (Hons) in Mathematics and a Machine Learning certification from Stanford.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: From Vision to Intelligence: How Cortex AI Accelerates FSFB’s AI Journey
abstract: Join Fundación Santa Fe de Bogotá (FSFB), a top-250 global hospital, to see how it is empowering clinicians with generative AI. This session explores FSFB's NEXUS engine, built on Snowflake, which interprets and structures complex clinical data. By leveraging Snowflake Cortex Search, FSFB has enabled doctors to "chat with the EHR" to get instant patient summaries in the ER and identify cancer suspicions. This early detection saves lives by allowing for more effective, less aggressive treatments with better outcomes. The result: Processing 5,000 clinical records now takes six hours, not 583. AI doesn't replace doctors; it empowers their ability to save lives.

speakers.bio: Systems Engineer, Master’s in Data Analytics, and Project Management Specialist. Big Data Scientist and currently Head of Data, Analytics & IA at Fundación Santa Fe de Bogotá.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Full Throttle from Day One
abstract: This fast-paced executive conversation takes different positions on how organizations should activate the Snowflake platform. Through structured debate across five platform pillars and the governance framework connecting them, the session surfaces the real decisions leaders face when building an AI-ready data platform. The discussion contrasts the urgency to deliver AI outcomes with the architectural discipline required to avoid long-term technical debt. The session provides a balanced perspective, equipping technical leaders with language to communicate business value and giving executives the architectural insight needed to make stronger platform decisions.

speakers.bio: Christopher Marcolis is Head of Presales for CDW's Data & Analytics Professional Services practice, where he leads a team of principal consultants and solution architects driving enterprise data platform and AI strategy across CDW's largest accounts. He and his CDW colleagues developed the MVDG framework — a practitioner model for governing the data layer that makes AI trustworthy — and have brought that thesis to the Gartner Data & Analytics Summit, Microsoft FabCon, the Databricks Data + AI Summit, and now Snowflake Summit. Christopher believes the enterprise AI race is won or lost before the first model is ever deployed, and he spends his career proving it!

I help organizations make data strategy actually work—and I help leaders and teams rethink what's possible. As a practice leader and advisor, I operate at the intersection of data strategy, architecture, and team development. I'm passionate about sharing what I've learned through speaking, writing, and partnering with data leaders and teams navigating complex data transformations.

title: Gallus Insights: AI-Powered Embedded Analytics for the Mortgage Industry
abstract: The most ambitious AI goals live or die by the quality of the underlying data. To truly harness AI, organizations must ensure they have a high-performance data foundation. See how Gallus Insights has built a robust, scalable architecture on Snowflake and seamlessly integrated ThoughtSpot Embedded to power customer experience. By leveraging cutting-edge agentic analytics, they deliver self-service insights to clients who can ask natural language questions and understand not just what's happening—but why. By combining Snowflake and ThoughtSpot, Gallus is experiencing 10x faster performance and reduced data costs and turning complex data into a competitive advantage.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Gaming at Scale: How NEXON Built an End-to-End AI Platform on Snowflake
abstract: NEXON operates 40+ live games serving over 1.9 billion users worldwide. To overcome analytics limits at unprecedented scale, we built the gaming industry’s first end-to-end AI automation platform on the AI Data Cloud. Our three-tier architecture empowers nontechnical teams with natural language analytics via Snowflake Intelligence, processes unstructured data through Cortex AI Functions for real-time game balancing, and integrates external data using Snowflake Openflow. Cortex Agents now handle 12% of the workforce, delivering 74% productivity gains, 60% faster decision-making and $2.3M in annual savings. This session shows how we scaled AI from experimentation to production across our portfolio.

speakers.bio: Jaemyoun Lee is a Staff Data Engineering Team Leader at Nexon Korea, leading the architecture and technical strategy for Monolake, Nexon’s unified data cloud platform built on Snowflake. With a Ph.D. background in distributed file systems, he specializes in building petabyte-scale data platforms that support analytics, AI workloads, and enterprise data governance. Earlier at Nexon, he led the standardization and governance of tens of thousands of game logs, accelerating data-driven decision-making across the organization. A Data Superhero since 2024, he actively contributes to the global data community and shares Nexon’s journey in building AI-ready data platforms for the gaming industry.

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: GEICO's Journey to Unified Enterprise Lakehouse Governance with Horizon, Polaris
abstract: GEICO, a leading insurer, struggled with data fragmentation across its multi-engine open-source lakehouse (Iceberg, Spark, Flink, Trino) and its EDW. Managing consistent security and governance across these disparate systems where Polaris served as the lakehouse catalog and Horizon governed the EDW created complexity. We'll showcase GEICO’s implementation of new Horizon features: full embedding of Polaris and support for IRC. This provides a single, universal governance layer, enforcing consistent FGAC, tagging and lineage across all data assets (OSS or Snowflake) achieving a true enterprise lakehouse, ensuring secure, multi-engine Iceberg access for AI-ready data discovery, without vendor lock-in.

speakers.bio: Kenneth Kang is a software engineer on GEICO's Data Lakehouse, built with open source components. He balances right-scaled big data tech with the business processes that work and evolve. Previously, he's worked on Oracle transactional sharding in C++ and go, https://github.com/ModEtchFill

title: Get ROI from Your Agents with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, MCP and Snowflake
abstract: AI agents are no longer a research experiment. They are running in production, closing support tickets, managing data pipelines and making decisions at enterprise scale. This session shows you how to architect, deploy and operate production-grade AI agents on AWS, from a single agent to complex multiagent systems. Using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, walk through real-world design patterns covering secure serverless deployment, tool and API integration via MCP, persistent memory, identity and access management, and end-to-end observability. See how AgentCore Gateway integrates with a Snowflake managed MCP server to orchestrate specialized subagents across your enterprise data ecosystem.

speakers.bio: Nidhi Gupta is a Sr. Partner Solution Architect at Amazon Web Services (AWS). She spends her day working with customers and partners, solving architectural challenges. She is passionate about data & ai integrations and orchestration, serverless and big data processing, generative ai and machine learning (ML). Nidhi has extensive experience leading the architecture design and production release and deployments for data workloads.

title: Getting Your Data Cortex AI-Ready with Fivetran, Snowflake and dbt
abstract: In 90 minutes, you'll go hands-on building a production-ready AI chatbot — taking raw data from ingestion all the way to an experience powered by Snowflake Intelligence using Fivetran and dbt Platform. Guided step by step, this lab is designed for all skill levels. Whether you're new to the modern data stack or a seasoned practitioner, you'll walk away with working knowledge of Snowflake Cortex AI and a repeatable blueprint you can take back to your organization.

speakers.bio: Luis Leon is a seasoned data and technology expert with a decade of experience in data analytics, cloud optimization, and sales engineering. He excels at bridging the gap between technical solutions and real-world problems. In his current role as a Partner Solution Architect at dbt Labs he supports technology and implementation partners across the ecosystem.

title: Golden Records: AI-Ready Data Products with Snowflake Native Apps
abstract: Master data management (MDM) is the missing link between Medallion lakehouses and reliable AI/BI outcomes. In this session, you’ll see how Semarchy runs as a Snowflake Native App to unify trusted golden records directly in the Snowflake AI Data Cloud — with no egress costs and no extra infrastructure. We’ll walk through an end-to-end certification design pattern to: Ingest and match, choose field survivorship, workflow-driven stewardship and publishing of governed data products. We’ll also cover hybrid patterns that support both analytical and transactional needs using Snowflake Hybrid Tables and Snowflake Postgres.

speakers.bio:
Your AI is only as smart as the data behind it.

If your data lake initiatives are stalling, if your data products are inconsistent, or if your MDM strategy is missing entirely - then this session was built for you.

As a Solutions Architect at Semarchy - a Gartner Magic Quadrant leader in Master Data Management - Benny bridges the gap between MDM strategy and modern AI-ready data products native to Snowflake. A seasoned conference speaker with recent appearances at Microsoft FabCon, he draws on 25 years of enterprise data experience to deliver real-world scenarios with zero fluff.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Governed Zero-Copy Data with Horizon Catalog and Iceberg Catalog Federation
abstract: Enterprises need analytics platforms that support multiple query engines, open table formats and governed data exchange across clouds without being tied to a single engine. Booking.com’s data team shares how they standardized on Snowflake Horizon Catalog as a central Iceberg catalog using Open APIs (Iceberg REST + Apache Polaris) to govern metadata and enable interoperability across Spark, Snowflake and federated compute.The session covers discovery and governed access using Iceberg catalog federation and catalog-linked databases, along with multi-engine governance patterns such as Immuta-backed fine-grained access control, enforcement limits and read-write trade-offs in multi-cloud environments.

speakers.bio: Regis Siqueira is a Software Engineering Manager at Booking.com, where he leads teams building data governance and access management platforms for large-scale data ecosystems. His work focuses on policy enforcement and secure data access across platforms such as Snowflake and AWS S3. He specializes in building scalable systems that enable secure, compliant data access in complex environments.

Mike Ogren is a Senior Software Engineer at Booking.com, who builds the foundational data governance platforms that power granular access at scale across the enterprise. He specializes in the engineering of automated policy enforcement across Snowflake and AWS S3, creating scalable systems that bridge the gap between complex data infrastructure and secure, compliant user access.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Hands-on Lab: Apache Spark on Snowflake
abstract: In this hands-on lab, you will learn how to develop, deploy, orchestrate and monitor PySpark applications on Snowflake. Discover how to execute heavy-lifting Apache Spark jobs directly where your data lives, eliminating siloed platforms and risky data movement. You will migrate existing code, automate pipelines and apply unified governance. Join us to see how our fully managed runtime simplifies your architecture, allowing you to focus on insights rather than infrastructure management.

speakers.bio: Jason drives key initiatives for the Snowpark and Spark developer platform, enhancing its capabilities and user experience. Feel free to ask him about anything pertaining to Snowpark, Python, UDFs, observability, DevOps, etc.

Brandon Carver solves problems. He is currently building scalable migration solutions for Snowflake while making it easier to navigate the Snowpark. He has experience creating meaningful change that scales across industries and data platforms in both the public and private sector.

title: Hands-on Lab: Autonomous SQL Pipelines for AI Agents
abstract: Modern data engineering is no longer just about moving data or building static data pipelines; it’s about making data AI ready at scale. In this hands-on workshop, we'll build an end-to-end data pipeline to power an AI agent in Snowflake. We'll use Snowflake Openflow to ingest REST API data and semi-structured documents into Snowflake. We'll define our pipeline objects declaratively, implement Dynamic Tables to quickly transform raw data into structured assets, and use Cortex Code along to way to generate new data models and objects. Finally, we'll build a Cortex Agents and use it with Snowflake Intelligence to extract insights from the data via a conversational interface.

speakers.bio:
With over a decade of experience as a technical developer educator, Gilberto has made complex concepts accessible to over 100,000 students and engineers through both online learning platforms and in-person experiences.


He's worn multiple hats as both product manager and developer educator at developer-loved tech companies, including MongoDB, Domino Data Lab, Plaid, and Snowflake.


Gilberto is passionate about crafting exceptional developer experiences and educational resources. He frequently writes about data engineering, AI, and application development.


Connect with him on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/gilberto-hernandez/

Ovi is a Solutions Architect dedicated to transforming how organizations leverage data to make smarter, more sustainable decisions. He specializes in designing and implementing robust data-driven applications and efficient data pipelines, with a strong focus on collaborating with customers to develop actionable data strategies that enable AI/ML-driven use cases. Outside of technology, he enjoys exploring the complexities of human psychology through reading and debating, maintains his resilience through endurance sports, and is committed to raising the next generation of children to value critical thinking and deep, focused work.

title: Hands-On Lab: Performance Tuning and Cost Optimization
abstract: In this interactive lab, participants will diagnose real-world performance bottlenecks and apply optimization techniques to reduce cost and improve query efficiency. Explore clustering strategies, warehouse right-sizing and query profile analysis in a guided, practical environment.

speakers.bio: Christine Avanessians is the Director of Product Management at Snowflake, where she leads the strategy for the core data warehouse/compute, FinOps, organization management, and billing platforms. A veteran of the cloud industry, Christine has spent her career scaling demanding compute environments. Before joining Snowflake, she managed the core infrastructure powering the Netflix experience and spent a decade at Microsoft, driving innovation across Azure’s compute and confidential computing portfolios, as well as Dynamics enterprise applications.

title: Honda’s Data Mesh Strategy: Apache Iceberg, Cortex AI and Horizon Catalog
abstract: As the mobility industry undergoes a once-in-a-century transformation, Honda drives rapid software-defined vehicle innovation by redefining its data platform. To match this pace, we established a foundational strategy that balances agility with governance. This session unveils our hybrid data architecture, seamlessly integrating centralized governance with a flexible Data Mesh. Learn how we harness Snowflake to build an AI-ready data mesh, utilizing innovations like Snowflake Horizon, Iceberg Tables and Cortex AI capabilities to create a vehicle platform that continuously evolves.

speakers.company: Honda R&D Co., Ltd.  /SDV R&D Center
speakers.job_title: Head of Digital Platform Strategy Dev
speakers.bio:
Tadafumi Nogawa is Head of the Digital Platform Strategy Development Division at Honda R&D’s SDV R&D Center, leading digital and cloud platforms for Honda’s Software-Defined Vehicle (SDV) transformation.

Since joining Honda in 2003, he has worked across internet services, backend systems, and early floating vehicle data initiatives.

He later led global telematics and connected vehicle platforms, launching worldwide server systems.

From 2015 to 2021, he was based in the U.S., leading HondaLink and AcuraLink in North America.

He currently drives SDV digital platform strategy, advancing cloud-based architectures and connected technologies.

Masaki Nakano is responsible for data platform strategy within the Digital Platform Strategy Development Division at Honda R&D’s SDV R&D Center, where he leads the planning and execution of data utilization initiatives in Honda’s Software‑Defined Vehicle (SDV) domain. After joining Honda as a mid-career hire in 2021, he has been engaged in vehicle data utilization initiatives. As a data platform strategy lead, he has worked on the introduction and adoption of Snowflake, as well as data governance initiatives.

title: How AI Accelerates Ideas to Results with Snowflake Notebooks
abstract: In this session, we showcase the latest AI capabilities in Snowflake Notebooks, which are integrated directly with Cortex Code. Learn how admins can leverage Cortex Code for unified notebooks governance and data teams can be 10x more productive in writing/reviewing code and delivering results and business impact.

speakers.bio:
Aileen Lu is a Senior Product Manager at Snowflake, where she leads developer frameworks and tools that streamline enterprise analytics, data engineering, data science, and machine learning workflows. She previously worked on Streamlit, one of the fastest-growing open-source Python frameworks for data scientists, with over 42,900 GitHub stars, more than 8 million total downloads, and a community of over 300,000 monthly active developers. 
Aileen is currently working on Snowflake Notebooks, a Snowflake-native development environment that gives data teams instant access to their data alongside optimized ML and AI frameworks, enabling faster experimentation and production workflows.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)
