title: How Amperity Helped the Seahawks Build a Unified Fan Identity in Three Minutes
abstract: The Seattle Seahawks couldn't identify their highest-value fans. Fan identity was limited to email addresses, ticket data was siloed to single-game purchases, and there was no way to connect across departments. By building a unified fan data ecosystem on Amperity, the Seahawks replaced fragmented profiles with rich fan views, including lifetime value scores, top-fan segmentation and passholder flags. Snowflake Bridge cut data sharing to three minutes with automatic daily updates. The outcome: 5,000 previously unidentifiable fans, now fully visible and reachable. Hear what this means for your own fan and customer data strategy.

speakers.bio: Jackie Jones loves Sports and how her company, Amperity, has used unified customer data to help Sports teams and fans of Sports alike. Amperity turns customer data into revenue with AI, personalize experiences in real time and monetize paid media audiences. Jackie has been in Tech for 15+ years at Salesforce, Slalom and now Amperity--so she knows this to be true.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: How a Universal Semantic Layer Provides Context for AI
abstract: As organizations deploy AI copilots and agents on enterprise data, a core challenge emerges: AI doesn’t understand business meaning. Metrics, hierarchies and policies are often scattered across BI tools, data platforms and application logic. Humans can spot inconsistencies —AI cannot. This session shows how a universal semantic layer on Snowflake provides the context AI needs. By defining business semantics once and routing AI queries through a semantic query engine, organizations can deliver governed, deterministic answers while extending existing BI semantics to power trusted AI experiences.

speakers.bio:
Dave is the founder and CTO of AtScale, where he leads innovation in semantic layer technology that powers AI-ready, governed analytics at scale. Dave is passionate about helping organizations unify their BI and AI workflows on a trusted data foundation.

At AtScale, Dave helped enterprises like Home Depot, Netflix, Fidelity Investments, and Cigna deliver trusted data and analytics to thousands of users by:

• Delivering a universal semantic layer for governed metrics and definitions

• Enabling natural language queries and agents for driving self-service insights and automated workflows

• Optimizing cloud costs by decoupling compute from consumption

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: How Canva Built a Petabyte-Scale Optimization Ecosystem
abstract: Petabyte-scale environments require granular visibility and disciplined governance. This session explores cost attribution models, storage lifecycle management and compute segmentation strategies that drive accountability without slowing innovation. This session is ideal for large enterprises managing complex, multi-domain workloads.

speakers.bio: Anna Bisset works in the Data Platform Operations team at Canva, where she focuses on observability, orchestration, and compute infrastructure. She has driven Snowflake cost attribution and optimisation work that brought over 80% of compute and storage spend under attributed ownership, giving engineering teams clear visibility into their data costs. She believes that making cost and performance data visible and actionable is the key to empowering engineering teams to make smarter decisions about their data pipelines at petabyte scale.

Haoyang Cui is a Data Engineer at Canva, where for the past three years he has played a key role in building and scaling Canva’s Data Platform.
He has led major initiatives in Snowflake storage and compute optimization to improve cost efficiency at scale, and spearheaded the rollout of a large-scale Data CI system—introducing robust testing frameworks that strengthen data quality and reliability across the organization.

title: How Canva Uses Event-Driven APIs and Scalable Pipelines with Snowflake Openflow
abstract:
Polling for data is inefficient, and manual resource management breaks at scale. This deep dive, co-presented wtih Canva, explores how Snowflake Openflow evolves into a programmable layer of the AI Data Cloud. We'll show how to design event-based flows with Snowflake Openflow Endpoints, a new capability that exposes listeners to handle inbound events.

Participants will learn how to design "push-based" architectures that react instantly, backed by the new Snowflake Openflow APIs. We'll demonstrate how to programmatically manage the lifecycle of Runtimes and Connectors using the new API. Join us to build a system that's not only event-driven but fully deployable via code.

speakers.bio: Inam Imthiyaz is a Senior Analytics Engineer at Canva, enabling data-driven marketing at scale. He builds robust data platforms and self-serve analytics solutions that transform complex data into trusted, insight-ready assets. His work centers on making reliable data accessible in near real-time, helping marketers respond faster, experiment smarter, and drive measurable growth.

Sam joined Snowflake as a Senior PM with the Datavolo acquisition, where he served as CTO and led Product and Field Engineering teams. Prior to that Sam was at Google Cloud and led Customer Engineering teams supporting ISVs on GCP in the areas of integration, data analytics, and ML. Sam has been close to data engineering and data architecture for his entire career and focuses on helping customers solve tough challenges to support continued innovation to inform better analytics, forecasting, and decision-making.

title: How Capital One Architected for Interoperability in a Multi-Catalog/Table Setup
abstract: As one of Snowflake's earliest and largest adopters, Capital One has continually driven innovation in cloud data management, focusing on optimizing cost and governance at a massive scale. This session details our strategic journey toward a Bring Your Own Compute model, illustrating how we evolved our architecture to meet rigorous security, governance and cost optimization requirements in a federated data environment. A core component of this evolution was implementing a sophisticated architecture that allows data consumers flexibility while maintaining central control via multi-catalog and multi-table format architecture, achieving data residency and compliance, and maximizing finops efficiency.

speakers.bio: Making trustworthy data available to drive business growth ,improve customer relationships & fuel innovation.

Temesgen (Teme) Meheret is a Director of Engineering at Capital One, leading the team responsible for the optimal usage and risk management of Snowflake.

Previously the VP of Engineering for Data & Intelligence at Amobee/Videology, Teme is a specialist in large-scale data efficiency. He is a vocal advocate for the lakehouse strategy, focusing on giving customers total control of their data and the flexibility to bring specialized compute engines directly to their data lake.

title: How CarGurus Freed 24K Engineering Hours to Allow Data Teams to Build Cool Sh*t
abstract: CarGurus runs a hub-and-spoke data organization — a central team with embedded analytics teams across the business. That model created friction: PR bottlenecks, inconsistent standards and inefficient backfills consuming engineering capacity. This session covers how CarGurus deployed the dbt Fusion engine on Snowflake to intelligently optimize builds, reclaim 24,000 annual engineering hours and maximize business value, achieving ROI in just five months. We also cover how that recovered capacity is fueling AI experimentation with Snowflake Cortex AI, Streamlit and the dbt semantic layer.

speakers.bio: Seasoned data leader with a focus on modern data and analytics and data science stacks.  Leading data teams to deliver data products that will drive business outcomes is something that I take a lot of pride in.

title: How Cyera Enables Semantic Data Layers for Analytics and AI with Secure Sharing
abstract: Modern SaaS products generate a flood of operational metadata that customers want to explore in BI — and now feed into AI. Instead of fragile CSV exports or one-off REST pulls, Snowflake Secure Data Sharing lets you publish governed, continuously updated data layers at scale for clients.

speakers.bio:
Shaun Chaudhary is Director of Analytics at Cyera, where he builds data products and AI agents that turn data into action for internal teams and customers. He created DataPort - Cyera's customer-facing data sharing product built on Snowflake - and leads a team focused on accelerating time to value through automation and self-service analytics.

Shaun holds a BA from Northwestern University and is based in New York City.

title: How DraftKings Ensures Regulatory Compliance at Scale Using Snowflake
abstract: DraftKings operates in one of the most heavily regulated digital industries, where data accuracy, lineage and timeliness are nonnegotiable. In this session, we’ll share how DraftKings designed its data architecture to meet the demands of operating across multiple highly regulated jurisdictions, with Snowflake as the core platform that enables scale and consistency. We’ll walk through how data is ingested, standardized and transformed, how compute is orchestrated, and how Snowflake’s native security, governance via Horizon Catalog, and observability features enable consistent, repeatable compliance across all our jurisdictions.

speakers.bio: Ronak Mistry is a Principal Data Engineer at DraftKings, where he leads efforts to modernize data warehouse design and improve performance, governance, and usability across large-scale data systems. His work centers on creating consistent, trustworthy data foundations while supporting newly emerging production grade AI use cases. He brings a practical perspective on what it takes to make data systems, and the teams behind them, work at scale.

title: How DTCC Created a Cultural Shift Around Proactive Visibility and Governance
abstract: DTCC, a leading financial market infrastructure company, depends on data to support securities clearing and settlement. Managing large cloud data sets can make spend visibility and forecasting difficult. This session explains how DTCC improved insight into Snowflake usage and costs, set proactive alerts for spikes, unified data into a single view, and fostered a culture of monitoring and optimization. We’ll share best practices and how DTCC improved unit economics across 87% of workloads.

speakers.bio: Edward Rips is Associate Director of Data Engineering at Depository Trust and Clearance Corporation (DTCC), where he leads the design and optimizationof entrprize-scale data platforms. He specializesin Snowflake and cloud-based architecturess, standard deployment patterns, with focus on performance , security and resiliency. At Snowflake Summit 2026, Edward will share how DTCC gained greater visibility into its spend and usage, created proactive alerts to stay abreast of cost spikes and anomalies, and colatedtheir data into single pane of truth for their Snowflake environment. He will showcase how DTCC fostered a more coznizant and proactive monitoring and optimization culture.
John Conrad is a Solutions Architect at Capital One. John has 28 years experience in software and technical sales and holds a Computer Science degree from the University of Kentucky.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: How DTCC Enabled AI-Driven Data Cataloguing at Scale with Cortex and Streamlit
abstract: Scaling intelligent metadata tagging across a modern data estate is one of the most persistent challenges in enterprise data management. This session demonstrates how the Depository Trust and Clearing Corporation (DTCC) used Snowflake Cortex AI and Streamlit to automate the creation, approval and management of business definitions from structured and unstructured data. Learn how DTCC eliminated manual cataloguing bottlenecks with scalable architecture, intuitive workflows and an AI-powered hub that drives metadata consistency and visibility across the enterprise — marking their first gen AI application in production and driving $1.2 million in cost avoidance.

speakers.bio: Rohit Sharma is a Director of Enterprise Data who turns complex data estates into simple, AI‑powered outcomes. He leads enterprise AI on cloud platforms like Snowflake—moving from pilots to production with speed, governance, and measurable ROI. A research‑minded innovator, Rohit focuses on generative AI to streamline processes, improve data trust, and accelerate decisions—without sacrificing security or compliance. His north star: make AI practical, responsible, and effortless for the business at scale while embedding intelligence directly into everyday workflows that drive clarity, efficiency, and sustainable transformation.

Sukanya is an AI/ML Solutions Architect at Snowflake, where she empowers organizations to navigate the complexities of the modern data landscape. Her background includes a Master’s in Data Science from the University of Pennsylvania and diverse industry experiences from data-centric roles at H2O.ai, Coursera, and Mercer. She is also a published data science researcher with her research work being in the intersection of social media, healthcare, and psychology. In her free time, Sukanya can be found exploring national parks, scouting cafes in the city for the perfect cup of coffee, or enjoying life with her mini goldendoodle.

title: How Eutelsat Uses Data and AI to Manage Global Connectivity
abstract: Running a satellite network isn’t like running a terrestrial one: coverage is global, demand is volatile and orbital resources are finite. Eutelsat uses data and AI to build a unified intelligence layer that orchestrates satellite traffic across continents and ground stations. Forecasting anticipates spikes, optimizes capacity and latency, and uses analytics to strengthen resilience and sustainability — turning satellites into adaptive infrastructure for aviation, maritime, enterprise and government. This session shows how to manage at planetary scale, ensure reliability and convert insight into services and revenue.

speakers.bio:
Global Head of Data & AI at Eutelsat , the world's first satellite operator with an integrated GEO-LEO infrastructure.

Working in the Data & Analytics field for more than 15 years, heading business intelligence, data science and data platforms teams and has worked with traditional dbms, big data platforms and on multiple business intelligence tools, following all the evolutions of the technology over the last years.

Pedro Jose – Telecom Field CTO, EMEA at Snowflake

Pedro Jose is the Telecom Field CTO for EMEA at Snowflake, where he helps organizations harness the power of data, AI, and cloud to drive innovation and business transformation. 

With over 20 years of experience leading data-centric initiatives, Pedro is passionate about making complex technology accessible, scalable, and impactful. 

He advises enterprises on building modern data platforms, operationalizing AI, and creating value through data collaboration.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: How Fanatics Manages dbt Metrics with Cortex Code for Natural Language Analytics
abstract: In this session, Christine Kwon, Senior Director of Data at Fanatics, and Brett Pendleton, Manager of Analytics Engineering at Fanatics, share how they used dbt to define and govern Fanatics’ metric source of truth using scalable, metadata-driven models (YAML-based definitions, testing and documentation). They will walk through how those curated models power Snowflake semantic views and Snowflake Intelligence, enabling business users to ask trusted, governed questions in natural language.

speakers.bio:
Brett Pendleton is an Analytics Engineering Manager at Fanatics Betting & Gaming, where he leads the development of scalable data models that support the entire organization. He focuses on building reliable, efficient data pipelines and transforming raw data into actionable insights for stakeholders across the organization.
At Fanatics, Brett plays a key role in bridging data engineering and business analytics, helping teams leverage modern data tooling and frameworks to deliver impactful results. He also drives the evolution of analytics by turning high-quality data models into AI-powered solutions that enable more advanced insights, automation, and decision-making.

Christine Kwon is the Sr. Director of Data at Fanatics Betting & Gaming, where she leads data strategy and foundational functions including data integration, governance, analytics engineering, and reporting. She has extensive experience driving scalable, compliant, and reliable data solutions that enhance operational efficiency and decision-making. Christine is passionate about building strong data platforms that support long-term business growth and empower teams with modern technologies. Prior to Fanatics, she held data leadership roles in enterprise organizations and brings deep expertise in fostering data-driven cultures.

title: How GitLab Saved 90% in Engineering Costs by Keeping AI Inside Snowflake
abstract: GitLab had more than 75,000 sensitive Zendesk tickets full of valuable feedback that were inaccessible due to governance constraints, and manual review wasn’t scalable. An external AI pipeline seemed viable until we uncovered its real cost: complex setup, ongoing maintenance and over $73,000 a year in operational burden. Instead, we chose the simpler path: Snowflake Cortex AI. By keeping all processing inside Snowflake’s governance boundary, we eliminated infrastructure overhead and processed all 75,000+ tickets for about $1,500 without any data leaving our account.

speakers.bio: Principal Data Engineer at Gitlab

Radovan Bacovic is a Staff Data Engineer at GitLab who lives, enjoys, and codes in Novi Sad, Serbia.

Part of the Snowflake Squad and dbt spotlight members.
An experienced data engineer and “wanna-be” the best bad conference speaker. He is always eager to discover new data technologies in a rapidly changing environment. He has a profound application development background in large international companies around the globe. 
Strongly advocates for the open-source community and open-core approach. 

Undoubtedly, he is a fervent data geek who is delighted to share his experience with a broader audience. 

Trapped in the Data world for 20 years. 
Passionate Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu practitioner.

title: How LendingTree Marketers Built a Snowflake Agent for Smarter SEM Decisions
abstract: Marketing teams waste hours digging through campaign data to find actionable insights. See how LendingTree transformed its search engine marketing with an AI agent that instantly surfaces performance trends, identifies optimization opportunities and recommends high-impact actions — cutting analysis time dramatically. A LendingTree marketer will demonstrate how the team built an intelligent assistant connecting Google Ads performance with customer data, enabling faster, data-driven decisions without waiting on analysts. Learn how this AI-powered approach is driving measurable campaign ROI improvements and freeing marketers to focus on strategy rather than spreadsheets.

speakers.bio: Thomas Ralls is Senior Director of Marketing Analytics & Operations at LendingTree, where he leads the team behind some of the company’s most impactful data and marketing initiatives. With over a decade of experience at companies like Google and Rakuten Advertising — and a Master’s in Applied Data Science from the University of Chicago — Thomas brings a rare blend of technical depth and marketing instinct to every problem he tackles. Today, he’s focused on understanding what marketers actually need, shaping data strategy at the organizational level, and finding the places where AI can make a real business impact.

title: How Norwegian Cruise Line Optimizes Fuel, Compliance and Costs in Snowflake ML
abstract: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) built a first-of-its-kind, innovative Snowflake native fuel forecasting platform using Snowflake ML, unlocking millions of dollars in annual savings and strengthening regulatory compliance and procurement strategy. By unifying massive volumes of Internet of Things, routing and climatology data in Snowflake, NCLH trains and deploys ML models that predict fuel use for every ship, leg and port call with minute-by-minute granularity. The solution enables accurate procurement planning, reduced carbon credit exposure and near real-time insights — all powered end to end by Snowflake ML.

speakers.bio: Dynamic business leader known for using business intelligence to drive business value and cultural change with over 15 years in the Cruise Industry's complex analytics space

With a Ph.D. in ship power plant operations and more than ten years at the intersection of maritime engineering and data science, Artem has built a career around one question: how do you make ships cleaner at scale? He has deployed machine learning solutions across global fleets, achieving significant annual fuel reductions, and actively contributes to the industry's push toward IMO 2030 and EPA Clean Ports goals. He is the author of Marine Hull Fouling, written for the growing community of maritime decarbonization practitioners.

title: How Sanofi Scales AI Across 80 Countries with Dataiku and Snowflake
abstract: What does it take to scale AI across 80 countries in a highly regulated industry? Join Jacob Kuijpers, Head of Data Operations at Sanofi, and Conor Jensen, Field CDO at Dataiku, for a candid conversation on turning AI ambition into enterprisewide execution. Discover how Sanofi uses Snowflake as its global data foundation and Dataiku to connect business and IT, operationalize governance and accelerate the shift from pilots to production. Learn practical lessons on data quality, platform strategy and operating model decisions for scaling AI in a complex, regulated organization.

speakers.bio: Jacob Kuijpers serves as the Global Head of Commercial Data, where he leads strategic data initiatives to transform Sanofi's commercial operations worldwide. He has shaped Sanofi's data mesh approach, established federated governance across the enterprise, and spearheaded the Sanofi AI Foundry platform for data, AI/ML, and GenAI workloads. Based in the Toronto AI Hub, Jacob is part of the leadership team guiding 150+ Sanofians dedicated to advancing AI innovation.

Conor Jensen is a Technology, AI, and Strategy executive currently leading the Field CDO team at Dataiku. His path to here meandered along a circuitous route through the worlds of barista, archery and climbing instructor, mathematics student, Air Force weather forecaster, insurance actuary, and strategy consultant. When not working with customers, you can find him at home on the outskirts of Chicago, trying to read a book or spending time with his wife, their seven children, a small flock of ducks, and one very fancy goose.

title: How SPS Commerce Scaled a Multimillion-Dollar Analytics Business with Sigma
abstract: SPS Commerce is reinventing its embedded analytics platform, SPS Analytics, by replacing legacy BI with Sigma on Snowflake. The platform helps retailers and partners unify sell-through, inventory and supplier data to improve forecasting, optimize inventory and drive stronger outcomes across the retail ecosystem. In this session, Troy Prothero, Senior Director of Product Management at SPS Commerce, shares why SPS replatformed its analytics platform and how Sigma enables collaborative workflows with writeback capabilities that allow users to refine operational data in Snowflake. Learn how this foundation is preparing SPS Commerce for the next generation of AI-powered applications.

speakers.bio: Troy Prothero is a senior product and analytics leading for SPS Commerce’s Analytics business, where he is driving the migration to a Snowflake + Sigma platform and building next-generation retailer–supplier analytics. With 20+ years in retail, CPG, and supply chain technology, Troy has led products spanning demand planning, inventory optimization, and data analytics for global brands and retailers. He currently focuses on turning granular retailer and brand data into actionable insights, AI-assisted workflows, and measurable financial outcomes for customers. He partners with engineering and GTM teams to scale modern data stacks and share lessons from Snowflake-native analytics programs.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: How to Build and Share AI-Powered Multimodal Data Products
abstract: Organizations are increasingly looking for cohesive AI-powered data products that combine structured, semi-structured and unstructured data. This session details how to build, package and securely share AI-powered multimodal data products. We define "interoperable AI-powered multimodal data products" and discuss architectural considerations for reliable product evolution with live demonstrations. Learn how to leverage catalog-linked databases with open table formats, multimodality features and AI layering (semantic views, Snowflake Cortex Search, managed MCP server and so forth) alongside industry-leading AI and data collaboration capabilities to deliver comprehensive data products at scale.

speakers.bio: Jeevan Rag, a Principal Industry Architect in Snowflake's Industry CTO group, specializes in leveraging Snowflake's capabilities to address the unique business needs of the Healthcare Industry. With nearly two decades of Industry Experience, he led digital transformation engagements, strategizing and implementing enterprise-wide data and AI platforms for healthcare clients. He is adept at fostering business engagement, sponsorship, and adoption of data-driven decision-making. In his personal time, Jeevan enjoys traveling with his family, reading, and movies.

Jwala has 3 decades of experience in architecting, designing and delivering data platform modernization projects. 
Experienced in collaborating with cross-functional global teams. He currently advocates snowflake customers on data collaboration best practices, strategic planning and execution.

He has proven track record in modernizing end-to-end data technology stack from Ingestion, transformation, modeling to enabling analytics and AI/ML workloads. Expertise in team building, technology evaluation, vendor management, change management, pre-sales and customer solutioning.

title: How to Evaluate Snowflake Gen2 Safely in Production and the Outcomes at Scale
abstract: Upgrading core infrastructure requires rigor. This session outlines a controlled framework for evaluating Snowflake Gen2 in live production environments — covering A/B validation, workload benchmarking and risk mitigation. Hear measurable outcomes and lessons learned from enterprise-scale deployment.

speakers.bio:
Muthupalaniappan Ramanathan is a Senior Software Engineer with 13+ years of experience building large-scale data platforms. He specializes in lakehouse architectures, data reliability, and cost-efficient systems, with a strong focus on cost governance and optimization. His work includes designing cross-engine SQL translation and anomaly detection platforms to improve interoperability at scale.

He actively contributes to the data engineering community through publications, judging, and technical leadership.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: How to Give Snowflake Intelligence the Customer Context It's Missing
abstract: Snowflake Intelligence is transforming how teams interact with data. But insight quality depends on the data underneath. Snowplow's customer context layer delivers validated, enriched behavioral data into Snowflake with prebuilt semantic models that accelerate Snowflake Cortex Analyst, letting anyone self-serve insights without the data team. Crucially, Snowplow captures every digital interaction, with a human and AI agent alike, so teams can distinguish and optimize for both within the same Snowflake Cortex AI queries. The same context layer powering internal analytics also fuels customer-facing AI agents, creating a compounding intelligence loop where every interaction sharpens every insight.

speakers.bio: Yali Sassoon is Co-founder and Chief Technology Officer at Snowplow Analytics. Yali is principally focused on empowering data teams to meet the challenges they face around data delivery, meaning, quality and governance, with a particular focus on AI applications and use cases.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: How TripAdvisor Unlocked Its Data to Make Every Traveler Better
abstract: TripAdvisor set out to make every traveler's experience more personal — but their legacy CDP was holding them back. In this session, learn how TripAdvisor migrated to a composable CDP on Snowflake, centralizing complete customer and event data while giving marketers true self-serve access. Hear the challenges they faced along the way, and the results that followed: AI-powered real-time decisions, lower acquisition costs, a thriving retail media network, and richer, more personalized experiences that make every traveler better.

speakers.job_title: Sr. Director Data & AI Strategy and Ops
speakers.bio: Data executive with a passion for data engineering, data product, analytics, and data science/ML.  Successfully created business value from data at Fortune 500 companies, start-ups, and in-between. I've built and managed big data infrastructure and data products that enable data-driven decision making, optimization, and personalization for business partners. I've designed and launched Data Strategy and Digital Transformation initiatives, and guided companies through cloud migrations. At Tripadvisor I lead our Data & AI Strategy and Operations team, which includes our AI partnerships.

title: How Truist Is Building the Data Foundation That Makes Cortex AI Possible
abstract: In a regulated bank, AI is only as reliable as the data feeding it. Truist is building that foundation on Snowflake with Ataccama, starting with FDIC regulatory reporting, and embedding a data trust layer into the Snowflake medallion architecture. Now they're expanding further, migrating more of their data estate into Snowflake, rolling the architecture across additional regulatory programs, and building toward the point where Cortex AI runs on data they already trust. Their team shares what they built, where they're taking it and what any regulated institution needs to prioritize to get there.

title: How We Made Snowflake 10x Faster for Real-Time Interactive Analytics
abstract: This session will cover Snowflake Interactive, including how we made it to be 10x higher in concurrency and 15x lower on ingestion latency. We'll provide ideal business use cases, a technical overview, a deep dive and a demo.

speakers.bio: Will is a Principal product manager currently building Snowflake Interactive. He focuses on building tools for developers in the real-time analytics space. Previously, he was the product manager for several open-source databases, including Apache Druid, Apache Hive, Apache HBase, and Apache Phoenix. He also ran data products at Meta, Microsoft, and was the first PM of Datadog.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Identifying Chronic Conditions Using Snowflake Cortex AI
abstract: Due to the high risk of their patient population and the strain on primary care physicians, chronic illnesses often go undiagnosed and untreated. Agilon's goal is to leverage LLMs and its massive patient data set to help uncover and diagnose such conditions, providing its patients a happier, healthier life. Agilon is utilizing its centralized clinical data with Snowflake Cortex AI to reason through millions of patient documents. This enables Agilon to instantly and accurately flag potential undiagnosed ICD-10 codes at scale for its 600,000-patient population. The financial uplift: Accurately capturing these missing codes is expected to drive roughly $100 million annually of incremental revenue.

speakers.bio: Alex leads AI work at agilon health, and is focused on using generative AI and ML to drive outcomes in value based care. Prior to agilon, Alex led the Data Science team at n1 Health, and was the first data hire at Wellframe (acq. by HealthEdge). Alex lives in Boston with his partner and 2-year old daughter, and spends most of his free time coaching and play competitive ultimate frisbee.

Seasoned Data Platform Architect with a proven track record in Data Science, Generative AI, Developer Relations, and Partner Alliances. With expertise across Retail and CPG, Insurance, Consulting, and FinTech industries, I specialize in bridging the gap between cutting-edge technology and impactful business solutions.

As Principal AI Architect at Snowflake, I lead the design and deployment of advanced AI and ML solutions, enabling clients to leverage their data for transformational outcomes. My role also includes fostering strong relationships with partners and managing alliances to drive innovation and business growth.

title: Implement Sensitive Data Governance in Snowflake Using AI
abstract: Safeguarding sensitive data at scale is nonnegotiable for regulatory compliance. Join this essential hands-on lab to master the art of automated sensitive data discovery and protection. You will leverage the power of AI to instantly generate robust data protection policies, learn to troubleshoot policy issues and establish an airtight audit trail for access to sensitive data. Achieve compliance confidence with advanced data governance.

speakers.bio: Ankit Gupta works as a Product Manager on the Data Governance team. Prior to joining Snowflake, Ankit held a product management role at Microsoft, where he worked on various products ranging from Azure, IoT, and Data Governance & Compliance.

title: Indeed’s Playbook: Auto-Scaling APIs, Lower TCO and Fast Queries on Snowflake
abstract: Employer analytics is hard, with low-latency, high-concurrency customer APIs plus compute-heavy batch refreshes of aggregates — without an infrastructure empire. This talk shares how Indeed's Employer Analytics group moved from self-managed Vertica to Snowflake Virtual Warehouses for all data access. Warehouses let our APIs auto-scale with traffic, delete years of ops scaffolding, free engineers for product work and cut TCO with lower compute costs (projected ~$2M/year savings). We'll also cover Interactive tables and Interactive warehouses, improving p95 latency ~30% and laying a foundation for next-gen agentic analytics.

speakers.bio:
Mike Cohen is a Technical Fellow at Indeed, leading the Marketplace Data and Analytics group. He specializes in building high scale distributed systems and streaming data systems, with a focus on building platforms that are both operationally simple and cost-effective.

He recently led a migration from self-managed Vertica to Snowflake Virtual Warehouses. The transformation delivered approximately $2M in annual savings, reduction in query latency, and eliminated years of operational complexity. His work demonstrates how bringing software engineering discipline to data platform challenges can unlock both immediate performance gains and durable architectural advantages.

Sainath Rachaputi is a Principal Analytics Solutions Lead in Indeed’s Marketplace Engineering organization, focusing on data engineering for the Architecture & Data Products Technology(ADaPT) teams, designing and scaling data platforms and pipelines that power trusted analytics for Indeed Marketplace. Sainath partners with engineers, analysts, and product leaders to turn complex event and platform data into reliable, reusable building blocks for decision-making and low-latency analytics at scale.

title: Industry Use Cases: Data Modeling for Enterprise Scale and AI Readiness
abstract: Join us for a C-level discussion on how Snowflake unifies product data, enables real-time analytics and drives measurable business impact. Attendees will learn how centralized product intelligence can transform decision-making at the executive level, supporting faster go-to-market strategies, operational efficiency and cross-functional collaboration.

speakers.bio: Serge Gershkovich has nearly two decades of experience in data architecture and business intelligence. He is currently Head of Product at SqlDBM, a Snowflake Data Superhero, and author of the bestselling Data Modeling with Snowflake.

title: Inside Snowflake: How to Monitor, Alert and Stay Ahead
abstract: Have you ever wondered what’s really happening inside Snowflake and the workloads running on it? How do you move beyond treating the platform as a black box, proactively monitor what you run and stay informed as things change? This talk explores how Snowflake monitoring addresses these questions, including AI-driven alerting workflows that enable no-code alerts and notifications. Built on next-generation event tables with improved telemetry management and access, these capabilities help organizations monitor the platform more effectively, with a customer example showing how they are applied in practice.

speakers.bio: Vaishnavi is a Staff Product Manager at Snowflake, where she leads the Observability Framework, including Snowflake Trail. With a strong background in product management and engineering, she has previously worked at AWS and Facebook, focusing primarily on compute technologies. She holds a Master’s degree in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon University and has a keen interest in Distributed Systems.

title: Interoperability without Complexity: Snowflake Storage for Iceberg Tables
abstract: Running Apache Iceberg at scale often means managing buckets, IAM policies and background jobs. Interoperable storage removes that burden. In this session, learn how Snowflake brings native table simplicity to open Iceberg data. Snowflake handles compaction, metadata cleanup, security and lifecycle management. Tables remain fully interoperable and accessible to external engines through IRC APIs in Horizon. See how teams can migrate Iceberg workloads, support multi-engine access, and reduce operational complexity and total cost compared to customer-managed storage alternatives.

speakers.bio: Kaushal leads Product for Storage at Snowflake, driving innovations across structured, unstructured, and lakehouse data. Earlier, he led cost and performance optimization efforts at Snowflake and previously shaped key products in Microsoft’s Azure and Ads divisions. Kaushal earned his Bachelor’s and Master’s degrees from IIT Bombay and MBA from Harvard Business School.

times.room: Builders Hub Theater (Basecamp, South Hall D)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Interoperable Lakehouse 101: Single Governed Data Copy for AI Readiness
abstract: Stop the data silo and fragmentation tax. In this introductory hands-on lab, you will learn how to work on a single governed data copy that slashes costs and engineering effort. You will create Apache Iceberg tables in Snowflake Storage, apply Snowflake Horizon governance and secure multi-engine, and build Semantic Views to use AI to query your lakehouse with natural language. Achieve true data autonomy over your data and make your architecture AI-ready.

speakers.bio: I have been working in technology sector for about 18 years . With a deep understanding of data engineering, software development, and cloud solutions,I have successfully led and contributed to numerous projects that span various industries, including finance, healthcare, and e-commerce.

Sahil Walia is a Solutions Architect at Snowflake specializing in data engineering and modern data platforms. He advises enterprise customers on data architecture and AI initiatives, helping organizations design scalable, trusted, and interoperable foundations for business-critical workloads. His work spans modern data engineering, platform strategy, and architectural decisions that support long-term data and AI adoption.

title: Intuitive Surgical’s AI for BI: Moving from Dashboards to Agentic Analytics
abstract: Intuitive Surgical leads the minimally invasive care sector with innovative robotic devices and an emphasis on data-driven insights. To advance this aim, it is launching AI for Numbers — a cutting-edge project that merges generative AI and natural-language queries with business intelligence tools. By leveraging Snowflake and ThoughtSpot, Intuitive is transitioning from static dashboards to dynamic, agent-based analytics, allowing teams to pose complex questions and get immediate, actionable answers at scale. This session outlines the technical infrastructure, strategic benefits and lessons learned from this initiative.

speakers.bio: Strategic Data Leader with more than 20 years of experience in developing enterprise data products and AI-driven BI solutions. Skilled in designing technical systems across various sectors including Medical Devices, Pharmaceuticals, and Banking. Expertise lies in implementing business intelligence solutions and guiding international teams to connect complex data infrastructures with advanced analytics and AI.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Itron Powerflow for Grid Planning on Snowflake ML
abstract: Itron is redefining grid management by delivering structure-level Powerflow analysis for the world’s largest energy utilities. Electrification is fundamentally reshaping demand. Learn how Itron is using Snowflake ML to generate granular, high-frequency insight across both load and generation to maintain reliability, manage congestion and control emissions.

speakers.bio: Applied ML engineer focusing on distributed computing for AI and machine learning. Especially interested in performance and infrastructure challenges that arise when scaling models and complex workloads.

Business Growth Manager at Itron, working with electric utilities across North America

Focused on load forecasting and distribution grid planning

Partners with utility leaders and planning teams on electrification, DER growth, and localized load impacts

Helps translate planning assumptions into clear, defensible investment decisions

Holds an electrical engineering degree from NC State University and an MBA in Business Development

Based out of the Nashville, TN area with his family.

title: Lakehouse Transformations: Build Production Pipelines for Your Iceberg Tables
abstract: Stop pipeline sprawl and the cost of data duplication. In this advanced lab, you will learn to perform secure, in-place transformations across your entire data estate. You will connect externally managed Iceberg tables with Catalog Linked Databases to always work on fresh data without ETL, build efficient and declarative pipelines with Dynamic Tables for Iceberg preserving multi-engine access to your data, and implement business continuity to ensure your production data is always available.

speakers.bio: Kamesh Sampath is a Lead Developer Advocate at Snowflake, author, and long-time open source contributor with over two decades of experience in enterprise software. He’s passionate about helping developers and data practitioners harness the power of cloud, data, and AI to build real-world innovation. Through advocacy, community, and storytelling, Kamesh bridges the gap between complex technology and human creativity, inspiring developers to learn more, do more, and share more.

With over a decade of experience as a technical developer educator, Gilberto has made complex concepts accessible to over 100,000 students and engineers through both online learning platforms and in-person experiences.


He's worn multiple hats as both product manager and developer educator at developer-loved tech companies, including MongoDB, Domino Data Lab, Plaid, and Snowflake.


Gilberto is passionate about crafting exceptional developer experiences and educational resources. He frequently writes about data engineering, AI, and application development.


Connect with him on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/gilberto-hernandez/

title: Leaders from Affirm, Booking.com, DoorDash and Indeed Discuss Apache Iceberg
abstract: Curious about Apache Iceberg? Want to learn from data leaders from the world's biggest data companies? Join us for a fireside chat rich with practical experiences, frameworks and key lesssons as industry leaders from Netflix, Booking.com, Doordash and Indeed discuss how  they are modernizing their architectures with Iceberg. Learn about their vision, investment areas in the next years, and how Iceberg is unlocking value faster while reducing costs.

speakers.bio: Dimitar Nedev is a Principal Data Engineer with Booking.com’s AI & Analytics Platform. He works on cloud data infrastructure and the shared systems that support large-scale data processing, machine learning, and Conversational BI. Over more than a decade, his work has spanned e-commerce, logistics, and cloud infrastructure, with a focus on scalable pipelines, data modeling, governance, and Lakehouse systems built on open table formats such as Apache Iceberg.

Ankit Singh combines deep experience in data processing with a contextual awareness of the BNPL industry to help drive technical decision making at Affirm. A part of Affirm's Data Storage & Services (DSS) technical leadership team. Technical background in OLAP and OLTP storage infrastructure platforms and Data Engineering services. Focused on building and optimizing the critical data systems that power Affirm's financial products. Data Storage & Services (DSS) manages all data related operational and analytical infrastructure needs at Affirm. Storage systems including OLTP and OLAP, batch processing capabilities, and enterprise data governance.
Worked on exabytes and small startup projects  with constant emphasis on advanced, emerging technologies with high ROI

David leads the Data Lake and Snowflake Engineering teams at Indeed, the world's #1 job site. His team successfully migrated Indeed's analytics data lake to the cloud and is now converting its 75 PB of data to Iceberg format — positioning Indeed's lakehouse to interoperate with whatever comes next in the rapidly evolving world of AI and data analytics.

title: Leveraging AI-Powered Analytics to Manage the Guest Experience with Fogo de Chão
abstract: Fogo de Chão is an internationally renowned restaurant from Brazil that elevates the culinary art of churrasco into a cultural dining experience. Fogo offers a continuous selection of high-quality proteins carved tableside and access to a market table with a selection of seasonal salads, exotic fruit, superfoods and more. Managing guest satisfaction and operational excellence at scale requires more than intuition — it demands data. Fogo de Chão's partnership with Domo is a cornerstone of their data strategy, enabling real-time insights. Discover how Fogo de Chão leverages AI-powered analytics to manage reputation and drive operational efficiency to make smarter decisions every day.

speakers.bio:
Sashi Kommineni is the Vice President of Data & Analytics at Fogo de Chão, where he is leading the modernization of the company’s data foundation to enable AI readiness and scalable, data-driven decision-making. He oversees omni-channel analytics, customer data platform, and personalization strategies to enhance guest experience and business performance.

Prior to Fogo de Chão, Sashi held leadership roles at Chipotle, where he led Customer and Digital Insights & Analytics, driving impactful data strategies across digital and customer domains. Earlier, he was Director of Data Science at L Brands, building advanced analytics and data science capabilities for specialty retail.

Mohammed Aaser is Domo’s Chief Strategy Officer (CSO), where he helps customers create innovative data solutions and modern data experiences that accelerate key business processes and optimize strategic outcomes. Prior to Domo, Mohammed was CDO at McKinsey and Company, where he led the firm's data innovation capabilities, accelerating development of data apps by combining deep industry knowledge with cutting-edge data sets, data management technology and data engineering. Mohammed also held leadership roles at Ameriprise Financial, where he led the organization’s marketing strategy and analytics transformation.

title: Making Mainframe Data AI Ready: Hydrating Snowflake Data Lakes
abstract:
Many financial service enterprises still rely on mainframe systems to power critical financial and operational workloads. But activating that data for modern analytics and AI readiness often requires complex pipelines, replication or disruptive modernization.

In this session, we walk through how we’re helping enterprises bridge the mainframe and the Snowflake AI Data Cloud using IBM DataGate and modern data engineering patterns. Learn how organizations can securely hydrate Snowflake data lakes with trusted mainframe data, creating a foundation that enables cross-platform analytics and AI while preserving the stability of mission-critical systems.

speakers.bio: Anand Pandya is a technology and data leader at Hakkoda, part of IBM, focused on helping financial services organizations turn data, analytics, and AI into measurable business outcomes. With more than 20 years of experience in financial services technology, Anand works closely with CDOs and CTOs to modernize data platforms, break down data silos, and accelerate AI adoption. His expertise spans cloud transformation, enterprise data strategy, and building scalable architectures. A recognized thought leader in data and AI transformation, Anand focuses on practical strategies that help banks, insurers, and asset managers move from legacy systems to modern, outcome-driven data ecosystems.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Making Medicine Computable: Scaling Multimodal Foundation Models on Snowflake
abstract: We introduce APOLLO, a multimodal clinical foundation model trained on 25 billion longitudinal records from Mass General Brigham for advanced similarity search and outcome prediction. Aevius will showcase a Snowflake Native App that generates APOLLO embeddings within a client’s secure environment — enabling hospitalwide search without exposing PHI. This deployment provides a roadmap for enterprise intelligence, using agentic workflows and reasoning to automate complex analytical tasks.

speakers.bio: Rowland is a physician-scientist, pathologist, and co-founder and CEO of Aevius Labs. Aevius is building large-scale multimodal foundation models for clinical data to bring frontier reasoning to medicine. Rowland trained in clinical pathology at Mass General Brigham and Harvard after completing his MD, PhD, and MBA. He was previously on the founding team at Modella AI, acquired by AstraZeneca.

speakers.job_title: MGH Vice Chair for Pathology Informatics
speakers.bio: Long Phi Le a molecular and computational pathologist with over 15 years of experience in clinical genomics, digital pathology, pathology laboratory operations, and clinical informatics. Long leads a software development and data science team dedicated to creating clinical, research, education, and artiticial intelligence applications at Mass General Brigham.

Murali Gandhirajan is Global Regulated Industries CTO at Snowflake, leading data, analytics, and AI strategy for healthcare, life sciences, public sector, and manufacturing. He combines deep expertise in modern data platforms and applied AI to deliver measurable outcomes across regulated industries. A recognized thought leader, Murali works with executives and technical teams to build secure, next-generation data and AI platforms that power data sharing, mission insights, and responsible AI at scale.

title: Manage Your Snowflake Cost Effectively with Snowflake Cost Governance
abstract: Interested in learning how you can track and analyze your Snowflake spend and control your overall spend? If you are, this session is for you. Come learn about Snowflake’s new cost governance capabilities and how customers are leveraging them to manage Snowflake costs effectively.

title: Maximizing Performance and Cost Efficiency with Virtual Warehouses
abstract: Warehouse configuration directly impacts cost, performance and user experience. This session explores workload-aware sizing, auto-suspend strategies, multi-cluster optimization and concurrency scaling patterns to help teams deliver predictable performance without overspending. Leave with practical tactics to tune compute for diverse workloads.

speakers.bio: Srujana Kandimalla is a Senior Product Manager leading efforts to help customers manage Snowflake at scale. With a decade of experience creating products in Healthcare Financial Technology, Usage-based Billing and now Manageability, she is passionate about eliminating operational inefficiencies and bringing transparency to usage, governance and cost. An Aerospace Engineer from Cornell University, Srujana thrives on tackling intricate problems, and driving innovation at Snowflake.

title: Messy Pipelines Are Expensive: A Hygiene Framework for Cutting Snowflake Waste
abstract:
dbt’s dependency graphs make building and scaling data pipelines easier than ever, but hidden inefficiencies can quietly drive up costs. Redundant pipelines, oversized warehouses, forgotten workloads and slow queries can add up to serious Snowflake waste. 

In this session, you will learn a practical hygiene framework to identify and fix inefficiencies across your pipelines. We’ll also show you how autonomous agents can make this a continuous process, turning cleanup from a one-time chore into an ongoing advantage.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Millions Saved: Unity's Native Apps, Data Sharing and AI Strategy
abstract: Learn how Unity, known for its industry-leading game engine, transformed its analytics platform with Snowflake, delivering seamless data and app sharing with cost-efficient scalability. Discover how Unity's native application solution saved millions annually and enhanced customer experience. See how it's incorporating Cortex Agents with Streamlit to enable conversational analytics, helping customers maximize the value of their data. Walk away with insights on building native apps that deliver customer value while driving significant cost savings.

speakers.bio: Keir Sweeney is a Staff Software Engineer at Unity Technologies, where he's spent the last six years building the data platforms that power hundreds of thousands of games worldwide. With 12 years of experience in software engineering, Keir has been instrumental in delivering Unity Analytics 2.0 on Snowflake, architecting the data and customer features behind the Unity Developer Framework, and building out Unity's new Diagnostics & Analytics tooling — giving developers the insights they need to ship better games. When he's not wrangling data pipelines, you can find him shuffling through a competitive card game, making music, or out exploring trails with his dog.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Model Registry to Production API: Real-Time Inference Endpoints on Snowflake ML
abstract: Real-time applications need real-time predictions — but production serving often introduces extra infrastructure, data movement and governance gaps. In this session, learn how to operationalize low-latency inference on Snowflake by deploying models with Snowflake ML and exposing them through secure external endpoints. We cover reference architectures for request/response serving, feature retrieval patterns and techniques for scaling concurrency while managing latency and cost. You’ll also hear from customer VALID Systems on how it powered real-time financial decision-making in Snowflake in under 100 ms.

speakers.bio: Michael Ring is Chief Information & Technology Officer of VALID Systems, where he has led enterprise technology strategy since 2007. A financial services technology executive, he oversees platform architecture, data modernization, and ML integration within highly regulated banking environments. Under his leadership, VALID has embedded machine learning into core decision processes for over seven years and is actively evolving its data ecosystem on Snowflake. Michael partners with enterprise clients and executive stakeholders to ensure responsible AI adoption aligns with institutional governance and regulatory expectations.
Sumit is the lead of Snowflake MLOps Platform - leading the ML Platform team for last 4.5 years. Before that he had a long career at Google leading multiple teams under Google Ads.

title: Modern Data Governance and Security at Scale with Snowflake at T-Mobile
abstract: As data, AI and analytics adoption accelerates across T-Mobile’s Snowflake environment — supporting over 4,000 users, 3,100+ virtual warehouses and more than 10 petabytes of data — strong governance and security are essential to enabling trust, scale and innovation. This session explores our security strategy, including the move away from passwords toward stronger authentication mechanisms, continuous monitoring of security posture, network access controls and SOX controls. We will also highlight how T-Mobile enables emerging AI security considerations and governed data sharing across all departments through an internal data marketplace that balances accessibility with enterprise-grade governance.

speakers.bio: Chris James, T-Mobile's Principal Architect, brings 22 years of expertise in Data Warehouse strategy, Architecture, Administration, and Performance optimization. He led the transition to Snowflake, enhancing analytics and cost-efficiency. His role was pivotal in steering T-Mobile's data infrastructure towards efficiency, reliability, and scalability, ensuring that the company's data management systems are not just robust but also future-proof. As a respected thought leader, Chris has shared insights at various conferences, showcasing his dedication and leadership in modern data management, benefiting both T-Mobile and the wider tech community.

title: Modernizing Banking: From Legacy Spark to Near Real Time with Snowflake Openflow
abstract: UnionBank Philippines’ Quickloans workflows were constrained by legacy Informatica BDS and Cloudera (Apache Spark), blocking Cloudera decommissioning. By refactoring and migrating these complex pipelines to Snowflake Openflow’s native constructs, the team fully preserved business rules while materially boosting processing speed. The new design proved stable with low latency during peak windows (for example, paydays), outperforming the previous solution. This outcome validated retiring Cloudera and enabled a full move to a scalable, cloud-native Snowflake data architecture.

speakers.bio: Gil Brian D. Mangussad is a seasoned technology leader with deep expertise in data platforms, solutions engineering, and DevOps automation across large‑scale enterprise environments. He currently serves as the AI/ML Platform Head at UnionBank of the Philippines, where he leads initiatives that ensure the reliability, performance, and scalability of the bank’s data, model management, and integration platforms driving innovation and operational excellence in the organization’s AI and advanced analytics ecosystem.

Strategic Solution Architect & Data Leader driving complex business transformations through the convergence of Data, Analytics, and GenAI. Proven track record in overseeing the full lifecycle of data-intensive solutions—from initial vision to enterprise-scale implementation

title: Modernizing Data Security: How Ally Financial Scaled Native Classification
abstract: Discover how Ally Financial modernized data security by migrating from third-party solutions to Snowflake Horizon Catalog's native data classification capabilities. In this session, we’ll share our journey to scaling classification across the enterprise, enhancing data protection and compliance. Learn about the challenges faced, key implementation strategies and measurable improvements in security management. Gain practical insights to help your organization streamline and strengthen data security using Snowflake’s native tools.

speakers.bio: Data Engineering, Enterprise Data and Analytics

Joshua is a Data Engineer at Ally Financial, where he has been driving modern data platform initiatives since 2020. He began his career through the Workforce Investment Network, completing an intensive six-month boot camp that launched his path into data engineering at Ally.

In his role, Joshua has built and supported enterprise data pipelines and, more recently, has led Ally’s implementation of Snowflake’s native data classification capabilities across the Snowflake environment. His work focuses on strengthening data governance and accelerating responsible, scalable use of data by enabling consistent discovery and classification of sensitive information.

title: Modernizing Enterprise Business Intelligence: PowerBI to Snowflake
abstract: This case study demonstrates replacing Microsoft PowerBI with Snowflake AI Data Cloud, achieving 98.8% cost savings ($300K→$3.6K annually) with superior performance. The platform leverages Snowflake Cortex AI Analyst for natural language queries, intelligence agents for autonomous analysis, Cortex Search and AI Functions, delivering advanced analytics through open-source technologies (React, FastAPI). This modernization eliminates vendor lock-in and proves cloud-native AI platforms can replace traditional BI tools at a fraction of the cost while providing enhanced capabilities and flexibility.

speakers.bio: Siddharth Prasad is a Lead Architect driving data and cloud modernization initiatives at Cox Automotive. With 19+ years of engineering experience, he specializes in AWS, Azure, Snowflake, Kafka, and large‑scale data architecture. Known for designing resilient pipelines and leading cloud migrations, he blends deep technical expertise with innovative thinking to deliver high‑impact, scalable solutions.

title: Modernizing Public Health: The State of Illinois' AI and Data-Sharing Strategy
abstract: Public health agencies have long relied on traditional, siloed informatics platforms that hinder data sharing, collaboration and real-time decision-making. The Illinois Department of Public Health (IDPH) is addressing these challenges through the CDC Data Modernization Initiative — a comprehensive effort to enhance data collection, integration, analysis and dissemination. To support this transformation, IDPH is leveraging Snowflake’s leading-edge cloud-native platform features to create a scalable, interoperable and AI-driven data ecosystem. This session explores IDPH’s strategic approach to modernizing public health data infrastructure and how Snowflake is helping achieve these goals.

speakers.bio: Dejan Jovanov is the Chief Data and Informatics Officer at the Illinois Department of Public Health. He leads Illinois’ Data Modernization Initiative, overseeing enterprise data governance, interoperability, AI innovation, and cloud transformation. As the health department first CDIO, he is building a predictive, trusted, and interoperable public health data ecosystem that enables faster response, stronger partnerships, and better outcomes for Illinois residents.

title: Morgan Stanley’s Journey to an AI-Driven Marketing Engine
abstract: For years, Morgan Stanley Wealth Management's use of Snowflake was focused on analytics and reporting. Following a three-year martech modernization, the team transformed this platform into a foundation for omni-channel activation across its brands: E*TRADE, Morgan Stanley Online and MS@Work. With deep integration into Adobe and other martech platforms, leveraging predictive and generative AI models, the platform now provides data, marketing-accelerated campaign execution, improved targeting precision and increased revenue. Hear how the journey unfolded — and what’s next in the year ahead.

speakers.bio: Frank Hong is the Head of Wealth Management Marketing Technology at Morgan Stanley. He has been with the firm for 4 years, focusing on spearheading a 5-year transformation strategy, execution, and change management for Morgan Stanley Wealth Management Marketing and Technology.  He has modernized and consolidated redundant platforms across multiple Wealth Management brands (E*TRADE, MS Online, Shareworks) spanning multiple products and services (Brokerage, Bank, Advisory, Stock Plan).  Frank manages a diverse team of over 60 professionals across the US and India, fostering collaboration and innovation.

title: Move Fast, Don't Break Trust: New Developer Blueprint for AI-Driven Governance
abstract: Governance is often a finish line hurdle that slows delivery. This session introduces developer-native governance using Snowflake Cortex Code to move guardrails upstream. Learn how to embed AI-assisted peer reviews directly into Snowflake Workspaces, grounding LLMs in Snowflake Horizon Catalog metadata — including schemas, tags and masking policies. We demonstrate a "shift-left" workflow where Cortex identifies gaps and generates compliant DDL in real time. See how to automate policy mapping, produce human-readable audit justifications and reduce manual reviews by 60%. Transform governance from a bottleneck into an intelligent assistant for scalable, trusted data engineering.

speakers.bio: I am a Principal Engineer at Salesforce, leading enterprise-scale data platform architecture and governance. With over a decade of experience across Snowflake, AI-driven data engineering, and cloud-native platforms, I specialize in building secure, scalable, developer-first data ecosystems.
Mike has spent his career implementing and supporting data warehouses. Currently supporting Snowflake on the Salesforce data platform team.

title: NBCU Adsales Platform and Operation Model
abstract: This session reviews NBCU products as well as the current operating model, exploring how Adsales DCR data products fit into the current data fabric architecture and how the operating model empowers data producers to democratize data across key functions such as audience insights, measurement, identity and activation. This model facilitates the governed and scalable flow of information to various endpoints, including data clean rooms, internal shares, visualization tools and adtech platforms. By operationalizing these processes, we ensure that data is accessible and actionable across the organization while maintaining strict governance and security standards.

speakers.bio: Natalie Mellon is the VP of Identity & Data Product at NBCUniversal, where she leads the strategy and delivery of enterprise identity and data products powering advertising, measurement, and audience activation. Her work focuses on building scalable, privacy‑first identity solutions, advancing identity graph capabilities, and enabling secure data collaboration across partners and platforms.

Gavin Zhu is a Senior Director of Data Science at NBCUniversal, where he leads 1P and 3P advanced measurement and analytics initiatives. He specializes in outcome‑based measurement, including attribution, incrementality, and media mix modeling (MMM), with a focus on translating complex data into clear, actionable insights for advertisers and internal stakeholders.

title: Neo4j and Snowflake: Turn Flat Tables into Graph Intelligence
abstract: Learn how Neo4j unlocks graph insights from relational Snowflake tables. Use Graph Analytics for Snowflake to run graph algorithms directly on Snowflake data — no ETL required. With Snowflake Cortex AI integration, you can describe a business problem in plain language and generate graph-powered insights written back to a Snowflake table. Graph analytics help solve problems like fraud detection, customer 360, recommendations and supply chain optimization by revealing relationships hidden in tabular data. When a full property graph is the best fit, Neo4j’s Data Importer makes it to bring Snowflake data into Neo4j with gen AI-assisted graph model generation and simple point-and-click schema editing.

speakers.bio: Corydon has nearly a decade of experience in data science consulting and currently works as a technical product marketer focused on Graph Analytics at Neo4j. He is interested in how graph algorithms can help businesses uncover relationships, improve decision-making, and maximize ROI, and is the author of "A Practical Introduction to Graph Algorithms".

title: New Jersey Department of Education: State Longitudinal Data System
abstract: The New Jersey Department of Education (NJ DOE), which oversees K-12 public education, selected Snowflake in 2021 to modernize its longitudinal education data platform. This replaced Oracle and legacy file workflows with a single, governed cloud platform for student and staff data, submissions and analytics. On Snowflake, the NJ DOE now loads weekly deltas and EOY snapshots, runs nine major SLEDS submissions (including student, staff and course rosters), and utilizes production integrations (Tableau, dbt). The result is streamlined data collection, improved quality, and accelerated statewide reporting and analytics.

speakers.bio: Shashi Yellambhatla spearheads the digital transformation of one of the nation’s most complex K–12 data ecosystems. A strategic leader of large-scale modernization, he directed the New Jersey Department of Education’s pivotal transition to a centralized Snowflake architecture, replacing antiquated workflows with cloud-native, real-time analytics. His expertise lies at the intersection of data warehousing and system design, with a relentless focus on optimizing performance and security at a statewide scale. Shashi’s work ensures that data is not just a record, but a catalyst for educational equity and excellence throughout New Jersey.

title: New Opportunities in the Consumer Goods Industry with AI and Accessible Data
abstract: The consumer goods industry is unlocking new opportunities with AI and technological advancements. This session will explore emerging trends shaping the market and how brands are leveraging data-driven strategies to drive growth, improve efficiency and enhance consumer engagement. Leading experts will provide industry insights, followed by a discussion with a consumer goods executive on how to harness AI and data analytics to transform the company. Join us to discover how leading companies stay agile, competitive and future-ready in a rapidly evolving market.

speakers.bio: Freddy is part of Snowflake’s Retail & Consumer Goods Industry team and serves as the Global Head of Consumer Goods.  He has spent the past 20 years driving business outcomes through data and analytics for large consumer brands and retailers across the globe. Freddy was most recently at Circana (formerly IRi), a leading data and analytics company where he served as VP of Commercial Strategy and Enablement for the Supply Chain Center of Excellence. Prior experience includes leading analytics teams at PepsiCo, ConAgra Foods, and Retail Solutions Inc. .

speakers.job_title: Head of AI and MLE Platform
speakers.bio: Ryan Wolbeck is the Head of AI & ML Platform at Kraft Heinz, where he leads the deployment of early Generative AI innovations across the enterprise. His work focuses on building scalable AI platforms, operational governance, and responsible adoption models that accelerate business value. With a decade of experience leading development teams, Ryan brings a deep technical background spanning computer vision, traditional machine learning, and applied statistics across multiple industries.

With 20+ years of experience, Sam Wong is a seasoned data and AI executive driving digital transformation. As Senior Director of Data and AI for Mark Anthony Group, Sam oversees the mission-critical infrastructure and operational frameworks required to power AI for a leading international beverage company. He architected a modern data ecosystem and built a high-performing team designed to scale with the AI landscape. A cornerstone of his leadership is his AI Incubator program, which transforms raw data into measurable ROI through production-ready AI. A sought-after speaker and instructor, Sam is known for translating complex technical foundations into practical business outcomes.

title: NHS London: Transforming Healthcare for 10M Patients Through Data Sharing
abstract: London serves a population of 10 million patients. NHS OneLondon has embarked on an ambitious program with Snowflake to improve health outcomes for this population by enabling secure data sharing across NHS trusts, local authorities and research organizations. Learn how this program is breaking down data silos to improve patient outcomes and the health and well-being of London's citizens — and discover how these data-sharing patterns can be applied to your own multiparty collaboration challenges.

speakers.bio:
Mark Kewley is the Director of the London Secure Data Environment. He leads the city’s work to establish a linked longitudinal record for the full population of 11m patients as an asset for proactive care, intelligent planning, and globally distinctive R&D. He was previously Chief Officer for a London Health Innovation Network, enabling clinical teams to adopt novel diagnostic and therapeutics.

Prior to joining the NHS Mark was a Senior Policy Adviser to the UK Prime Minister, working in the No.10 Policy Unit. He specialised in policies to improve public service delivery and the Life Sciences sector. He studied Molecular & Cellular Biochemistry at Oxford University.

title: Nissan's Journey to Connected Product Intelligence and Agent-Driven Innovation
abstract: Join Nissan's CDO, Francois Peltier, as he shares Nissan's transformation toward an intelligent, connected product ecosystem powered by Snowflake. Discover how Nissan bridges vehicle, customer, and dealer 360 through connected products data, enabling a data mesh architecture that allows real-time decision-making. Learn their approach to building certified data products that unlock new revenue streams through connected mobility, personalized customer experiences and predictive maintenance. They'll showcase breakthrough use cases including Bill of Materials forecasting, digital customer behavior analytics and competitive dealer pricing intelligence through connected product insights.

speakers.bio:
Chief Data & Innovation Officer with global automotive and mobility experience, leading enterprise-scale data, AI, and digital transformation across a Fortune-level OEM. 
Proven track record of building data platforms, enabling software-defined vehicles, operationalizing AI, and driving measurable cost, efficiency, and revenue impact. Trusted executive partner to C-suite and senior leadership, bridging technology, product, and business strategy. 
Experienced in scaling teams, modernizing operating models, and delivering transformation in complex, regulated environments.
Board member of the Chamber of Commerce of Nashville and Distinguished Adjunct Professor at Vanderbilt University

abstract: Nokia and Snowflake have created an analytical data product to be shared and utilized by AT&T in Snowflake to have insights into, visualize and enrich other telecom data.

speakers.bio: Kemal Parlar – is the Sales Director for Cloud and Network Services at Nokia, based in Atlanta, where he focuses primarily on AT&T and T-Mobile clients. With more than 28 years of experience across four countries, Kemal has held diverse engineering and leadership roles within global technology organizations, spanning vendor, consulting, and service provider environments. He earned his MBA from Emory University’s Goizueta Business School, concentrating on Strategy and General Management. His current areas of focus include Data Management, Artificial Intelligence, Analytics, Software, Cloud Technologies, and Consulting Services for mobile and fiber telecommunications.

title: NTT DOCOMO’s Data Platform Evolution for the Agentic AI Era
abstract: In the era of Agentic AI, platforms must truly understand context. Last year, NTT DOCOMO presented an architecture for autonomous network operations. This system is built on scalable Snowflake Openflow pipelines, Apache Iceberg and Cortex AI. This year, we'll share how Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Agents bridge the semantic gap, allowing AI to analyze perabytes of mobile network data alongside unstructured files like rack diagrams and procurement documents. We'll also show how we moved from centralized storage to a decentralized data mesh, managed through an Internal Marketplace on Snowflake. Join us to see how this approach connects data and gives useful insights to many different users.

speakers.bio:
Since joining NTT DOCOMO, Inc., I have primarily been involved in developing petabyte-scale big data analytics infrastructure. Currently, I am committed to transforming our internal data ecosystem from a centralized data lake to a decentralized data architecture (Data Mesh) to enable more sophisticated and data-driven mobile network operations.
And I've been selected as a Snowflake Data Superhero for 2026.

I am a Data Engineer at NTT DOCOMO, INC., specializing in the design and operation of large-scale data platforms.
My expertise lies in building robust ETL pipelines using SaaS-based architectures and Apache Spark.
By focusing on system optimization, I have consistently achieved significant performance gains and cost efficiencies.
I am passionate about architecting resilient data systems capable of handling massive-scale environments.
