title: Ohio Tax: Call Center Analytics, Call Transcription and Agentic Workloads
abstract: The Ohio Department of Taxation administers most state and many local taxes for Ohio, including income, sales and business taxes. This year, it's standing up a Snowflake environment in Commercial Gov to run a Snowflake Cortex AI POC focused on call-center call quality analytics and a taxpayer chatbot, ahead of full FedRAMP High feature parity. Expected outcomes are faster, more consistent taxpayer support, better insight into call drivers and a reusable AI pattern for future tax and fraud analytics.

speakers.company: State of Ohio Department of Tax
speakers.bio: Budwhite has rich IT experiences in various industries. Prior employed by State of Ohio, he was Asia CIO for Polestar Automobile, CIO for WBA International and Chief  Architect  for Philips.

title: Onboarding AI: From Business Ambiguity to AI Accuracy with Semantic Models
abstract: AI initiatives often stall not because of algorithms but because underlying data lacks structure, meaning and governance. Without a semantic foundation, AI agents and analytics tools struggle to interpret business context. In this session, we explore how semantic modeling bridges the gap between business meaning and AI-ready data. Learn how modern data teams use structured semantic layers, governed data models and collaborative design workflows to turn ambiguous business questions into accurate AI outputs, enabling trusted analytics, reliable AI agents and scalable data products on Snowflake.

speakers.bio: Serge Gershkovich has nearly two decades of experience in data architecture and business intelligence. He is currently Head of Product at SqlDBM, a Snowflake Data Superhero, and author of the bestselling Data Modeling with Snowflake.

title: Ontology on Snowflake: From Unified Data to AI-Powered Intelligence
abstract: Discover how Snowflake’s ontology-driven architecture unifies data, semantics and AI. This session explores how to model real-world knowledge directly on Snowflake using metadata, inference rules and ontology layers — then reason over it with Snowflake Cortex Analyst and Snowflake Intelligence. See how consistent semantics enable context-aware analytics, natural language reasoning and a foundation for truly intelligent data applications — all built natively on Snowflake.

speakers.bio: Tianxia Jia is a Principal AI Architect on Snowflake’s Applied Field Engineering team, helping enterprises scale AI and data-driven transformation. With 15 years of technical leadership across AWS, Snowflake, and BP, he specializes in delivering end-to-end AI/ML solutions that connect data, cloud, and advanced analytics into measurable business impact. Tianxia holds a Ph.D. in Geophysics from Columbia University and is a CFA® charterholder.

title: Open Data Infrastructure: The Next Era of Data Architecture
abstract: The modern data stack solved for siloed analytics, but AI and operational workloads demand a more adaptable foundation. In this thought leadership session, Fivetran explores open data infrastructure, an evolution beyond the modern stack that separates storage from compute and applies open standards across layers. Learn how to preserve interoperability, avoid lock-in, and scale analytics and AI with control over cost and architecture — then see ODI brought to life in a live demo.

speakers.job_title: Sr. Director Global Partner Sales Eng
speakers.bio: Kelly Kohlleffel leads Fivetran’s Global Partner Sales Engineering organization, working with technology and consulting partners to deliver modern data and AI solutions. He also hosts the Fivetran Data Podcast, where industry leaders share their data journeys, visions, and challenges. Prior to Fivetran, Kelly held leadership and sales roles at NTT Data, Hashmap, Hortonworks, and Oracle. Connect with Kelly on LinkedIn for insights on data, AI, and partnerships.

George Fraser is the CEO and co-founder of Fivetran, the global leader in the modern data movement. George founded Fivetran with Taylor Brown, the company’s Chief Operating Officer, in 2012 after completing the prestigious Y Combinator accelerator program. Under his leadership, Fivetran has grown from a startup into a global enterprise valued at $5.6 billion, serving thousands of companies worldwide to automate data workflows and drive innovation. He holds a PhD in Neurobiology from the University of Pittsburgh and a Bachelor of Science in Cognitive Science and Biology from Carnegie Mellon University.

title: Opening Keynote
abstract: Start Snowflake Summit 26 with a look at what’s next. Snowflake's CEO, Sridhar Ramaswamy, shares how organizations are turning AI’s promise into real business transformation and how the Snowflake AI Data Cloud is enabling a new era of enterprise intelligence and agentic innovation with data at the center. Joined by industry leaders and luminaries, he explores how agentic AI is already reshaping global industries and the critical challenges and opportunities facing today’s top business leaders.

title: Open Semantic Interchange: Dimensions and Ontologies for Agents
abstract: As data, BI and AI converge, enterprises need a shared semantic foundation that works across tools, models and workloads. This session introduces Open Semantic Interchange (OSI) — a minimal, expressive metadata standard built on three primitives: concepts, relationships and relational formulas. OSI supports both dimensional BI models and ontological application semantics, enabling interoperability across ecosystems like dbt, Legend, Palantir, custom applications and BI dashboards. We’ll show how OSI enables a durable way to define semantics once and use them everywhere, while insulating models from schema drift and accelerating cross-system integration in Snowflake.

speakers.bio: Ankit Tandon is a product leader at RelationalAI focused on bringing decision intelligence to enterprises by combining data, business logic, and AI reasoning. Previously, he led product at Google across Search experiences including Image & Video Search, and earlier helped build large-scale mapping platforms at Uber and Microsoft. His work focuses on enabling organizations to move from data to trustworthy, explainable decisions using semantic models, knowledge graphs, and advanced reasoning systems.

Josh Klahr is the Head of Analytics Product Management at Snowflake, where he is responsible for driving the core database product roadmap and strategy. Josh has over 25 years of experience working with data and analytics. Prior to joining Snowflake, Josh was VP of product management for Splunk, where he was responsible for the Splunk Enterprise and Splunk Cloud product lines. He has also spent time in product management roles at Yahoo!, Pivotal Software, Greenplum, and AtScale.

title: OpenTelemetry and Iceberg: Foundation for Observability at Scale
abstract: AI-driven systems generate massive volumes of metrics, logs and traces with extreme cardinality and long retention needs. Traditional observability backends struggle to scale, govern and control costs. In this session, learn how Observe uses Snowflake as the backbone of an open observability architecture built on OpenTelemetry and Apache Iceberg. We’ll show how telemetry flows from OTel-instrumented apps through collectors into user’s own Iceberg data lakehouse, enabling open access, strong governance and analytics at petabyte scale. This approach lowers storage and query costs while delivering faster incident detection and resolution — without vendor lock-in.

speakers.bio: Jiayuan Chen is a Software Engineer at Observe (acquired by Snowflake in 2026). He is passionate about designing and building large scale data-intensive applications, and has been working in the areas of observability, data lake, query engine, ingestion, and analytics. He keeps up with latest technologies and innovates things that spark joy.

Kai is a Software Engineering Manager within the Observability BU at Snowflake, where he leads the Data Management and Ingest Platform teams. He is dedicated to building end-to-end solutions that empower customers to collect data using open standards like OpenTelemetry (OTel). By combining the openness of Apache Iceberg with the power of Snowflake’s high-performance query engine, his teams enable organizations to manage massive datasets within their own data lakes while ensuring effective and low-latency troubleshooting at a petabyte scale.

title: Optimize Your Entire Lakehouse with Snowflake
abstract: Join Snowflake's Apache Iceberg Product Leaders to understand the different interoperable lakehouse architectures now supported, the tradeoffs for each, and how Snowflake can integrate in your infrastructure. We'll share how to bring Snowflake to your data managed by your catalog, the benefits you can expect as you graduate to Horizon Catalog, and we'll cover Snowflake Storage for Iceberg Tables. With each step, Snowflake improves performance, effort and costs.

speakers.bio: Kaushal leads Product for Storage at Snowflake, driving innovations across structured, unstructured, and lakehouse data. Earlier, he led cost and performance optimization efforts at Snowflake and previously shaped key products in Microsoft’s Azure and Ads divisions. Kaushal earned his Bachelor’s and Master’s degrees from IIT Bombay and MBA from Harvard Business School.

title: PagerDuty Uses Snowflake Cortex AI for Efficient, Consistent Customer Engagement
abstract: PagerDuty, in collaboration with Snowflake Professional Services, has transformed how its leadership, sales and field teams access information and make decisions, thanks to the implementation of Snowflake Cortex AI services. The solution leverages Cortex Agents, Cortex Analyst and Cortex Search to gather data from multiple sources on the customer's next best action, contact information and recommended next steps. This information is then concisely summarized and presented in a unified, actionable format. PagerDuty estimates this initiative will result in multi-million-dollar savings while ensuring organizational consistency in customer interactions throughout the customer lifecycle.

speakers.bio: Manu Raj is Senior Director of Enterprise Data, Analytics & AI at PagerDuty, with 20+ years of experience transforming data into strategic business value. A seasoned data leader, he has built and scaled analytics organizations across product, sales, marketing, and customer success. Known for bridging the gap between data engineering and business outcomes, Manu has led enterprise-wide initiatives that drive operational efficiency and revenue growth. With prior leadership roles at Fortune 500 companies, he brings deep expertise in enterprise data strategy, AI-driven decision-making, and building high-performing data teams.

title: PayPal: Merchant Fee Predictions in Snowflake ML
abstract: At PayPal, third-party providers could predict interchange and scheme fees but granular transaction-level fees were arriving only at the end of the month. This would take one month for most cases and three days in best-case scenarios with an error-prone process. In this session, PayPal engineers explain how PayPal built a modern near real-time AI/ML pipeline in Snowflake to accurately predict merchant fees daily at the transaction level. Learn how this accelerates settlement times, saves costs and enables faster and more expansive merchant acquisition.

speakers.bio: Marisa Tania is a Software Engineer at PayPal, where she builds and deploys production machine learning systems for large-scale financial applications. Her work focuses on distributed training, model lifecycle management, and building reliable ML pipelines on modern cloud data platforms to support scalable AI systems.

Applied ML engineer focusing on distributed computing for AI and machine learning. Especially interested in performance and infrastructure challenges that arise when scaling models and complex workloads.

title: Platform Keynote
abstract: See how to move beyond AI experimentation and deliver measurable results in this keynote with Christian Kleinerman, Executive Vice President of Product at Snowflake. You’ll discover how Snowflake is redefining what’s possible with the Snowflake AI Data Cloud — and what that means for your organization. Hear directly from Snowflake leaders and customers transforming their businesses, and learn how to unify your data, govern AI with confidence and deploy secure, scalable agents that turn insight into action — making AI real for business at every scale.

speakers.bio:
Christian Kleinerman serves as Snowflake’s EVP of Product and has been with the company since 2018. He oversees the company’s global product strategy and vision.

Christian is a database expert with over 20 years of experience working with various database technologies and has more than 15 years of management and leadership experience. Most recently, Christian worked at Google leading YouTube’s infrastructure and data systems. Prior to that, he served as General Manager of the Data Warehousing product unit at Microsoft where he was responsible for a broad portfolio of products. Christian holds a BS in Industrial Engineering from Los Andes University in Colombia, and he is a named inventor on numerous Snowflake patents.

title: Postgres and Snowflake Open Lakehouse: ETL is dead, ELT is dead. T lives on.
abstract: Learn how data engineering roles continue to evolve as the world of OLTP and OLAP merge with Snowflake new capabilities. With increasing integration between Snowflake and Postgres, this session demonstrates new architectures that are now feasible with Snowflake and Postgres. You'll see how new features like pg_lake, in combination with Iceberg, will bridge the divide between Snowflake and Postgres as you say goodbye to older extract and load design patterns.

speakers.bio: Firvin Janardanan is a Senior Solutions Architect at Snowflake, specializing in large-scale platform migrations and the modernization of legacy data estates. With a deep background in traditional OLTP and on-premise/cloud data warehouses, Firvin helps organizations de-risk the transition to the Snowflake AI Data Cloud by building resilient, future-proof architectures. His work focuses on collapsing the divide between transactional and analytical worlds, replacing brittle ETL patterns with high-performance data engineering solutions.

Elizabeth is PostgreSQL contributor and developer advocate at Snowflake. Elizabeth is passionate about open source advocacy and education. She is on the board of the United States PostgreSQL Association (PgUS), hosts monthly Postgres Meetup for All, and co-hosts annual PostGIS Day. She is also active in local tech communities in Lawrence, Kansas and Kansas City.

title: Power Analytics and AI Wherever Your Data Lives
abstract: Connect to Apache Iceberg tables in AWS S3 via Catalog Linked Databases with zero data movement. Register existing AWS Glue catalogs in seconds, making external tables instantly discoverable and queryable. With support for Apache Iceberg V3 tables, Snowflake supports the VARIANT data type, enabling efficient storage and querying of semi-structured data. Apply Snowflake Horizon governance including RBAC and dynamic masking policies to your Iceberg tables. Then leverage Snowflake Intelligence to ask natural language questions against your lakehouse with AI-generated verifiable SQL at enterprise scale.

speakers.bio:
Vino Duraisamy is a builder, strategist, and expert translator of complex technology. With a decade of experience architecting scalable data and AI solutions for industry leaders in retail, consumer tech, legal, and big tech, she possesses a rare, end-to-end perspective on what it truly takes to bring high-impact data products from concept to production.

In her current role as a Developer Advocate at Snowflake, she is a key voice in shaping the go-to-market strategy for Snowflake's Data Engineering and AI products.

title: Powering Intelligent Customer Recommendations at David Yurman with Snowflake ML
abstract: Learn how David Yurman’s DM&A team leveraged Snowflake and dbt to build their first in-house, enterprise-scale recommendation system. To deliver personalized suggestions across diverse segments, the team developed high-fidelity analytical marts using a hybrid of content-based and collaborative filtering. By using dbt for orchestration and Snowflake as the ML serving layer, they eliminated external platforms, reduced complexity and improved performance. This modern architecture now serves as a scalable foundation for continuous innovation in customer personalization and eliminated the need for external ML platforms.

speakers.bio: Seasoned technology professional with a proven record delivering end to end technology solutions. Outstanding ability to capture business processes and transform technology solutions to evolve the business functions, Design ecosystems with a focus on long-term stability and reliability.

title: Powering Production ML at Scale with Snowflake Feature Store
abstract: Production ML relies on features that are consistent, governed and available in real time. In this session, we share what’s new in Snowflake Feature Store and how teams productionize large-scale ML with minimal operational overhead. We'll highlight new product capabilities that simplify feature reuse and improve freshness, reliability and performance improvement around efficient aggregations. You will hear about Attentive's customer journey migrating its feature store to Snowflake for streamlined production-grade ML efficiently across the organization at scale.

speakers.bio: Staff Software Engineer on the Machine Learning Platform at Attentive, where I design and build the infrastructure that powers data and ML at scale. With over 20 years of experience spanning data engineering, streaming systems, and platform architecture, I'm passionate about mixing creativity with technology to solve hard data problems.

title: Powering the Future of Merchant Experiences with Snowflake Cortex AI
abstract: Learn how Global Payments is integrating a talk-to-your-data agent powered by Snowflake Cortex AI into its merchant portal. This innovation allows merchants to use natural language queries to instantly extract insights from complex business data, eliminating the need for reports or SQL. The customer value is instant, actionable insights for faster decision-making. Merchants can ask direct questions like "sales trend last month?" or "highest dispute status?" and receive reliable answers from both structured and unstructured data. This democratizes data access, accelerates daily operations, and offers a personalized, efficient self-service experience, freeing up merchant time for strategic growth

speakers.job_title: SVP of Data Engineering and Platforms
speakers.bio: Mark Kubik is a Senior Vice President of Data Engineering and Analytics Global Payments Inc. Mark built and launched Global Payments’ cloud native data lake house in 2019 and now maturing it to and Open Lakehouse Architecture in 2026. He is responsible for data management and delivery, data platform, business insights, and data as a service products that serve internal partners and millions of external customers worldwide. Mark has 24+ years experience in Financial Services and 34+ years in the data space which has earned Mark the reputation as a data specialist and authoritarian in data management, analytics, quality, and governance

Billy Cummins leads Enterprise Data Platforms at Global Payments, where he drives the strategy and execution of the data and technology platforms that power one of the world's largest payment technology companies. Billy brings deep expertise in building scalable, enterprise-grade data architectures on Snowflake. His work sits at the intersection of platform engineering, data strategy, and business enablement, helping Global Payments turn its vast data assets into competitive advantage. Billy is passionate about solving complex data challenges at scale and sharing practical lessons learned from the front lines of enterprise data transformation.

title: Practice 360: Unifying Practice Leadership Metrics on Snowflake
abstract: Healthcare is one of the most data-intensive industries, yet leadership teams often lack a single, trusted view of enterprise performance. Florida Cancer Specialists & Research Institute (FCS) built Practice 360 on Snowflake to deliver a governed source of truth for leadership metrics across finance, clinical operations, revenue cycle, workforce and quality. Snowflake enabled FCS to centralize structured and semi-structured data, standardize KPIs, isolate workloads and scale real-time analytics across 100+ clinics without performance tradeoffs. Practice 360 is now the executive system of record, enabling faster decisions, benchmarking and scalable growth.

speakers.company: Florida Cancer Specialists and Research Institute
speakers.bio: Sanitha Mugala is a Manager of Advanced Analytics at Florida Cancer Specialists and Research Institute, where she leads the development of scalable data platforms, advanced analytics capabilities, and AI/ML solutions. With a strong foundation in data engineering, she blends hands-on technical skills with a talent for designing enterprise analytics frameworks that transform data into meaningful, actionable insights. Sanitha has held engineering and analytics roles across the healthcare and financial service industries, bringing deep experience in optimizing Snowflake and modern data stacks. A true lover of data, she brings both technical depth and business acumen to everything she builds.

Aya Alajrash is a Clinical Data Engineer at Florida Cancer Specialists, where she builds scalable Snowflake-based data platforms that support oncology analytics, research, and quality reporting. With over nine years of experience in healthcare data and enterprise analytics, she specializes in designing reliable data models and automated pipelines that transform complex clinical data into actionable insights. Aya holds a master’s degree in Healthcare Informatics and Analytics and is passionate about using modern data technologies to advance data-driven healthcare.

title: Prep Exposure‑Level Advertising Data for Enterprise AI with Snowflake Cortex
abstract:
Exposure‑level advertising data is one of the most powerful — and most difficult — assets to operationalize analytics and AI. In this session, The Trade Desk shows how it transforms trillions of granular exposure logs into a governed, AI‑ready data product in Snowflake that can be used by clients, analytics teams and Snowflake Intelligence. 

Attendees will learn how exposure logs can be modeled, enriched and governed in Snowflake to support interactive analytics, real‑time inference and agent‑driven workflows — while maintaining enterprise‑grade trust, privacy and scalability.

speakers.bio: Natalie leads the build, execution and go-to-market for all external APIs between The Trade Desk and CDPs, clean rooms and cloud platforms. Her goal is to advocate for more 1P data to be pushed to and from The Trade Desk for activation, conversions and exposure logs to drive overall better campaign performance for advertisers. She previously worked at LiveRamp where she was focused on driving client success within their clean room suite.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Preventing the Snowball Effect: Morgan Stanley’s 10 Principles for Scale FinOps
abstract: Unchecked consumption can quietly compound into runaway spend. Morgan Stanley shares 10 guiding principles that prevent cost snowballing while enabling innovation. Learn how governance guardrails, real-time monitoring and cross-functional accountability create sustainable cloud economics at enterprise scale.

speakers.bio: Maneesh Chhabra spearheads the Technology, Media, and Telecom division at Snowflake, focusing on guiding clients through Snowflake-specific Financial Operations (FinOps) and cost management strategies. He assists in crafting strategic roadmaps for data stack modernization, utilizing his deep consulting experience to impart Snowflake-centric insights and best practices. Maneesh's efforts are pivotal in enabling businesses to maximize the value from their investments in Snowflake's platform, optimizing their tech infrastructures for superior business outcomes

Howard Goldberg is an Executive Director in Data & Analytics Technology at Morgan Stanley, where he leads the firm’s Snowflake platform team, including engineering, performance, and cost management. He brings deep expertise in operating large‑scale data platforms, with a focus on delivering secure, high‑performance solutions at global enterprise scale. At the Snowflake conference, Howard will share practical insights on adopting Snowflake at a global financial institution, balancing innovation, governance, and efficiency.

title: Providence Health: How Data Mesh Architecture Unlocked an AI-Powered Future
abstract: Over the past six years, Providence Health & Services has transformed the data and analytics strategy across the enterprise, implementing a data mesh architecture that focuses on a governed and trusted data foundation. This foundation is the crucial start to unlocking and accelerating value to be gained from expanding self-service analytics, AI capabilities and application development. Come hear Arek Kaczmarek, AVP of Data Engineering, Health Intelligence, and Kellie Larkin, VP of Identity Access Management, Enterprise Information & Security, as they share the journey to today and the path ahead.

speakers.bio: Arek Kaczmarek, AVP of Engineering at Providence Health.

Kellie Larkin is Vice President of Identity and Access Management at Providence Health, where he leads enterprise identity strategy, platform engineering, and large-scale modernization of identity infrastructure across one of the largest healthcare systems in the United States. With a background spanning software engineering, data platforms, and enterprise architecture, he focuses on building scalable identity services that improve security, operational resilience, and caregiver experience. His work centers on modern identity automation, telemetry-driven operations, and aligning identity platforms with clinical and enterprise systems at scale.

title: Real-Time Lead Prioritization with Snowflake Model Serving Boosts Podium Sales
abstract: Discover how Podium turned call-level data into real-time lead recommendations entirely inside Snowflake ML. Using the Snowflake Feature Store, we assembled a leakage-safe data set, trained an XGBoost model on a GPU compute pool and registered it in the Snowflake Model Registry — then deployed it through Snowpark Inference Services for real-time scoring. Our business app queries the REST API to prioritize outreach instantly, leveraging engagement momentum, appointment readiness and temporal patterns — driving measurable increases in lead conversion and showing how live, data-driven scoring can accelerate sales outcomes for any business.

speakers.bio: Andrew Christensen is a Staff Data Scientist at Podium, where he architects and scales the machine learning infrastructure that powers real-time growth and retention. With over a decade of experience in applied statistics and analytics, Andrew specializes in bridging the gap between complex model serving and measurable business outcomes. At Podium, he leads the strategic deployment of ML models within Snowflake to drive enterprise-wide efficiency. He holds an MS in Business Analytics from the University of Utah and a BA in Marketing from Southern Utah University.

Collin Austad is a seasoned analytics and data engineering leader currently driving innovation at Podium, where he architects Snowflake-native solutions to accelerate insight and decision-making. With deep experience in building production analytical systems—including machine learning in Snowflake and self-service frameworks—Collin bridges the divide between data and business teams. He is known for translating complex technical capabilities into practical, high-impact outcomes: reducing time-to-insight, empowering non-technical teams, and freeing analytics engineers to focus on strategic initiatives.

title: Regional Data Moves at Scale: Solving Data Locality Without Breaking Analytics
abstract: As global SaaS platforms scale, data locality and regional compliance become continuous operational requirements. Customer data must move across regions safely and predictably, while analytics pipelines remain consistent and trustworthy. In this session, we share how Zendesk designed a Snowflake-native approach for cross-regional account moves by aligning operational cutovers with explicit UTC day boundaries. By introducing a preemptive data-processing lock coordinated with upstream cutover guarantees, downstream layers process only fully closed days. The result is a predictable, low-maintenance pattern for regional data moves at scale.

speakers.bio: Shahid Faiz is a Principal Data Engineer at Zendesk, where he designs large-scale analytics infrastructure for global SaaS workloads. His work focuses on multi-region data architectures, compliance-driven data systems, and scalable data modeling on Snowflake. Shahid has spent over a decade building data platforms and enabling teams to operate reliable analytics systems at scale. He is passionate about simplifying complex data problems through practical architectural patterns.

title: Reinventing FP&A at NOV: From Excel Sprawl to Governed Analytics with Sigma
abstract: National Oilwell Varco (NOV) is a $9 billion global energy technology company operating across a complex landscape shaped by decades of acquisitions and more than 100 ERP systems. Historically, finance teams relied on spreadsheets, Access databases and siloed reporting processes — spending more time assembling data than analyzing it. By adopting Sigma on Snowflake, NOV built a governed finance data model that allows FP&A teams to explore data, drill from financial metrics into ERP transactions and collaborate on a shared source of truth. With this foundation in place, NOV is positioning its finance organization to move beyond reporting and toward AI-powered analysis and decision support.

speakers.bio: Robbie Prejean is Vice President of Financial Planning & Analysis at NOV, where he leads financial planning, forecasting, and analytics that support strategic decision-making across the company’s global operations. Over more than a decade with NOV, he has held finance and operational leadership roles, including five years based in Dubai supporting international business operations. His global experience and close partnership with operational teams inform his focus on modernizing FP&A through governed data models, cloud data platforms, and advanced analytics that enable finance teams to move beyond reporting toward faster insights and better decisions.

title: Replatforming 161 Data Products: How We Scaled Secure Delivery with Snowflake
abstract: Data providers have traditionally relied on bulk file delivery to replicate data — custom extracts, long build times and independent client ingestion pipelines that can introduce version skew and data drift between provider and consumer. In this session, we walk through how our data engineering team replatformed 161 highly customized property and analytics products from file-based delivery to Snowflake using secure views and private listings. We’ll cover the architecture, operational decisions and governance patterns that preserved customization, protected intellectual property and supported cross-region distribution — while reducing customer time-to-value from weeks to hours.

title: RGA's Journey: Scaling High-Volume Risk Models with Snowpark
abstract: This session explores how RGA transformed its large-scale risk model pipeline by migrating from a complex Spark and Docker architecture to a unified, Snowflake-native solution. By leveraging Snowpark and user-defined table functions, RGA eliminated data movement and the security risks of external processing. Attendees will learn how consolidating onto a single platform simplified infrastructure while improving performance, security and operational manageability. We will highlight how this streamlined architecture provides a highly scalable framework built to handle massive volume growth, ensuring the pipeline remains resilient as data demands increase.

speakers.bio: Data Engineering solution architect with Snowflake PS with a strong background in building scalable data processing pipelines and systems. With expertise in Apache Spark, Scala, Python, Snowflake and Cloud technologies have working experience on a wide range of projects involving large-scale data processing, real-time analytics, and cloud-based data infrastructure. Have implemented optimizing data engineering workflows, ensuring reliability, performance, and cost-efficiency.

Drew Marco is Executive Director of AI Enablement at Reinsurance Group of America with 30 years professional experience building and scaling AI, Data Science, Machine Learning, Analytics and Data capabilities through innovative platforms and organizational adoption strategies across financial services, healthcare, telecommunications, and CPG industries. Prior to RGA, Drew led a 100 person data and analytics practice providing strategic data and analytics delivery consulting to several Fortune 500 clients.  Drew holds a B.S. in Chemistry from Drake University.

title: Roche's Data Mesh Strategy: Governance, Observability and AI in Pharma
abstract: F. Hoffmann-La Roche, the world's leading provider of cancer treatments and Europe's third largest company by market cap, is building a cutting-edge data platform on Snowflake. This session explores Roche's approach to data mesh with mandatory data governance through object tagging, Snowflake Cortex AI integration including MCP server, and data observability supporting Roche's data mesh architecture. Learn how Roche is implementing these capabilities across its pharmaceutical operations, including through real-world use case deep dives, success stories and the company's technical domain roadmap.

speakers.bio: 12+ years’ experience in Data & AI Solution Engineering, Stephan has an international track record of identifying, scoping and developing enterprise-level solutions that substantially increases clients' ability to turn data into actionable insights.
Passionate Data Engineer | Community Builder | Innovator in Data Governance

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Samsung Ads: Building Open Analytics on a Single Data Copy with Apache Iceberg
abstract: Samsung Ads launched a unified, Iceberg-centric lakehouse via Snowflake Managed Iceberg Tables and Open Catalog. This shift solves the siloed data problem that hindered cross-system interoperability and slowed global workflows. With Apache Iceberg, they’ve enabled a global data mesh featuring cross-engine synchronization and automated, self-service ingestion. Some of the key results include 20-30% reduction in data processing costs and TCO, faster vendor onboarding and streamlined SLA-driven analytics, and global interoperability across all analytics teams. This architecture accelerates time-to-insight while drastically lowering operational overhead.

speakers.bio: Swati Goel is a Solutions Architect in Snowflake - APJ. She has over 16+ years experience across different domains and technologies in Data, Analytics and AI . Worked with organizations such as Google, Deloitte, Accenture and more in the past.

Ramnath Jayachandran is a Senior Engineering Leader at Samsung Ads, specializing in large-scale data platforms and advertising technology. With a deep background in data engineering, he leads the development of high-performance architectures that power global ad ecosystems. Ramnath focuses on building scalable, privacy-first solutions that bridge complex data sets with actionable insights.

title: SAP and Snowflake: Build a Business Data Fabric to Fuel Enterprise AI
abstract: Make enterprise AI work in practice by unifying SAP and Snowflake into a governed, business-context-driven data foundation. This session introduces SAP Business Data Cloud and explores how the SAP and Snowflake partnership helps enable high-impact use cases across analytics, planning and AI. Learn how zero-copy, bidirectional data sharing connects trusted SAP data products with Snowflake’s capabilities. Discover how to preserve business context, maintain governance and accelerate enterprise AI with a scalable, unified data foundation.

speakers.job_title: SVP, General Manager – SAP Data & AI
speakers.bio: Niclas Schlautkoetter is a seasoned business technology leader with over 20 years at SAP. He has a strong track record of advising global customers on data strategy and leading complex, multi-year SAP transformation programs. As SVP and Adoption General Manager for SAP Data & AI, Niclas focuses on helping customers realize tangible value from their data investments by accelerating adoption and driving measurable business outcomes. Previously, he served as Chief Architect and Global Head of Strategic Architecture Advisory for SAP Business Data Cloud, where he led global architecture teams supporting strategic customer cloud transformations and close alignment with Product Engineering.

title: Scale AI Capabilities with Genpact’s Autonomous AI Platform on Snowflake
abstract: Explore how Genpact’s Snowflake native autonomous AI platform, powered by Snowflake Cortex AI, supports enterprise-scale AI enablement. Built on AI Maestro™, Genpact’s advanced platform for managing AI workflows, and delivered through the Genpact AI Gigafactory model, this solution enhances the delivery and deployment of AI systems. It supports AI CoE setup with governed ecosystems designed to foster enterprise-wide AI adoption and deliver measurable business value. Learn actionable strategies to scale AI, enhance intelligent enterprise operations and achieve meaningful business outcomes. Explore this opportunity to stay at the forefront of AI innovation.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Scale Intelligence, Not Infrastructure: Unify Your Data Estate with Microsoft
abstract: Stop managing infrastructure and start scaling intelligence. Data silos shouldn't hold your AI initiatives and business insights hostage. In this session, you’ll learn how Microsoft and Snowflake make it easy to unify your data estate with native integrations that embrace interoperability, powering your analytics, AI and business applications — without complex infrastructure maintenance. We’ll showcase the latest advancements in connecting Snowflake with Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, Microsoft 365 Copilot and Teams to fuel everyday workflows, enhance productivity and drive business transformation.

speakers.bio: Shankar Narayanan SGS is a Snowflake Data Superhero with decades of experience implementing BI and Analytics Solutions to organizations across various industries. He has implemented Snowflake for some of the large Fortune 500 organizations. At Microsoft, he is responsible for enabling Snowflake Customers to be successful in their Data Journey and supporting Microsoft integrations with Snowflake.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Scaling AI in Manufacturing: Executive Panel
abstract:
The AI conversation in manufacturing has shifted from "if" to "how fast." This executive panel explores how industry giants leverage Snowflake to move beyond experimental pilots into scalable, production-ready AI. Core discussion pillars:

  • Value realization: Moving past the "pilot trap" and aligning AI initiatives with core business KPIs.
  • Unified data architecture: Consolidating IT/OT/IoT data for real-time operational transparency.
  • Trust and governance: Upskilling the workforce and establishing governance to balance automation with human oversight.

Join us to ensure your industry 4.0 roadmap transforms data and AI into a competitive advantage.

speakers.bio: Joined Toyota in 2001, I have spent 20 years in R&D designing cars. Geek with passion for Technology and Data. Leading Toyota Motor Europe's Data Mesh initiative. My mission: To make the company future-proof in Data and AI.

Vishwa Ram is the Vice President of Data Science and Analytics at Penske, where he plays a pivotal role in shaping the company’s data-driven strategies.  Under his leadership, Penske has achieved significant milestones in digital transformation, including the development and launch of a cutting-edge cloud-based analytics platform and several AI powered applications. Vishwa has also spearheaded initiatives to digitize the customer experience, enhancing operational efficiency and customer engagement.
 
Vishwa resides in the Seattle, WA area with his wife, their middle school-aged son, and their beloved dog.

Tim Long is the Global Head of Manufacturing in Snowflake's go-to-market team where he leads Manufacturing customers through data and AI transformations while fostering industry partnerships to expedite customer success. Before joining Snowflake, Tim was the Head of Data and Analytics at adidas North America and Head of Analytics and Data at Micron Technology, where he led the global manufacturing data environment to Snowflake in just four months. Tim holds a Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Wyoming and  a Master's degree in Business Analytics from NYU Stern.

title: Scaling AI in the Public Sector Showcase: From Pilots to Real-World Impact
abstract: Moving from AI "potential" to public sector reality requires more than just code — it requires a roadmap for scale. Join us for a showcase featuring public sector leaders who have successfully transitioned AI from proof of concepts to enterprise-grade production. We’ll dive into key mission outcomes, outline a blueprint for architecture patterns and governance, and share key considerations for how other organizations should navigate the complexities of government environments. We will also outline how Snowflake’s end-to-end AI value proposition — one founded on flexibility, security and ease of use — meet the specific needs of governments globally.

title: Scaling AI Product Analytics with Snowflake: Building Data-Informed AI
abstract: As organizations race to embed AI into their products, many face a core challenge: measuring AI feature performance — both algorithmically and in terms of customer impact — without significantly growing analytics teams. In this session, we share how we’re building an AI analytics foundation that enables data-informed AI product development at scale. Our work focuses on robust instrumentation, telemetry and analytics layers that support both model-level insights and customer-facing product insights. With a small team, we’ve leaned on Snowflake’s AI and analytics capabilities to accelerate insight generation, reduce manual overhead and scale impact without adding headcount.

speakers.bio: Hootan Kamran is an ML leader with 15+ years of experience in high-stakes predictive modeling and production AI. At SailPoint, he leads the evaluation and deployment of Generative AI features, specializing in AI product analytics and model governance. A Ph.D. in Industrial Engineering, his expertise spans causal inference and deep learning. He is highly experienced in Snowflake AI products, including Cortex, to drive mission-critical data applications.

Maryam Agahi is an AI Engineering Manager at SailPoint, where she leads teams building data platforms and machine learning capabilities for identity security products. Previously, she led data science and analytics initiatives at Meta, driving data-informed product decisions at global scale. Maryam focuses on connecting data infrastructure, analytics, and machine learning to transform complex data ecosystems into reliable platforms for AI-powered applications. She is passionate about helping organizations evolve their data capabilities to enable scalable, intelligent products and better decisions.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Scaling AI with Cyera, Snowflake and Your Company's Ecosystem
abstract:
Enterprises are investing heavily in AI, but many initiatives stall before production — not due to models or compute, but because of fragmented data, poor visibility, and weak access controls that undermine trust and compliance.

Snowflake accelerates AI by centralizing data and enabling scalable access, but this speed increases risk as sensitive data proliferates across environments.

Cyera addresses this gap as an AI-native data security control plane, providing full visibility, automated classification, risk prioritization and policy enforcement across Snowflake ecosystems — enabling organizations to scale AI with trusted, governed and secure data.

speakers.bio:
Amir Kafri is a Software Architect for Research, Data, and Analytics at Cyera, where he focuses on building data-driven systems and advancing modern data security and classification platforms.

Prior to Cyera, Amir held engineering leadership roles at Salesforce and Datorama, where he led teams working on large-scale data and analytics platforms.

Gal Pollak is a Senior Product Manager at Cyera, where she leads key initiatives within the company’s Data Security Posture Management platform. She focuses on building data-centric security solutions that help organizations understand, govern, and remediate risk across complex environments, specializing in Snowflake. Her work centers on turning deep technical challenges into intuitive, scalable products that security and data teams can operationalize with confidence.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Scaling Enterprise AI in a Multicloud Global Organization
abstract: Danaher, a global science and technology leader in life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology, operates through multiple independent companies worldwide. As the organization scaled, data became fragmented across clouds, regions and systems. To unlock enterprisewide intelligence, Danaher built a multicloud, multiregion enterprise data platform on Snowflake using a data product-centric approach. Delivered with Capgemini, the platform enables secure cross-company data sharing, unified governance and AI-driven insights at scale. Today, it supports financial optimization, commercial intelligence and advanced scientific analytics, accelerating AI adoption across the enterprise.

speakers.bio: I am the Delivery Architect Director at Capgemini Insights & Data Poland, specializing in data strategy, data platform development, and cloud-based analytics. With a strong background as both a developer and architect, I have led strategic initiatives across the telco, automotive, and logistics sectors, managing distributed teams in Germany, India, and other regions. Fluent in German and English, I work closely with clients as a lead architect. In addition, I serve as Chief Technology Officer at Capgemini Insights & Data Poland.

Eugene Kabanov is a Director of AI Infrastructure with deep experience in enterprise data platforms, analytics strategy, and operating AI at scale, bringing a long‑term practitioner’s perspective on running data platforms that support real business outcomes.

title: Scaling Smart: Optimization Framework Behind Samsung Ads DMP's 73% Cost Savings
abstract: Samsung Ads achieved a 73% cost reduction by implementing a structured optimization framework across compute, storage and workload governance. This session breaks down the methodology, monitoring mechanisms and cultural changes that sustained those gains — without sacrificing performance.

speakers.bio: Rahul Jain is an Engineering Leader with 14+ years building enterprise big data platforms and leading teams across AWS, Databricks, and Snowflake. He guides teams through the data engineering lifecycle while architecting data lakes and lakehouses processing billions of daily transactions.. He drives cloud migrations decreasing operational complexity and increasing scalability. His leadership enables cross-team platform adoption, automated infrastructure deployment, and strategic architecture evaluation. Rahul bridges technical depth with business strategy, achieving measurable ROI and operational excellence.

I am Data Engineer with over 11 years of experience building large-scale data platforms in the different industries including AdTech, Banking, Credit Bureau and Telecommunications. My specialization is in designing modern data architectures and robust data warehouses that power business intelligence using Snowflake, Apache Flink, Kafka, Python, Spark and cloud-native technologies. I am Passionate about data architecture and emerging AI capabilities. I am dedicated to building resilient data systems that drive next-generation analytics. I also passionate about automation, data modeling and mentoring the next generation of engineers.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: This session is currently open to Marketer's Forum registrants. Registration will open to the general event audience closer to the event.

title: SciPlay Accelerates Revenue Growth with a Unified AI Platform
abstract: SciPlay, a leader in social casino and casual gaming, modernized a fragmented data environment to accelerate player acquisition, engagement, monetization and retention. Through a zero-egress strategy, SciPlay consolidated its stack into the Snowflake AI Data Cloud. Using SnowConvert AI, they migrated from Databricks Spark to Snowflake Openflow for streaming and from Databricks ML to Snowflake ML, eliminating data hops and enabling governed MLOps with Model Registry. With 20+ AI agents deployed, SciPlay improved time to value, productivity and revenue efficiency to help offset rising user acquisition costs.

title: Secure by Default: Implementing Zero-Trust Identity and Access in Snowflake
abstract:
Managing static credentials and complex role hierarchies remains a leading cause of security incidents and operational fatigue. This 90-minute hands-on lab provides a deep dive into the next generation of Snowflake Identity and Access Management (IAM). Participants will gain hands-on experience with:

  • Secretless connectivity: Configure Workload Identity Federation (WIF) for service-to-service auth without managed keys.
  • Simplified access: Use new improvements to reduce role bloat and enforce least-privilege for AI workloads.
  • Automated posture: Use Trust Center to identify risks and apply one-click remedial actions.

speakers.bio: 20+ years experience in Networking, Crypto, and Security in startups to large enterprises. I drive cross-functional teams towards a unified product vision, helping expand Snowflake's adoption with a shared destiny model.

title: Security Benefit Achieves 4× Faster Legacy Migration with Snowflake and Coalesce
abstract: Security Benefit transformed its fragmented data stack by standardizing on Snowflake and Coalesce. Replacing legacy CDP pipelines built across AWS services, Python and SQL Server, the team rebuilt a critical process 75% faster, shrinking a two-month migration to two weeks. Engineers now test transformation approaches in 15-20 minutes instead of over a week, accelerating innovation while strengthening governance and lineage. Join this session to learn how Security Benefit modernized its pipelines, improved security with RBAC and dynamic masking, and built a scalable Snowflake foundation ready for AI.

speakers.bio: As the Director of Data Platform Architecture & Operations, Brittany focuses on empowering her team through architectural excellence and standardized best practices. She is leading Security Benefit’s migration from legacy data platforms, rebuilding months of logic into two-week sprints by establishing repeatable, high-velocity patterns. Her "governance-by-design" approach integrates automated RBAC and dynamic masking directly into the transformation layer, removing operational bottlenecks and enabling her team to innovate with confidence. By championing Snowflake-native execution with Coalesce, Brittany has slashed experimentation cycles by 99%, delivering a trusted, AI-ready foundation.

title: See the Light: Migrating ETL to Snowflake with SnowConvert AI
abstract: ETL migration is complex. In this hands-on lab, you'll migrate a real ETL pipeline to Snowflake using SnowConvert AI. We'll demonstrate an agentic workflow, automated analysis, code conversion, AI verification, plus data migration and validation — so you can see what to automate. You'll work in SnowConvert AI and leave with a migration checklist.

speakers.bio: With over 4 years of professional experience as a Software Engineer, I specialize in designing and implementing automated solutions, such as SnowConvert AI, for end-to-end migrations to Snowflake. My expertise spans the entire modernization lifecycle, from large-scale data migration and complex code conversion to the re-platforming of intricate ETL pipelines. I focus on building products that reduce manual friction and accelerate time-to-value for enterprises joining the AI Data Cloud.

Fernando is a Senior Product Manager, specialized in Tools and Applications, with over 10 years of experience in product development and 6 years specifically in code migration. He is leading the SnowConvert AI product strategy and roadmap to help Snowflake customers accelerate their adoption of the platform. With a degree in Software Engineering, Fernando spent five years working as a software developer before transitioning into product management.

title: Self-Healing Semantic Layers with Snowflake Cortex AI: How Sigma Built J.A.K.E.
abstract: Sigma's data platform team built J.A.K.E., a production-grade agent powered by Cortex AI that integrates directly into Slack and Sigma, letting teams interact with Snowflake in their everyday work environment. This session showcases a self-healing reference architecture that actively maintains dbt metadata and dimensional business matrices. By converting modeling frameworks into machine-executable logic, J.A.K.E. automatically generates Snowflake semantic views, eliminating weeks of manual effort. Attendees will see live AgenticOps examples: using Cortex AI to capture query failures, debug root causes in real time and automatically patch semantic definitions to create a continuous learning loop.

speakers.bio: Matt Senick is a Senior Analytics Engineer at Sigma Computing, building the data systems that power analytics and AI across the company, and using Sigma + Snowflake to do it. His career spans research, banking, aerospace, and SaaS at companies such as Sigma, 7shifts, StandardAero, and Affinity Credit Union. He also spent three years lecturing Empirical Economics at the University of Saskatchewan. Matt holds a Double Honours in Computer Science & Economics from the University of Saskatchewan and an Masters in Economics from the University of Toronto.

title: Sharing Data and AI on Snowflake Internal Marketplace
abstract: Join this lab for a hands-on experience of sharing, requesting and consuming data and AI assets within your organization. You will share and govern not only basic data products including Apache Iceberg but also semantic models, AI agents and other artifacts. This lab teaches you how to publish such assets on Internal Marketplace, define governance controls and request and gain access. You will also exercise the latest Internal Marketplace features such as custom data product attributes to tailor the marketplace experience to your requirements.

speakers.bio: Matthias is a Field CTO at Snowflake and serves as a trusted advisor to clients and partners worldwide. He has more than 25 years of experience in the design, engineering, and deployment of database and analytics technology. Matthias focuses on Enterprise Data Architecture, Data Collaboration, Data Governance, and he leads the Data Mesh field strategy at Snowflake. Prior to joining Snowflake Matthias worked for 21 years at IBM in the US and across Europe.

Active since 2001 as a data expert, focusing on delivering value through Data and Analytics solutions. Worked in roles in sales, development, implementation, and support, culminating in my position as a Principal Data Cloud Architect at Snowflake. Primary focus is to help businesses transform their data strategies into actionable insights, guiding organizations through the process of crafting strategic data roadmaps and architecting frameworks for data collaboration, ensuring seamless information flow and utilization in a governed manner. Industry experience spans Retail/Wholesale Banking, Insurance, Oil & Gas, Utilities, Energy, Public Sector, Telecommunication, and IT.

title: Siemens: Scaling Enterprise Data Sharing with Snowflake
abstract: At Siemens, we're building the future of industrial AI by enabling data sharing, discovery and consumption at scale. This session shares our journey in building the Siemens Data and AI Cloud, a unified data ecosystem powered by Snowflake with a data mesh architecture. We'll outline our strategic vision and technical foundations, explaining how federated data domains publish data as products for thousands of consumers to access trusted data. Learn how we democratize data access through the Siemens Data Cloud Marketplace, reducing time-to-value and transforming collaboration. We'll explore how this scalable, governed data-sharing approach accelerates industrial AI.

speakers.bio: Christian is Head of Platforms and has been with Siemens since 2017. He oversees the corporate data & AI platform ecosystem, known as the Siemens Data & AI Cloud with more than 170k users.

Passionate about building high-performing teams, data products, and platforms. With over two decades of experience in various technology roles. A firm believer in the power of scaling useful technology, have successfully led cross-functional teams across five countries, utilizing cloud technologies such as Snowflake to deliver over 100 impactful projects. These projects include cutting-edge machine learning initiatives and generative AI use cases. A strategic approach to aligning data initiatives with core business objectives has consistently produced significant results.

title: Smaller, Smarter, Cheaper: Custom LLMs Built in Snowflake
abstract: General-purpose LLMs are powerful but often expensive and not specialized for enterprise use. As AI scales, organizations need models that are cheaper, smaller and deeply aligned to proprietary data. In this session, we show you how to take advantage of comprehensive solutions for LLM customization, across all levels of complexity, directly in Snowflake. From APIs that simplify the workflow and provide easy experimentation loops to research-focused capabilities supporting reinforcement learning and advanced optimization, teams can start simple and evolve seamlessly, while ensuring they meet their quality, cost and latency goals.

speakers.bio: Vinay is a product manager on the ML Platform team. He's currently focused on enabling production-grade ML powered by Snowflake's Container Runtime. Prior to Snowflake he spent many years in the AI/ML platforms space leading product initiatives and teams across small, medium, and large enterprises.

title: Smarter Modernization: How SnowConvert AI Is Redefining Migration to Snowflake
abstract: Over the past year, SnowConvert AI has transformed from a code translation tool into a fully intelligent modernization engine. In this session, the SnowConvert AI team reveals what’s new: deeper AI integration, support for partial and incremental migrations, smarter schema mapping, and a completely reimagined user experience designed for speed and transparency. Learn how these innovations make migrations to Snowflake faster, more flexible and more predictable than ever before — helping organizations modernize with confidence and unlock the full power of the Snowflake Data Cloud.

speakers.bio: Federico Zoufaly helps Snowflake customers accelerate their adoption of the platform from his position as Director of Migrations and Modernizations @Snowflake. Federico previously served as Co-Founder  of Mobilize.Net. Federico has been a leader in modernizing software applications for almost 30 years.  With a technical background (Ph.D.c. from the University of Florida with a Fulbright scholarship and M.Sc. from the Technology Institute of Costa Rica) and hands-on management in real world technology projects, Federico is passionate about the intersection of the technical challenges and the business need for modernization. Federico is a fellow of the Aspen Global Leadership Network.

Venky Ganti is a member of the Snowflake product team helping customers unlock the full potential of data through the Snowflake Data+AI platform. His experience in data management and AI innovation positions him to drive impact within the Snowflake ecosystem. He co-founded Alation, pioneering the Data Catalog category as CTO, and led innovation at leading tech companies including Google, Microsoft, and early stage startups such as Numbers Station AI. His experience spans from foundational research in data systems and search to leading product development and engineering for AI applications. He is driven by a desire to help customers solve their most complex data and AI challenges.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Snowbash
abstract: Summit Reception Party

title: Snowflake-Hosted AI-Accelerated Analytics Catalog
abstract: Centene Corporation presents the Enterprise Data Catalog Streamlit App, a solution to centralize metadata and analytics assets from Snowflake, Alation and Power BI to deliver consistent, governed data discovery. Features include searchable assets, AI-generated descriptions, data lineage visualization and a dataset/report review system. The platform's key innovation is a semantic model accessible via a natural language chatbot, enabling users to easily and quickly find the right enterprise dataset or report. This transformation accelerates data discovery, fosters governance, and empowers confident, data-backed decision-making across Centene.

speakers.bio: Ramon Rivera is Vice President of Business Intelligence and Decision Intelligence, leading enterprise analytics and AI-ready data platforms in a highly regulated healthcare environment. With over 23 years of experience across operations analytics, BI, and data products, he is known for modernizing complex BI ecosystems into trusted, decision-centric platforms. His teams are advancing toward governed data products, semantic consistency, and marketplace-based analytics to support scalable, AI-driven decision making.

Jonathan Salmon is a Director of Enterprise Intelligence at Centene, a Fortune 25 healthcare company, where he leads enterprise intelligence initiatives and architects the Centene Data Store, the company’s enterprise metadata catalog and intelligence platform on Snowflake. 
His team is advancing semantic models, Cortex AI integrations, and agent-driven capabilities that shift BI from reactive reporting toward proactive, AI-enabled insight delivery. A builder at enterprise scale, he is focused on reducing complexity and creating the foundation for AI-powered analytics.

title: Snowflake Intelligence for Trading and Risk Management
abstract: Learn how ofi (Olam Food Ingredients) drives its decision-making processes by using Snowflake Intelligence agents. The agents have been built for sales, hedging and procurement insights, enabling teams to move from static reporting to proactive, insight-led decisions. These agents operate on top of historical, curated enterprise datasets combined with semantic data models, ensuring that insights are generated with consistent business context and shared definitions. As a result, they empower decision‑makers with timely, contextual and actionable insights — supporting better pricing and demand decisions in sales, more informed risk insights and improved optimization strategies in procurement.

speakers.bio: Strategic and visionary technology leader driving large‑scale transformations through enterprise data platforms, emerging technologies, and cultural change. Brings deep expertise in architecting and scaling modern data ecosystems, unifying regional landscapes into global data foundations, and embedding intelligence‑driven models that deliver measurable business value.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Snowflake Intelligence: Your Team's Most Complex 'Whys' at Your Fingertips
abstract: Snowflake Intelligence has evolved from conversational Q&A to an end-to-end insights platform. This session unveils capabilities reshaping how enterprises consume data. See how we move beyond "what happened" to answer complex "why" questions with root cause analysis for actionable insights. Explore our new visualization experience: charts you can save, subscribe to and share, replacing static dashboards with a seamless conversational workflow. We'll demo high-IQ features including zero-shot forecasting, anomaly detection with proactive alerts and memory for personalized answers. Whether you're a business user or data team, discover how SI is becoming the hub for enterprise intelligence.

speakers.bio: Effie is the Product Management leader for Snowflake Intelligence. She drives the vision for how business users discover insights, ask complex analytical questions, and take action on enterprise data through natural conversation. Before Snowflake, Effie built human-centered AI and product experiences across Apple, Google, Roblox, and Tavus, where she led work spanning device intelligence, Google Assistant interactions, expressive communication systems, and AI-powered digital humans. Effie is a recognized speaker on the intersection of AI and human experience, having delivered keynotes at BUILD London and represented leading technology companies at industry events worldwide.

Note: This session is currently open to Marketer's Forum registrants. Registration will open to the general event audience closer to the event.

title: Snow in San Diego: Operational Efficiency and Passenger Safety at SAN
abstract: San Diego International Airport (SAN) is partnering with Snowflake to architect the next generation of Fleet Intelligence, turning high-volume telemetry from aircraft and ground equipment into actionable foresight. Beyond monitoring the present, SAN applies time series and geospatial analytics to fuse real-time data with forward-looking flight operations. For this busy, single-runway airport, the shift is from reactive tracking to predictive resource orchestration. Learn how SAN uses this pilot to surface bottlenecks before they occur, optimize asset allocation and keep passenger flow smooth — transforming raw, third-party enriched data into operational insight in minutes at scale.

speakers.company: San Diego Co Regional Airport Authority
speakers.bio: Andre Bruckner is the Director of Business Analytics at the San Diego Airport Authority. Since a 2008 PETCO internship, his data journey has spanned consumer goods to automotive exhausts. A Snowflake fan since 2018, Andre is building BI from the ground up for America's busiest single-runway airport. He leads enterprise data strategy to transform fragmented aviation systems into actionable intelligence. Off the clock, Andre is an AT-AT pilot in training and an elaborate Christmas light architect.

Daria Rostovtseva is a Principal Sales Engineer at Snowflake. In her role, she helps state and local government customers solve complex business problems through data. Prior to joining Snowflake in 2021, she held multiple consulting and solution engineering roles at the SAS Institute, UnitedHealth Group, and the University of California San Francisco. Daria holds Master’s degrees from University of California, Davis, and Capella University.

title: Sony Interactive’s Blueprint for Unified Data and Catalog Federation at Scale
abstract: Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE) is defining a new standard for technical leadership by embracing the open, interoperable data ecosystem. This visionary session frames catalog federation not as a future goal, but as a present necessity. SIE will detail the strategic architecture that leverages Snowflake’s Iceberg tables and Horizon Catalog-linked databases to ensure every table — regardless of its open format — is universally discoverable and accessible by all compute engines, with an emphasis on advanced security and governance. This comprehensive blueprint provides technical leaders with a clear, actionable path to deliver an open, innovative data platform at scale.

speakers.job_title: Engineering Leader, Data and AI Platform
speakers.bio:
Preet Kaur is an Engineering Leader at Sony Interactive Entertainment shaping enterprise data strategy for PlayStation at global scale. She leads global engineering teams responsible for the end to end lifecycle of data from generation to AI activation, operating at terabyte to petabyte scale.

Preet partners across product, data, and AI organizations to transform complex data ecosystems into AI ready platforms that power real time insight, and intelligent player experiences for millions worldwide. She is passionate about building resilient AI ready data foundations and high performing engineering teams that turn data into strategic advantage.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall B)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Streaming into Interactive Tables with Interactive Warehouses
abstract: In this session, you will learn how to build a real-time streaming solution based on Interactive tables and Interactive warehouses. You will develop and deploy an end-to-end solution using Python, Snowpipe Streaming High-Performance Architecture and other data engineering features, which ingests data, transforms it and queries in seconds.

speakers.bio: With over 20 years of experience in engineering and leadership, Brad has designed and built countless applications leveraging huge datasets at high concurrency. At Twilio, Brad helped to deliver more than 3B emails on peak days and stored over 3B contacts for customers to drive marketing campaigns. Previous to Twilio, he designed and built solutions for SendGrid, Under Armour, MapMyFitness, Garmin, and others. Brad is recognized as an industry expert in columnar/kv datastores and developing highly-scalable/efficient architectures around them.

speakers.job_title: Principal Data Platform Architect, Applied Field Engineering
speakers.bio: Keith is a Principal Architect on the Applied Field Engineering team at Snowflake, specializing in Data Engineering.  Before joining Snowflake, Keith led the Data Engineering practice at an analytics consultancy, co-founded a tech startup, and held various consulting and software development roles. He holds an MBA from Georgia Tech.  Residing in St. Petersburg, Florida with his wife and two children, Keith enjoys boating, fishing, camping, and traveling.

title: Structured Data Sharing for Government: From Secure Sharing to Clean Rooms
abstract: In Massachusetts state government, cross-agency analytics is as much a governance and legal challenge as a data one. This session shows how ODIA is enabling structured data sharing across agencies keeping data under agency stewardship while supporting enterprise insights. We’ll cover a practical progression on Snowflake: secure internal data sharing, discovery and reuse via listings, and purpose-bound collaboration with Clean Rooms for aggregate-only analysis. You’ll leave with a repeatable pattern for safer, auditable, revocable cross-agency analytics without centralizing sensitive data.

speakers.bio: Proven data executive with a strong track record of delivering measurable business outcomes through enterprise data strategy, cloud modernization, and AI-driven enablement. Deep expertise in data architecture, governance, metadata, and entitlements, with experience leading teams across healthcare, insurance, financial services, SaaS, and public sector clients. Trusted advisor to C-level stakeholders with a consultative approach to solving complex enterprise data challenges. Thought leader and public speaker, with a forward-looking approach to building practices in emerging technology ecosystems.

speakers.company: Commonwealth of Massachusetts - Executive Office of Technology Services and Security
speakers.bio: Enterprise data, analytics, and AI leader with 20+ years of experience building and scaling secure, governed analytics ecosystems across multiple industries such as healthcare and the public sector.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Tableau and Snowflake: Demystifying the Open Semantic Interchange
abstract: Explore how Tableau and the Open Semantic Interchange (OSI) provide the essential metadata layer that ensures your metrics remain consistent and trusted for both humans and agents. You will learn how our current analytics integration and future roadmap for semantic interoperability allow agents to work seamlessly inside the systems that run your business.

speakers.bio: Tara Morton is a Product Manager, with prior experience as a Services Practices Leader and a Solution Architect. Tara is a fun-loving data geek who finds joy in helping people get to the root of the problem they want to address. She draws from her academic background to design experiments and identify KPIs to address each person's most pressing business questions in a delightful, visually appealing way. When she's not tweaking models in a viz or counting the poses 'til savasana, you can find her spinning fire with her husband on stage.

Erik Teutsch is a Distinguished Solution Engineer at Tableau and lifelong data nerd, having worked at an early database startup in the 1980s. As a 14.5 year veteran of Tableau, Erik spends much of his work time advising customers on everything from data visualization best practices to data governance for the largest strategic accounts at Salesforce. Outside work Erik can be found in swimming pools, on bicycles or in the kitchen with his wife, often with a glass of wine in hand.

title: TF1 and Netflix: Deploy ML Models in a Data Clean Room to Reinvent Media Collab
abstract: Learn how TF1 and Netflix are building a data clean room on Snowflake to collaborate on audience insights without exposing underlying data. We’ll walk through the end-to-end architecture, from data residency to deploying ML-trained models inside the clean room. You’ll see how strict governance, code validation and cost controls enable privacy-safe activation and measurement across broadcaster and streaming workloads.

speakers.bio: François-Xavier Pierrel has been Chief Data & Adtech Officer at TF1 Group (#1 Broadcaster in Europe) since 2023. A CELSA graduate (La Sorbonne University) and digital transformation expert, he leads the adtech, data and AI strategy for TF1+, TF1 Pub, and TF1Info. His career includes key roles at Meta, Microsoft, and Renault. As the first Chief Data Officer at JCDecaux, he orchestrated data solutions across 40 countries, earning a spot in the 2023 "Global Top 100 CDOs." Specializing in adtech and martech, he places innovation and data analytics at the heart of business growth and customer experience optimization.

Note: This session is currently open to Marketer's Forum registrants. Registration will open to the general event audience closer to the event.

title: The Age of Data Exhaustion
abstract:
No one talks about it, but data teams are tired. They're tired of managing thousands of tables, brittle transformation logic and AI pilots built on foundations no one fully trusts. The problem isn’t data volume. It’s overload. Code overload demands disciplined transformation. Data overload demands intelligent cataloging. And chaos erodes trust.

In this session, we’ll show how modern data teams restore order with a metadata-driven data operating layer that standardizes transformation, connects governance to build-time and makes data quality proactive instead of reactive. Because AI doesn’t fix disorder, it amplifies it.

speakers.bio: Michael Tantrum is Director of Sales Engineering at Coalesce and a 30-year veteran of data warehousing and analytics architecture. He has helped organizations navigate every major shift in the industry—from legacy enterprise warehouses to modern cloud platforms powering analytics and AI. Today he works with data platform leaders to accelerate Snowflake adoption using automation and modern engineering practices. Michael lives in the Pacific Northwest where he enjoys woodworking and furniture making.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: The Art of Data Relationship Chemistry for Global Financial Services
abstract: Global financial services firms operate where regulation, capital and trust collide. For private equity and their investors, unlocking tax value depends on understanding relationships across entities, partners, deals and jurisdictions — yet the data is often trapped in spreadsheets. In this session, we explore how a PE-backed global firm is rebuilding its tax data foundation on Snowflake, turning fragmented records into a connected, insight-driven platform that unlocks transparency, allows risk analysis to drive decisions and cuts review hours in half.

speakers.bio: Michael Tran is a Senior Manager in EY's Tax Technology & Transformation practice, focused on data and technology strategy for tax operations in financial services. He works on cloud-based platforms that pull together fragmented tax data, improve governance, and lay the groundwork for automation, analytics, and AI. Recently, he has been connecting structured tax data with knowledge graphs to move beyond aggregation toward contextual insight and practical decision support. His view: better tax outcomes start with better data.

speakers.job_title: Partner, Future of Work,  AI Leader
speakers.bio:
Claire is a Partner in Ernst & Young LLP's Financial Services practice with more than 15 years of experience in the financial services industry. She is a Global Tax Account Leader and Transformation Leader on some of EY's largest and most complex client accounts.

She also the creator of EY's Future of Work modern managed services delivery platform and a leader of our Financial Services Al & Data team specializing in knowledge retention and process mining.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: The Blueprint for Building SCM Intelligence for Perishable Business on Snowflake
