abstract: Dominating 70% of the U.S. tofu market, Pulmuone unified more than 20,000 products by migrating fragmented ERP, SCM and WMS data to Snowflake. This session reveals how it achieved a real-time integrated view, syncing production and sales for instant S&OP analysis. By leveraging Snowflake, Pulmuone streamlined the entire supply chain — from manufacturing to CS. With AI-driven demand forecasting and trend analysis, the company reduced SCM costs by about 25%, boosted analyst productivity by 50% and improved forecast accuracy by 20%. Learn how Pulmuone transformed a complex global operation into a lean, data-driven engine, proving that Snowflake is the ultimate ingredient for scalable business growth.

speakers.bio: He is currently serving as the Head of SCM at Pulmuone, following my tenure as CIO of Kolmar and a consulting career at Accenture.

title: The Builder’s Guide to Sigma AI Apps: Workflows, Cortex Agents and AI Functions
abstract:
What separates an AI app from a chatbot? Action tools that write data, control the UI and send notifications. This lab teaches data professionals to build code-free agentic workflows.

Step into the shoes of a Sigma AI app developer to build a "Sales Pipeline Analyzer" similar to the one that Sigma uses internally. You will orchestrate Snowflake Cortex Agents to parse unstructured transcripts and extract insights like deal health and risks.

Using Cortex AI Functions and Sigma's native writeback, you will land newly structured data securely in your warehouse. Finally, configure action tools to escalate at-risk deals via email and dynamically reconfigure the dashboard.

title: The Converged Enterprise: Closing the Gap Between SAP Ops and AI Intelligence
abstract:
SAP enterprises have invested billions in operational data. Most cannot activate that data for AI at scale, not because the technology is missing, but because two organizations own the problem and neither owns the seam between them.

In this session, we explore why the COO/CFO and CIO/CTO divide is the real barrier to enterprise AI returns, and how the convergence of SAP Business Data Cloud and the Snowflake AI Data Cloud creates both the technical foundation and the organizational window to close it.

Attendees will leave with a framework for unified governance, a model for co-funded AI data programs and a clear view of the cost of waiting.

speakers.job_title: Head of SAP Hybrid Cloud Analytics
speakers.bio: As the Practice Director for SAP Delivery at Hakkoda, Matt Florian leads the charge for clients integrating SAP-focused analytics within Snowflake solutions. With over three decades of experience, Florian has been instrumental in guiding enterprises toward adopting analytics and evolving into data-informed leaders. His recent efforts have concentrated on guiding global enterprises through their SAP to Snowflake data analytics transitions.

title: The Evolving SaaS Data Platform: How AI Agents Are Consolidating Intelligence
abstract: As enterprises accelerate AI adoption, the modern data stack is shifting from fragmented point tools to intelligence-driven platforms. AI agents are consolidating workflows that once required separate solutions for observability, profiling, lineage and governance — enabling faster decisions and automated remediation. In this session, we explore how Snowflake’s AI-native platform accelerates this shift and how Acceldata’s agentic data management (ADM) provides the governed automation layer that unifies trust, observability and action on top of Snowflake — scaling reliable, decision-ready data operation.

speakers.bio: Cam Davie is an experienced data and analytics professional with over 25 years experience in Analytics and Data Management. He specializes in helping customers and partners with their needs for analyzing information, developing insightful reports, and creating compelling visualizations to uncover key trends and drive strategic decisions. Cam is passionate about working with customers and partners to leverage data to solve business challenges and foster a data-informed culture within organizations. His expertise helps teams understand and identify opportunities to improve and trust data through enhanced data quality and data reliability.​​​​​​​

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: The Financial Services AI, Apps and Data Guide with Snowflake
abstract: Data sharing has evolved from simple access to the essential fuel for autonomous execution. The new mandate is "data for agency" — transforming static shares into the intelligence that powers agentic workflows. This session brings to life Snowflake’s AI Data Cloud for Financial Services, moving beyond retrieval to delivering AI-ready data products and governed semantic views. Hear how industry leaders tap into this ecosystem to drive operational leverage and measurable outcomes. Learn how Snowflake enables institutions to securely share the contextualized data required to accelerate agent-driven use cases across banking, insurance, capital markets and payments.

speakers.job_title: Head of AI for Financial Services
speakers.bio:
John Heisler leads AI for Financial Services at Snowflake, driving how financial institutions deploy production-grade AI.

His experience spans financial services, neuroscience, data engineering, data warehousing, and AI, shaping a multidisciplinary approach to building intelligent systems.

Prior to Snowflake, John led a data science and engineering team at a buy-side financial services data consultancy. He then worked in Snowflake’s Applied Field Engineering team, where he architected AI solutions for leading financial institutions.

John partners with business and technology leadership to implement trusted, enterprise-ready AI that delivers value at speed.

title: The Future of Financial Services: Agents with Industry Context and Connectivity
abstract: Financial services is entering a new operating era, one where AI is embedded into core workflows, grounded in industry context, governed by enterprise controls and securely connected across systems. The focus has shifted from pilots to execution at scale. In this keynote, leading institutions will share how they are deploying agentic AI across front-office decisions, risk oversight, compliance and operations to deliver real-time, measurable impact. Join industry innovators as they explore how context-aware, connected AI is redefining how financial services organizations operate, and why becoming agent-native is the next strategic imperative.

speakers.bio: Rinesh Patel is the Global Head of Financial Service at Snowflake where he is responsible for developing the financial service value proposition and supporting our customers' transition to an enterprise data & AI strategy in cloud.  Rinesh has been in the financial services industry for over 20 years in a variety of banking, consulting and business leadership roles with his most recent experience as Global Head of Cloud Platform at London Stock Exchange Company where he established and led a commercially-minded platform team to develop a modern cloud data platform to deliver new data solutions. Rinesh is based in London where he lives with his wife and two daughters.

title: The Future of HCLS: Modern Strategies for Interoperability and Agentic AI
abstract:
Navigating the next wave of interoperability and AI disruption across healthcare and life sciences means organizations need to ensure they are starting with the right foundational strategy, prioritizing the right use cases and demonstrating tangible ROI.
Join the healthcare and life sciences keynote at Summit 2026 to hear directly from industry innovators on how they are partnering with Snowflake to create the right strategy to tap into their multimodal data, accelerate interoperability, unlock enterprise AI and solve their most critical business challenges. We’ll discuss the latest Snowflake announcements and what they mean for the industry and show a live demo of Snowflake Intelligence.

speakers.bio: Arun is a seasoned data strategist and technical leader in life sciences. At Regeneron, he builds interoperable ecosystems that unify Sales, Marketing, and Market Access data across the US, Europe, and Asia, creating a single source of truth from early development through post‑launch. Previously at Medtronic and Amgen, he scaled analytics across the full product lifecycle. A champion of the Snowflake AI Data Cloud and Agentic AI, he leads the operationalization of autonomous data agents to deliver real‑time insights, robust governance, and AI‑ready, compliant assets at global scale, accelerating the path from data to action.

Jeff Morrison is a healthcare technology leader passionate about innovating with data to enhance decision-making and customer interactions. At Northeast Georgia Health System, he drives operational efficiency by transforming complex datasets into actionable insights that improve patient and provider experiences. With a keen eye for detail, Jeff proactively solves business and technical challenges, streamlining workflows and developing scalable solutions. His expertise in data-driven innovation empowers organizations to navigate the complexities of modern healthcare and optimize performance through smarter, more efficient use of information.

speakers.job_title: RVP, Global Head of HCLS GTM
speakers.bio: Jesse Cugliotta is the Global Industry Lead for Healthcare & Life Sciences at Snowflake. He has over 20 years of experience working with clients in the Healthcare & Life Sciences industries with a focus on driving business outcomes via data analytics & AI. He holds a graduate degree in Engineering from the University of Pennsylvania and has spent his entire career in Data & Analytics.

title: The Future of Manufacturing: Enabling Data Agents with IT, OT and IoT Data
abstract: The manufacturing industry has reached an inflection point, when AI agents can reason across plant-floor telemetry, maintenance logs and ERP systems to predict equipment failures and manage supply chain disruptions. Yet, how do organizations build a data foundation that enables agents to operate securely and autonomously across the enterprise? Join the manufacturing keynote at Snowflake Summit to learn more about the practical implementation of agentic workflows. Hear from industry experts and pioneering manufacturers, moving beyond pilot projects to deploy production agents that drive business results. This event outlines the strategic roadmap to prepare for the agentic future in manufacturing.

speakers.bio: In his current role, Tony Leopold is responsible for technology, strategy, and innovation at United Rentals.  He joined United Rentals in 2010 and has held a variety of leadership roles in business development, merger integration, operational excellence, and strategic projects. Mr. Leopold also has held roles as SVP of US Products at Elevate Credit, Inc., and Manager at Bain & Company where he began his career in 1999. Mr. Leopold holds a BBA in Finance from Texas A&M University and a Master of Arts in Philosophy, Politics, and Economics from Oxford University.

Chief Data & Innovation Officer with global automotive and mobility experience, leading enterprise-scale data, AI, and digital transformation across a Fortune-level OEM. 
Proven track record of building data platforms, enabling software-defined vehicles, operationalizing AI, and driving measurable cost, efficiency, and revenue impact. Trusted executive partner to C-suite and senior leadership, bridging technology, product, and business strategy. 
Experienced in scaling teams, modernizing operating models, and delivering transformation in complex, regulated environments.
Board member of the Chamber of Commerce of Nashville and Distinguished Adjunct Professor at Vanderbilt University

Tim Long is the Global Head of Manufacturing in Snowflake's go-to-market team where he leads Manufacturing customers through data and AI transformations while fostering industry partnerships to expedite customer success. Before joining Snowflake, Tim was the Head of Data and Analytics at adidas North America and Head of Analytics and Data at Micron Technology, where he led the global manufacturing data environment to Snowflake in just four months. Tim holds a Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Wyoming and  a Master's degree in Business Analytics from NYU Stern.

Ross Schalmo is the Chief Data and AI Officer at Eaton. Over the past four years, he has led Eaton's digitalization transformation efforts through a comprehensive enterprise data and AI strategy, inclusive of the establishment of a global Data Governance program, as well as implementing the company's first cloud-based data lakehouse. Under his leadership, data and AI programs in Eaton yielded a collective $200MM+ in business benefits in 2025 across Sales, Engineering, Manufacturing, Supply Chain and Service disciplines. His organization owns Master Data Management, data integration, storage, BI, advanced analytics, and generative and AI/ML data science, platforms, and governance.

title: The Future of Marketing: Turning Big Moments into Measurable ROI
abstract: Super Bowl. Olympics. Global stages where millions are invested — and impact is notoriously hard to prove. In this session, hear how Viral Nation uses Snowflake to measure real-time Super Bowl performance through social sentiment and “viral velocity,” and how Snowflake’s own marketing team quantifies the long-term brand impact of its Olympic partnership. Discover how AI in the Snowflake AI Data Cloud turns high-stakes moments into measurable outcomes — transforming prestige, attention and sponsorships into defensible ROI.

speakers.bio: Erin Foxworthy serves as the Global GTM Industry Lead for Marketing & Advertising at Snowflake.  Previous to Snowflake, Erin was the EVP of Partnerships and Innovation at Horizon Media.   Her role focused on guiding marketers as they transformed their organizations by unlocking data driven marketing technologies with unprecedented consumer insights.   Prior to Horizon Media, Erin was the Global Category Development lead at Microsoft Advertising, bridging product, marketing, and sales across the Microsoft Advertising Ecosystem.

Richelle is the EVP of Business Intelligence and Data Strategy at Viral Nation, where she leads the company’s vision for how data is collected, measured and transformed into strategic insight. With more than 12 years of experience across analytics, research and predictive modeling, she has built deep expertise in solving one of marketing’s toughest challenges: measuring the hard-to-measure.

Her career spans primary and secondary research, attribution modeling, forecasting and advanced analytics. Richelle has pioneered new approaches in influencer, creator and social measurement, creating proprietary frameworks that move beyond legacy metrics to quantify cultural relevance, organic impact and creative effectiveness. Her methodologies help global brands understand the true value of social ecosystems in ways traditional measurement often overlooks.

In her role at Viral Nation, Richelle leads the integration of Business Intelligence (BI) and Artificial Intelligence (AI), transforming how data is tracked, analyzed and operationalized. She develops AI-powered systems that streamline measurement pipelines, enhance predictive accuracy and deliver real-time, adaptive intelligence to support strategic decision-making across marketing and communications.

Known for her rigor, curiosity and forward-looking mindset, Richelle continues to push the boundaries of what modern measurement can be. She is committed to building analytics capabilities that bring clarity to a fragmented digital world and help organizations unlock higher levels of performance and precision.

With experience in big data, data science, and machine learning Dash Desai is able to apply 18+ years of full-stack, hands-on software and data engineering skills to help build solutions that solve business problems and surface trends that shape markets in new ways than imagined before. As a Senior Lead Developer Advocate at Snowflake, he is passionate about evaluating new ideas, trends, and helping articulate how technology can address a given business problem.

Dash Desai has worked for global enterprises and in agile environments–for tech startups in the Bay Area in varying verticals, such as VoIP, Social Gaming, Digital Health, NoSQL database, and Data Cloud platforms.

Note: This session is currently open to Marketer's Forum registrants. Registration will open to the general event audience closer to the event.

title: The Future of Media: Composable Solutions for People and Agents
abstract: The media, entertainment and marketing industries are entering a new era shaped by AI agents, real-time audience personalization and intelligence, and composable data and app ecosystems. Navigating this disruption requires a strong data foundation, a focused AI strategy, and clarity about use cases that will deliver the highest measurable ROI for your business. Join Dennis Buchheim at the media, entertainment and marketing technology keynote at Snowflake Summit 2026 to see how leading organizations are working with Snowflake and partners to modernize data platforms, power advanced advertising and media experiences, and unlock new revenue opportunities with agentic AI.

speakers.job_title: RVP & Global Industry GTM Lead, ACME
speakers.bio: Dennis leads Snowflake’s go-to-market efforts for ad/martech, communications, media, and entertainment. Over his 20+ years as a product-oriented leader in digital ads/media, he's led teams that developed DSPs; ad exchanges; identity, targeting, and measurement platforms; industry protocols; and more. Prior to Snowflake, Dennis founded advisory firm ThinkMedium, was VP of Advertising Ecosystem at Meta, was CEO of ad standards org IAB Tech Lab, and was a product exec at Yahoo and Microsoft. Before “finding advertising”, Dennis held engineering and product roles at Apple, Oracle, Actioneer, and iHarvest (a Pinterest predecessor he co-founded). Dennis graduated from Brown University.

Michael Jones is a Senior Director of Software Engineering at Warner Music Group leading the Data and Insights organization responsible for ML/AI, data engineering and infrastructure, and data serving for all of WMG. He has a background in software engineering and has extensive experience leading engineering teams across a variety of domains including digital ads, cloud, and unmanned systems.

Sherene Hilal is SiriusXM’s Chief Ads Product Officer, overseeing a global cross-functional team of engineers, product managers, UX designers, product marketers, and operations leads across SiriusXM and AdsWizz. Under Sherene’s leadership, her team is united in delivering the most effective, highest-performing, and engaging digital audio ad experiences, making audio ads easily accessible for advertisers to try, buy, and learn from the format, ensuring that audio is a part of every advertiser’s media plan. 

Previously, Sherene has held influential roles across technology and advertising, most recently as Chief Product Officer at Bluecore.

Eric is the VP of Product Management at The Trade Desk, where he leads core workflows, OpenTTD, and the evolution of agentic AI. A seasoned leader with 25+ years of experience across media, commerce, and ad-tech, Eric has consistently driven digital transformation at scale.

Prior to his current role, he was Chief Product Officer at Profitero (a Publicis company) and CPO at Nielsen, where he launched Nielsen ONE. His leadership history includes executive roles at TripAdvisor, SmarterTravel, and ChoiceStream, as well as his foundational work at AOL as the co-creator of AOL Instant Messenger.

title: The Future of Public Sector: Scaling AI for Mission Impact
abstract: The AI mandate is now a defining priority for the public sector. The technology is reshaping how agencies approach their most critical priorities — from enhancing citizen services to improving security postures to increasing government efficiencies. But implementing AI requires a unified data foundation, clear strategy and alignment to measurable outcomes. Join the public sector keynote to hear how leaders are modernizing legacy systems, unifying data and embedding AI into critical workflows. We'll highlight the latest Snowflake innovations, showcase real customer success stories and outline a blueprint for how organizations can leverage AI today.

speakers.job_title: Vice President, US Public Sector Sales
speakers.bio: Jennifer Chronis is Snowflake’s Vice President of US Public Sector Sales, bringing 25 years of IT executive experience from VMware/Broadcom, Verizon, Amazon Web Services and IBM. As VP of US PubSec, she is responsible for enabling secure data collaboration and unlocking the power of AI across federal, state, local, and education government sales. She is a two-time Wash100 and FedScoop 50 award winner. Prior to her corporate career, Jennifer was a distinguished US Army officer for 21 years, retiring as Lieutenant Colonel, with active duty during combat operations in Desert Shield/Storm and Enduring Freedom. She is a graduate of the University of Virginia's McIntire School of Commerce.

title: The Future of Retail and Travel: Architecting for the Agentic Era with Snowflake
abstract: Agentic commerce is the next wave of AI disruption in retail and travel that demands organizations to start with a solid technology foundation and focus on the right priorities. Join this retail and travel industry keynote at Snowflake Summit to hear directly from the leading innovators on how they leverage Snowflake and our partner ecosystem to solve business challenges, accelerate technical use cases and unlock the power of enterprise agentic AI. Whether you’re a business executive, a technology leader or a Snowflake practitioner, this session is a must-attend for your Snowflake Summit experience.

speakers.job_title: Sr. Director - Digital Consumer Data, AI
Regional Vice President, Global Head of Retail & Travel
speakers.bio: Rosemary DeAragon is the Regional Vice President, Global Head of Retail & Travel at Snowflake where she oversees the Retail, Consumer, Travel, & Hospitality verticals. She joined Snowflake after a five-year tenure at Walmart, where she led data strategy for the Chief Data Officer. Prior to Walmart, Rosemary worked in the transactions machine learning team at Amazon and was one of the first employees at InfoScout (Numerator). Outside of work, Rosemary has served on the Board for several AI companies. She holds degrees in Political Science and Business Administration from the University of California, Berkeley’s Haas School of Business where she serves as a Lecturer in Big Data Ethics.

title: The Future of Telecom: Agentic Workflows on Intelligent Infrastructure
abstract: AI-driven automation, decision intelligence, cloud/edge computing and open architectures are transforming telecom networks into intelligent infrastructure — shifting connectivity from a cost center to a value platform. Navigating this requires a unified data foundation, a clear AI strategy and high-value use cases with measurable ROI. See how telcos unify network, OSS/BSS and enterprise data in the Snowflake AI Data Cloud. Learn how to reduce cost, monetize connectivity, hit sustainability targets, speed up rollout, improve resilience and enhance customer experience. Discover how Snowflake transforms network operations, optimizes investments and powers agentic workflows.

speakers.bio: Joji Philip is the Global Industry GTM Lead for the Communications Vertical at Snowflake, helping telecom leaders build data and AI foundations for intelligent, resilient infrastructure. With 20+ years spanning wireless engineering, network analytics, customer experience, cloud transformation, and AI product strategy, he connects business and technology to turn network data into actionable insight. Before Snowflake, he spent 8+ years at Ericsson, including as Director of AI/ML Products, leading AI-driven solutions in network planning, optimization, digital twins, and automation for Tier-1 operators and enterprises.

title: The Healthcare and Life Sciences Executive Panel
abstract: Join this candid conversation with healthcare and life sciences innovators and leaders as they discuss how they are leveraging the power of data and AI to address critical industry challenges and enable better downstream healthcare outcomes. This panel session will explore approaches to modernizing your data estate, preparing for AI and unleashing the power of enterprise agents to accelerate key healthcare and life sciences use cases.

speakers.bio: Tina Esposito is Senior Vice President, Chief Data Officer at Advocate Health.  Tina and her team are responsible for enterprise data strategies, measurement, and analytics in support of improved patient outcomes and organizational performance.  Tina has served in numerous national governance and advisory roles including as an appointed member of the federal Office of the National Coordinator’s HIT Advisory Committee.  Tina has both authored and has been featured in numerous industry publications.  Tina has a masters in business administration degree from Depaul University Chicago and a Bachelor of Science degree in Health Information Management from the University of Illinois at Chicago.

Brooke resides in Bentonville, AR and after spending time at EMC and ServiceNow, she has been leading various sales teams across Snowflake for the last 7 years. She is excited to now lead the US Strategics Sales Teams for HCLS and loves helping Snowflake's customers drive tremendous value by transforming their data strategy with the help of Snowflake's partner ecosystem.

Richard Fisher is Chief Data Architect in the Chief Data Office at UnitedHealth Group, where he sets enterprise data architecture strategy to enable data mesh, interoperable data products, and AI at scale. With over two decades of experience across healthcare and other highly regulated industries, Richard is a recognized voice in building trusted, federated data foundations on modern platforms such as Snowflake and Apache Iceberg. His work modernizes complex, regulated data estates into secure, AI‑ready ecosystems, thereby accelerating insight, enabling responsible AI, and translating data strategy into meaningful, real‑world healthcare impact.

title: The Infrastructure Behind Grindr’s AI Transformation
abstract: Grindr serves 15 million monthly active users across matching, insights, recommendations and safety — all powered by AI. Getting there required one key infrastructure decision: computing features at inference time, not in advance. In this session, Grindr's CPO, AJ Balance, and Chalk's CEO, Marc Freed-Finnegan, break down the Snowflake and Chalk stack that’s powering Grindr’s AI (aka gAI pronounced “gay-eye”) and what it means to ship real AI products at consumer scale for a global community of tens of millions.

speakers.bio: Austin (AJ) Balance is Chief Product Officer of Grindr, Inc., a role he has held since December 2021. Previously, he was Lead Product Manager of the Driving Team at Uber Technologies (2016–2019) and co-Founder and CEO of Dispatcher, Inc., a logistics platform for truckers and freight shippers (2013–2016). Earlier roles include Product Manager at Gigwalk (2011–2013) and Corporate Strategy Analyst at The Walt Disney Company (2009–2011). Mr. Balance holds an MBA and a bachelor's degree from Stanford University.

Marc Freed-Finnegan is the co-founder and CEO of Chalk, the data platform for AI inference. Previously, he co-founded Index, which was acquired by Stripe in 2018 and became Stripe Terminal, Stripe’s in-person payments platform. Earlier, Marc spent many years at Google, where he helped launch the first version of Google Wallet and worked on strategic partnerships for early-stage mobile products. He began his career in investment banking and holds a BA in Political Science from Yale University.

title: The Latest AI Tools on Snowflake Marketplace: Data Agents and Beyond
abstract: Discover how marketplace data becomes an agentic offering through data agents that enable natural language interaction. Learn to enrich agents with third-party expertise using Snowflake Cortex Knowledge Extensions, leverage shared semantic views that make complex data instantly queryable, and preview MCP server sharing for connecting AI systems to marketplace data. See how to deploy prebuilt solutions from leading providers that transform static data sets into intelligent, interactive tools.

speakers.bio: Ben Srour is a Product Lead at Snowflake, where he contributes to key areas of the company's AI product portfolio, including AI Marketplace and AI Research. Before joining Snowflake, Ben was a Group Product Manager at YouTube for 8 years, leading growth initiatives that drove +60-100M DAU annually. He previously spent 11 years at Microsoft shipping products across Windows, HoloLens, and Phone. He holds a BS in Computer Science from the University of Wisconsin-Madison.

title: The Optima Vision: Making it Impossible to Run Inefficiently on Snowflake
abstract: Snowflake Optima is our new optimization engine that represents a paradigm shift in effortless performance. It leverages deep intelligence, automatically analyzes your workload history and delivers intelligent optimizations that automatically accelerate performance. The result: industry-leading performance, made even simpler. This session dives deep into how Optima automatically optimizes your workloads and announces exciting new Optima features.

speakers.bio: Tim Sander is a Product Manager at Snowflake. He focuses on workload optimization, which includes features such as Automatic Clustering and Query Acceleration. Prior to Snowflake, Tim was a Product Manager at Microsoft, working on query engine and indexing features for Azure Cosmos DB. He has a degree in Systems Engineering from the University of Virginia.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 2 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: The Rise of AI Apps: Turning Snowflake Data into Intelligent Workflows
abstract: AI is rapidly evolving from chat interfaces to intelligent applications that automate business decisions. In this session, explore the emerging category of AI apps—applications that combine LLM reasoning, governed Snowflake data, and business workflows to help teams analyze, decide and act faster. Learn how organizations are embedding AI into planning, operations and analytics so business users can interact with trusted data using natural language and structured workflows. Through product examples and real-world use cases, see how Snowflake and Sigma enable a new generation of AI-driven applications built for real business impact.

title: The Snowflake Well-Architected Framework: Designing Efficiency
abstract: A well-architected Snowflake environment balances agility, performance, security and cost efficiency. This session introduces the Snowflake Well-Architected Framework, outlining core design pillars, lenses and implementation patterns. Learn how to assess your environment and apply prescriptive guidance to drive sustainable platform excellence.

speakers.bio: Zaki Bajwa is Head of Industry & Platforms Architects at Snowflake, partnering with leading firms to architect solutions for modern business challenges. Bringing two decades of GTM leadership (Stripe, ServiceNow, VMware), he aligns Line of Business needs with a modern architecture vision, driving Data and AI innovation for strategic customers. A proud New Yorker, he ensures strategy translates into real-world human-centric experiences.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Training // Track 1 - Snowflake GenAI Essentials Training
abstract: Spend the day learning how to turn your data into intelligent, AI-powered results with gen AI on Snowflake. Build, prompt and deploy real generative AI solutions to deliver measurable impact, all in a secure, scalable, environment.

title: Training // Track 2 - Snowflake Accelerator Training
abstract: Jumpstart your platform skills with this one-day Summit course for early to intermediate users. We'll move beyond theory with a hands-on exploration of curated, key features, from core tools to new AI. You'll leave with practical skills from Snowflake experts to solve complex problems and immediately unlock more value.

title: Training // Track 3 - Snowflake Platform Training + Exam - Monday
abstract: This session is designed for individuals with limited experience using Snowflake and provides a walkthrough of the AI Data Cloud architecture through interactive labs, guiding learners how to unify, integrate and share previously siloed data with virtually unlimited scalability and concurrency.

title: Training // Track 3 - Snowflake Platform Training + Exam - Wednesday
abstract: This session is designed for individuals with limited experience using Snowflake and provides a walkthrough of the AI Data Cloud architecture through interactive labs, guiding learners how to unify, integrate and share previously siloed data with virtually unlimited scalability and concurrency.

title: Training // Track 3 - Snowflake Platform Training + Exam - Thursday
abstract: This session is designed for individuals with limited experience using Snowflake and provides a walkthrough of the AI Data Cloud architecture through interactive labs, guiding learners how to unify, integrate and share previously siloed data with virtually unlimited scalability and concurrency.

title: Transforming Medtech with Snowflake Cortex AI Agents
abstract: Medtronic, a major global medical device manufacturer, is fully standardized on Snowflake for Data and AI, with 10+ Cortex Agents in production. Snowflake serves as their single system of intelligence, unifying all enterprise data (SAP, R&D, IoT telemetry) to achieve the "ONE Medtronic" vision and break down data silos. The company is building an agent-ready data foundation to enable an AI-first future, ensuring intelligent assistants (using the MedtronicGPT framework) operate on trusted, compliant data — a necessity for FDA/EMA regulations. This session will focus on how Medtronic has scaled AI agents companywide leveraging Snowflake.

speakers.bio: Ravi Achukola is a senior Data and AI executive and industry thought leader with deep experience transforming enterprise data into a strategic growth engine. He leads global Data and Advanced Analytics at Medtronic, shaping enterprise strategy across data platforms, advanced analytics, machine learning, Generative and Agentic AI. Ravi has led cloud‑native Data and AI transformations leveraging Snowflake and leading hyperscalers, delivering measurable impact across revenue growth, operational resilience, and risk reduction. With leadership experience spanning High Tech, Airlines, CPG, and MedTech, he is known for translating emerging technologies into practical, value‑driven outcomes.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Transforming Wealth Advisor Platforms with Snowflake
abstract: Citigroup's One Wealth initiative is revolutionizing the wealth management landscape by consolidating over 200 legacy systems into a unified, high-performance data platform. This session explores how Citi is leveraging the power of the Snowflake AI Data Cloud to create a next-generation adviser experience that eliminates data inertia and drives proactive client engagement.

speakers.job_title: Head of Data Analytics & AI, Citi Wealth
speakers.bio: Joseph Bonanno is the Global Head of Data Analytics and Innovation for Citi Wealth. In this role, he is responsible for overseeing the data strategy, analytics, AI, machine learning, and other innovation initiatives.  Prior to this, he served as the Head of Data, Digital, and Innovation at Citi Security Services, where he led the cloud and data strategy, artificial intelligence, analytics and reporting, blockchain and digital asset technology, along with strategic investments and client-facing systems.

times.room: Vertical Village Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Truist and Cognizant: Operationalizing AI at Scale with Trusted Enterprise Data
abstract: As enterprises shift from AI experimentation to scaled production, trusted and well‑governed data becomes essential. In this session, Truist and Cognizant share how they are modernizing their data ecosystem on the Snowflake AI Data Cloud to enable secure, governed, real‑time access for analytics, machine learning and generative AI. Learn how unified data, strengthened governance and cloud‑native architecture accelerate AI adoption, ensure compliance, and deliver responsible, explainable and enterprise‑grade AI at scale.

speakers.bio:
ransformational Data and Analytics executive with solid quantitative background & strong business acumen, driving business transformation, and growth through the power of Big Data, Analytics, and Leadership.

Executive experience in Product, Technology, and Business Development organizations. Proven track record in planning/executing mission-critical business initiatives, and achieving profitability objectives. Have built Data, Analytics, and Information Technology functions from scratch and lead multiple business/technology transformations delivering impactful solutions for complex business problems globally.

speakers.job_title: Head of Data, AI and Automation Tech
speakers.bio: My career has been defined by the enjoyment of developing technical solutions to diverse business challenges. Now, I'm eager to bring that same enthusiasm to organizations pursuing transformational objectives, where I can apply my holistic technology expertise. I've delivered successful outcomes across the entire IT spectrum—Software Engineering, Data & Analytics, AI/ML, IT Operations, and Cybersecurity—in Banking, Insurance, and Logistics. This breadth of experience empowers me to tackle complex problems and drive results that create efficiency, boost revenue, mitigate risk, and cultivate future leaders.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Trust by Design: How Toyota Motor Europe Governs Data and AI on Snowflake
abstract: Toyota Motor Europe reveals how it uses the Snowflake AI Data Cloud to build the disciplined, trust-first foundations needed for responsible AI. Inspired by the principle that AI is “20% fun, 80% hard work,” Toyota shows how strong governance with Snowflake Horizon Catalog, GDPR alignment, consent management, and EU Data and AI Act readiness are embedded into every data product. Learn how Toyota’s governed data mesh and data marketplace democratize high-quality, compliant data so teams can innovate confidently and sustainably across the organization.

speakers.bio: Lana Kern leads the Data & AI team at Toyota Germany, driving the company’s data strategy and enabling the business to make data‑driven decisions. She advances Toyota’s data transformation by developing compliant data products, fostering cross‑functional collaboration, and accelerating the adoption of modern analytics and AI capabilities across the organization.

Joined Toyota in 2001, I have spent 20 years in R&D designing cars. Geek with passion for Technology and Data. Leading Toyota Motor Europe's Data Mesh initiative. My mission: To make the company future-proof in Data and AI.

title: Trusting OT Data in Snowflake: How Devon Energy Ensures PI Data Quality at Scale
abstract: Devon Energy is migrating large volumes of PI historian data to Snowflake to support analytics, AI and reporting. Ensuring data quality — completeness, timeliness and accuracy — at scale is a key challenge. Manual checks don’t scale, so Devon partnered with Aperio to automate quality assurance. Aperio’s machine learning models detect drift, flatlines, noise and anomalies at the source, while its consistency product validates data alignment between PI and Snowflake. This closed-loop system ensures trusted, high-quality OT data for downstream analytics and decision-making.

speakers.bio: Don Morrison is a real-time systems architect at Devon Energy in Oklahoma City, OK. In his role, Don and his teammates integrate data from field operations into numerous business systems that enable operational intelligence across five operating areas. Don came to Devon Energy in 2014 and has 24 years of experience in engineering, software architecture, and automation system design. Don holds a Master of Science in Systems Engineering from the University of Southern California and a Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering from Oklahoma State University.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 1 (Basecamp, South Hall A)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Turn Your Data into Revenue: How to Achieve Real-Time Marketing and AI
abstract: Want more impact from your data team without adding tools, people or complexity? Learn how Fivetran Activations turns analytics-ready Snowflake data into real-time action. Operationalize trusted data for LLM workflows, power efficient go-to-market motions and personalize marketing, all without manual work. Hear how BraunAbility scaled AI across the U.S. and Europe by unifying data from multiple ERPs and Salesforce instances, simplifying their stack, accelerating integrations and empowering revenue teams with timely insights.

speakers.bio:
Marty is passionate about helping organizations move from “data‑rich” to AI‑enabled. As a leader in AI and data architecture, he designs enterprise solutions that activate warehouse data for advanced analytics and GenAI—without adding unnecessary complexity.

In this session with Fivetran, Marty shares how modern data movement and architecture patterns unlock Retrieval‑Augmented Generation (RAG) and AI at scale, transforming Snowflake data warehouses into dynamic, AI‑ready foundations. He is especially energized by guiding teams from experimentation to durable, business‑aligned AI solutions.

Alex Hauer is a Principal Product Marketing Manager at Fivetran. In his role, he helps leading data-driven organizations understand how Fivetran can help them achieve analytical and AI-driven success. Alex has been with Fivetran since mid-2022 and was previously at Salesforce via the acquisition of Datorama. Feel free to ask him about his latest travels or book he finished reading.

title: Under the Hood of Snowflake Hybrid Tables
abstract: Peek under the hood to learn how hybrid table architecture delivers mission-critical performance and reliability for your applications while reducing build time and complexity through full native Snowflake database integration. We also dive into recent optimizations that have delivered a 10x improvement in transactional workload performance.

speakers.bio: Tech lead for FDB Storage and Hybid Tables

Nikos is an engineer with expertise in scalable and distributed data management systems. Nikos has worked extensively in query planning, compilation, and execution optimization in disaggregated storage settings. Nikos' interests lie in performance, accuracy, and availability of real-time and analytical data management for traditional and agent workflows for AI applications (e.g., agentic AI applications). Nikos has been a founding member of the Unistore team.

title: Under the Hood: Snowflake Feature Store for Real-Time ML
abstract: Achieving real-time ML requires low-latency feature retrieval and support for streaming sources. In this session, we showcase how Snowflake’s online feature store unifies batch, streaming and on-demand feature views all in one platform. Join to learn how we deconstruct engineering hurdles — from optimizing high-throughput streaming to managing synchronization with the offline feature store. Expect raw benchmarks, architectural trade-offs and a close-up look at how we built our serving engine. We will present a case study of how a customer migrated a real-time ML workload from Tecton to Snowflake in production.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 3 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Unified Ingestion: Redefining Economics for Batch and Streaming Ingestion
abstract: For years, data engineers had to choose between batch and streaming based on costs, not latency. No more. In this deep dive, we pull back the curtain on the unified ingestion platform that powers Snowflake’s file-based and streaming workloads. We will explore the architectural overhaul and optimizations that allowed us to provide the new data volume-based pricing model for ingestion that redefines economics. Learn how the new pricing model reflects true resource consumption, providing better predictability and lower TCO.

speakers.bio: Ankit Gupta leads Product Management for Snowflake's core ingestion platform and services, including COPY and Snowpipe. He is passionate about creating seamless experiences that enable organizations to onboard petabytes of batch and streaming data into Apache Iceberg and Snowflake tables to establish a unified data foundation that fuels enterprise AI. Prior to Snowflake, he led product for Amazon MSK's connectivity and integration suite, including MSK Connect, MSK Replicator, and AWS Glue Schema Registry. Ankit holds an MBA from UCLA Anderson and a B.Tech from Delhi Technological University.

Boris Trofimov is a Director of Engineering at HUMAN Security with 20+ years of experience in commercial software development. For the past decade, he has specialized in architecting high-load, scalable data platforms within the AdTech and Cybersecurity domains.

A passionate Snowflake advocate, Boris focuses on large-scale migrations, performance and cost optimization, and data democratization via the Snowflake Marketplace. He holds a PhD in IT, co-founded the Odessa Java User Group, and is a veteran speaker at international conferences like Devoxx, as well as events across Latin America and Ukraine.

title: Unifying Data and AI Orchestration with Apache Airflow
abstract: In a high-pressure kitchen, great service depends on mise en place: everything prepared, organized and ready before the rush. Production AI demands the same discipline. This talk shows how Apache Airflow unifies data pipelines and AI workflows in one observable control plane and system of record, replacing fragmented observability, failure handling and team handoffs. We'll walk through real projects covering multi-agent orchestration, AI-driven pipeline branching and vision model integration, each illustrating a distinct production pattern, all running on Airflow. The best kitchens don't just execute recipes, they make the rush look effortless.

speakers.bio: Volker is a Senior Developer Advocate at Astronomer with 15 years of experience architecting data platforms. Previously in the gaming industry, he built systems processing billions of daily events using Apache Airflow. Now, he leverages this expertise to define modern orchestration, data engineering, and AI best practices and empower the global data community. As a regular speaker at international conferences, Volker blends deep technical knowledge with a passion for communication and knowledge sharing.

title: Uniting SAP and Snowflake for a Zero-Copy and AI-Driven Enterprise
abstract: Join us alongside a customer as they reveal how the new integration between SAP Business Data Cloud and Snowflake is redefining what’s possible for business innovation and AI. Discover how organizations are seamlessly connecting SAP and Snowflake with zero-copy bidirectional data sharing, unlocking governed and real-time data for every workload, from analytics to enterprise AI. Get a first look at how bringing governed, semantically rich SAP data together with Snowflake is empowering teams to break down silos, accelerate decisions and fuel the next generation of intelligent business, all through the power of an open and collaborative data ecosystem.

speakers.bio: Gopal Raghavan is the Global Solutions Engineering Leader for the Snowflake SAP partnership.  He was formerly a Field CTO specializing in Collaboration and Business Continuity at Snowflake. He has over 25 years of experience working with customers worldwide to help them accomplish their business objectives using data, AI and analytics. His areas of interest at Snowflake include Iceberg tables, dynamic tables, universal listings (private, marketplace and organizational) and related monetization strategies.

times.room: Basecamp South Theater 4 (Basecamp, South Hall C)

Note: Seating for this session will be on a first-come, first-served basis

title: Unlock Full Data and AI Potential with a Truly Open and Interoperable Lakehouse
abstract: Apache Iceberg is the foundation for open lakehouses, but consistent governance and true interoperability remain a challenge. This dedicated session cuts through the noise to detail Horizon Catalog’s competitive differentiation. Learn how bringing Polaris inside Horizon creates a truly open ecosystem and why our Iceberg REST Catalog supports secure read/write access across engines. We showcase our leadership in extending fine-grained governance, including enforcing row and column policies on external engines like Apache Spark. Snowflake Horizon is the only catalog that is fully interoperable, future-proof beyond Iceberg and offers zero lock-in for your evolving AI workloads.

speakers.bio: Anupriti has spent over a decade working across the data ecosystem, focusing on enterprise infrastructure products ranging from high-scale storage and lake analytics to data and AI governance. She is passionate about helping customers innovate and building solutions that solve problems at scale. She is based out of Seattle, Washington, and enjoys spending time with family and friends, cooking, and creating DIY crafts.

Ashwin Kamath leads the product efforts for Snowflake Horizon Catalog, Apache Polaris and Apache Iceberg investments at Snowflake. Before Snowflake, Ashwin held product management roles at Google and Microsoft, where he worked on observability, streaming analytics technologies and developer experience. Ashwin holds a master’s degree in computer science from University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign.

title: Unlocking RAG on Complex Documents: LandingAI Meets Snowflake Intelligence
abstract: You can stand up a RAG pipeline in an afternoon. Getting it to work on real documents — dense tables, scientific figures and irregular layouts  — takes months. This session cuts that gap. We'll walk through an end-to-end workflow using LandingAI's Agentic Document Extraction to parse complex, multimodal content and extract structured key-value pairs that break conventional tools. From there, you'll see how parsed output feeds the Snowflake Cortex AI stack — Cortex Search, Cortex Analyst and Cortex Agents — all surfaced through Snowflake Intelligence. The entire stack is created with one SQL script and can be versioned for your own use case in about half a day.

speakers.bio: Andrea Kropp is an Applied AI Engineer at LandingAI with over 15 years' experience delivering end-to-end solutions that transform raw data into critical business insights. She works with enterprise customers across the financial services, healthcare and energy industries to design intelligent document processing pipelines for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and field extraction use cases. Andrea is a co-instructor of the short course, Document AI: From OCR to Agentic Doc Extraction on the DeepLearning.AI platform. She holds a BS and MS in Chemistry, is an avid tennis player and loves to travel.

title: Unlocking the Fortress: Real-Time Oracle CDC for the Risk-Averse Enterprise
abstract: Oracle databases hold critical enterprise data, but extracting it in real-time often breaks the bank — or the database. In this deep dive, we reveal the architecture of Snowflake’s native Oracle CDC connector using XStream to minimize source impact. We will dissect the technical journey of overcoming deep-seated DBA and security friction, share internal performance benchmarks, and demonstrate a pipeline configuration that keeps security teams happy and data fresh. Leave with a blueprint for modernizing your most critical legacy workloads using Snowflake Openflow.

speakers.bio: Jakub Puchalski is a Staff Product Manager at Snowflake, leading the development of real-time CDC database and SaaS connectors on Openflow that power seamless and scalable data ingestion into the Data Cloud. With a strong technical foundation and a focus on solving real-world data challenges, Jakub is helping shape the future of cloud-native data movement.

Dan Goodman is a Staff Engineer at Yes Energy, which delivers Yes Energy’s high quality power market data catalog to consumers through the Snowflake Marketplace. Dan has several years of experience in database administration, architecture, and development using both Oracle and Snowflake.

title: Using MCP to Orchestrate Composable Identity Resolution at Scale
abstract: Enterprise‑scale identity resolution demands coordinated matching, enrichment and hygiene across regions and compliance boundaries. This session shows how Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables composable architectures that link Snowflake’s native capabilities with region‑specific identity engines. Using dentsu’s approach with Cortex Search and intelligent routing, we’ll illustrate how MCP orchestrates complex workflows without moving sensitive data, scaling globally while preserving sovereignty and accelerating insight.

speakers.bio: Morten Lileng is the Global Head of Engineering at dentsu, leading global teams building data and identity products for the world's largest marketers and pushing AI-first engineering at scale. He focuses on composable Snowflake Native Apps, privacy-safe identity resolution, and agentic engineering workflows. Morten is a returning Snowflake Summit speaker who brings real architecture decisions and production experience to every talk.

title: Using Snowflake for Advanced Spend Analytics at AT&T
abstract: This session will cover AT&T's deployment of Snowflake Cortex AI Agentic AI to automate technical sourcing data reconciliation within AT&T's Global Supply Chain division. It will feature use cases including contract processing, supplier unification, compliance automation, intelligent search and risk monitoring. Results have included eliminating 48,000 annual hours of manual work and saving $2.4 million yearly, while sourcing managers reclaim 24 hours weekly for strategic tasks.

speakers.bio: April is a technology and data leader within AT&T’s Transformation & Supply Chain organization, focused on building modern, scalable data platforms that drive business impact. April leads organizational data strategies, AI‑ready data architecture, and the development of data products that deliver actionable insights. Her work centers on AI/ML‑enabled analytics, Snowflake‑based cloud platforms, agentic use cases, and the migration of legacy data platforms to a trusted single source of truth. April and her team also empower AT&T Supply Chain and other AT&T organizations through advanced spend and operational analytics to drive efficiency, cost savings, and large‑scale transformation.

title: Using the Org Account to Protect Data Products in the Internal Marketplace
abstract: Sharing data internally doesn’t compromise on security or speed. This session showcases a hands-on approach to protecting sensitive data products using Snowflake Organization User Groups. We’ll show how centralized governance via the organization account can be pushed down to local accounts seamlessly and how to use this to protect your sensitive data. Learn how to architect row-access and masking policies that reference org-level groups, ensuring consistent protection across your entire data mesh to automate data privacy and scale your internal marketplace with confidence.

speakers.bio: I am a Solutions Architect at Snowflake, serving as an RSA for three global organizations. In this role, I guide them through their Snowflake implementation journey while helping establish best practices. With a strong background in data and AI/ML, I provide tailored solutions and actionable insights to address diverse business needs.

Matthias is a Field CTO at Snowflake and serves as a trusted advisor to clients and partners worldwide. He has more than 25 years of experience in the design, engineering, and deployment of database and analytics technology. Matthias focuses on Enterprise Data Architecture, Data Collaboration, Data Governance, and he leads the Data Mesh field strategy at Snowflake. Prior to joining Snowflake Matthias worked for 21 years at IBM in the US and across Europe.

title: VideoAmp’s Unified MLOps Architecture on Snowflake
abstract: For many enterprises, the barrier to ML value isn't a lack of data; it’s the "operational tax" of fragmented environments. VideoAmp has overcome this by consolidating the entire ML lifecycle — from feature engineering to real-time inference — within the Snowflake AI Data Cloud. This session provides a technical deep dive into VideoAmp’s environment design, focusing on how it eliminated egress costs and cross-platform silos to build a production-ready home for diverse ML use cases at scale.

speakers.bio: Andrew Harding is a Principal Machine Learning Engineer at VideoAmp, specializing in ML Ops and data pipelines. With a background in data engineering, Andrew builds the systems that gets models from ideas into production. When not debugging, Andrew can be found reading sci-fi or out hiking local trails.

title: What Apache Iceberg Is Bad at Doing … for Now
abstract: Apache Iceberg is rapidly becoming the open source table standard, but popularity doesn’t mean everyone is using it efficiently. The format has some strong features — ACID, metric pushdowns and hidden partitioning — but it also has some dangerous anti-patterns. In this talk we’ll go over bloated metadata, inefficient metrics and missing maintenance bogging down query planning. You’ll learn why these issues plague users of Iceberg tables and how to avoid them. Finally, we’ll also go through how some of these problems won’t even be an issue in version 4 with new proposals like “single file commits.” Come and learn to avoid pitfalls like a pro and get a preview of what’s coming next!

speakers.bio: Russell Spitzer received his Ph.D from UCSF in 2013 after performing a lot of comparisons of protein binding sites. Following that, he became deeply invested in distributed computing and involved in several Apache projects. While working at Datastax he was a key contributor to the DataStax Spark-Cassandra Connector and also worked on many Apache projects. After leaving Datastax he worked at Apple growing the then nascent Apache Iceberg project where he worked on data file management and advancing the table format. Currently Russell working on OSS software at Snowflake and is a PMC member of the Apache Iceberg project, and PPMC member of the Apache Polaris (Incubating) project.

title: What Powers Your Platform? Capital One’s Shift to Composable Marketing
abstract: Financial services marketers must balance speed and security. Capital One shows the way: By giving marketers self-serve access to unified data with a composable CDP, it cuts time to market for complex campaigns by 75%. In this session, learn how Capital One centralized data in Snowflake, connecting all customer interactions into profiles that can support acquisition strategies; democratized access with Hightouch’s composable CDP, while maintaining strict governance and security; and activated data across campaigns to drive more cross-sells, more efficient paid media and more personalized customer experiences.

speakers.bio: Bill has been with Capital One since 2019. Bill led the Small Business Acquisitions Marketing team for over 3 years and spent the next 3 years leading Small Business MarTech & Customer Marketing where he was responsible for all communications with our existing customer base. Currently, Bill oversees Marketing Transformation efforts and is responsible for converging all lines of business within Capital One onto a single MarTech stack. Before Capital One, Bill spent 6 years working in Retail for Sears Holdings and held a variety of Marketing roles. Bill is based out of the DC area and lives in Reston, VA with his wife, Angela, and their twin four and a half year old boys.

Note: This session is currently open to Marketer's Forum registrants. Registration will open to the general event audience closer to the event.

title: What's New: Agentic ML in Snowflake
abstract: Learn how the latest agentic capabilities in Snowflake ML accelerate productivity for your ML teams. Join us for new feature overviews and a live demo showcasing our latest innovations.

speakers.bio: Pavan Pothukuchi is the Director of Product Management for AI at Snowflake. His teams are responsible for Snowflake ML, which is a set of capabilities that enable ML practitioners to productionize machine learning workflows end-to-end inside Snowflake.  He is in addition responsible for Document AI, which is an AI product that enables business users to extract information from unstructured documents and automate business processes, and AI observability - which enables developers to build safe and trusted AI applications.

Jeffrey Geevarghese is a Senior Manager at Snowflake and leads engineering for Snowflake ML. Previously, he led several machine learning teams at Amazon Web Services (AWS). He has a graduate degree in Computer science specializing in Machine Learning from University of Massachusetts, Amherst.

title: What’s New: AI-Powered Observability at Scale with Observe by Snowflake
abstract: Snowflake is redefining observability with Observe by Snowflake, a lakehouse-native observability platform. Learn how organizations can scale cost-effectively to hundreds of terabytes of telemetry per day and share data across business and operational contexts using Apache Iceberg on a telemetry lakehouse foundation. We will demo AI SRE, an AI agent that accelerates enterprise reliability workflows, and show how teams can engage with it through the Observe UI or coding agents to boost engineering productivity.

speakers.bio: Gabriel Tavridis is Head of Product, Observability at Snowflake, following the acquisition of Observe where he was SVP of Product. He focuses on AI-powered observability at enterprise scale, built on open standards like Apache Iceberg and OpenTelemetry. Before Observe, he was SVP of Product at Imply Data, where he scaled a real-time analytics SaaS from zero to $10M ARR and grew an open source community to 1,500+ new organizations annually. Earlier, he led product for Salesforce's internal observability platform, growing MAUs 4x and onboarding 100+ engineering teams. He holds an MBA from Stanford GSB.

title: What's New: Connectivity for AI-Ready Pipelines, with AI
abstract: To power AI, your data needs a nerve system. For data engineers, the challenge is going beyond the cost of moving data to focus on the instant value of AI activation. This session explores how Snowflake’s connectivity offerings act as the connective tissue for your entire data estate, transforming pipelines from a bottleneck into a strategic enabler. We’ll dive into our AI-driven approach to automation and the latest engineering breakthroughs across replication, streaming ingestion, file processing and zero-copy integration sharing. Learn how to build the connective tissue required to light up AI across the enterprise.

speakers.bio: Saptarshi (Sap) Mukherjee leads Product Management for Data Connectivity and Streaming platforms at Snowflake, where he owns connectivity, core ingest, and streaming. His team builds native, high-performance ingestion and integration capabilities for enterprise apps, SaaS, databases, and streaming data, making data movement into Snowflake reliable and scalable. With over 20 years in data platforms, analytics, and AI, Sap previously held product leadership roles at Google and Microsoft. He holds a bachelor’s in computer science engineering and a master’s in information management.

With over a decade of experience as a technical developer educator, Gilberto has made complex concepts accessible to over 100,000 students and engineers through both online learning platforms and in-person experiences.


He's worn multiple hats as both product manager and developer educator at developer-loved tech companies, including MongoDB, Domino Data Lab, Plaid, and Snowflake.


Gilberto is passionate about crafting exceptional developer experiences and educational resources. He frequently writes about data engineering, AI, and application development.


Connect with him on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/gilberto-hernandez/

title: What’s New: Innovate at Scale: Powering a Secure and Resilient AI Estate
abstract: This session unveils Snowflake innovations designed to protect sensitive AI assets and ensure continuity. Explore what’s new in security for AI, featuring capabilities to secure Cortex flows, Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Code. We’ll cover AI security posture in the Trust Center, adaptive guardrails against prompt injection and sandboxing to mitigate local risks. Learn about new data movement policies to prevent exfiltration and seamless agent access via agentic identity and JIT provisioning as well as Account Replication 2.0, the next evolution of our BCDR engine. Join us to see these features in action and build a bulletproof, AI-ready estate.

speakers.bio: Nitika Gupta, Director of Product Management for Security at Snowflake, is an experienced product leader and security expert. Previously, she led the Microsoft Entra Security PM team responsible for highly leveraged security products like Conditional Access and Identity Protection.

Sid Ramadoss is the Product lead for Snowgrid and Backups at Snowflake which provides resilience platform. Snowgrid is the replication framework that enables cross-cloud and cross-region capabilities such as business continuity, collaboration and migrations.

title: What's New: Innovations in Data and AI Governance
abstract: Maintain rigorous control while scaling your AI enterprise. In this session, we dive into the latest innovations within Snowflake Horizon Catalog, our universal AI catalog with context and governance for AI over all data. Learn how Horizon Catalog powers the semantic layer to bridge the gap between technical metadata and business meaning. We also cover enhancements to interoperability and embedded open APIs (Apache Polaris and Iceberg REST Catalog), ensuring consistent policy enforcement across your entire data estate. Finally, we explore core governance, including external lineage updates, automated data classification and data quality monitoring.

title: What's New in Snowflake AI Research
abstract: Snowflake AI Research is focused on delivering high-quality, efficient AI for real enterprise workloads — bridging state-of-the-art research with practical product impact. In this session, we’ll share what’s new across our research agenda, highlighting key directions including model-agent co-design to improve task success and reliability, vertical models specialized for enterprise use cases, and advanced training and inference systems that enhance usability and performance while reducing cost and latency. We’ll also share principles and lessons learned, and how research transitions into production to help teams build trustworthy AI applications on governed data at scale.

speakers.bio:
Samyam Rajbhandari currently leads the inference optimization efforts at Snowflake for reducing latency and cost. Previously, he co-led the design and development of Snowflake Arctic, an innovative foundation model achieving state-of-the-art enterprise intelligence with best-in-class cost efficiency at the time of release. 

Prior to Snowflake, Samyam was a co-founder and the system architect for DeepSpeed at Microsoft, where he developed transformational system technologies like  ZeRO and 3D parallelism for training LLMs, and led inference optimization effort for models like Dalle.2 and Dall.E 3 models from OpenAI.

Yuxiong He is a Distinguished AI Engineer and Research Scientist at Snowflake, leading research on foundation models, agentic systems, and high-performance AI infrastructure for enterprise applications. She and her team built Arctic LLM and Arctic Text2SQL, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy, and developed Arctic’s inference and training stack with industry-leading efficiency. Their work powers Snowflake Intelligence, Cortex Agents, Search, and Inference systems. A strong advocate for open source and open research, she co-founded DeepSpeed at Microsoft, pioneering ZeRO and 3D parallelism. She has published over 100 papers and received best paper awards at SIGIR, ICDE, and WSDM.

title: What’s New: Modernizing Your Data Estate in Snowflake
abstract: Modernizing a legacy data estate is complex — from embedded procedural logic to large-scale data movement and tight timelines. In this session, discover what’s new in helping organizations accelerate modernization on Snowflake. We’ll showcase the latest innovations that reduce risk, disruption and manual effort through automation, AI-assisted remediation, workload analysis and database virtualization. Learn how teams migrate with greater flexibility, minimize code rewrites, validate with confidence and transition to a scalable, cloud-native foundation for analytics and AI.

speakers.bio: Federico Zoufaly helps Snowflake customers accelerate their adoption of the platform from his position as Director of Migrations and Modernizations @Snowflake. Federico previously served as Co-Founder  of Mobilize.Net. Federico has been a leader in modernizing software applications for almost 30 years.  With a technical background (Ph.D.c. from the University of Florida with a Fulbright scholarship and M.Sc. from the Technology Institute of Costa Rica) and hands-on management in real world technology projects, Federico is passionate about the intersection of the technical challenges and the business need for modernization. Federico is a fellow of the Aspen Global Leadership Network.

Rima joined Snowflake via the acquisition of Datometry, a pioneer in database virtualization. At Snowflake, Rima is launching Datometry, a critical product differentiator and migration accelerator for enterprises moving away from legacy databases like Teradata. Prior to Snowflake, Rima has worked with Fortune 500 companies and CSPs on mission critical legacy platform modernizations and migrations, leveraging Datometry. Additionally, Rima helped build out the State Local Government business at Apptio for Technology Business Management. At Microsoft she led two successful go to market programs for which she twice received the Platinum Club Award for Excellence.

abstract: Stop wrestling with pipelines to move data between transactional and analytical silos. title: What's New: Simplify Warehouse Management with Snowflake Compute Capabilities
abstract: Experience the next generation of the Snowflake engine as it evolves into a single, adaptive compute layer. This session explores how this unified architecture drives price performance, delivering the latest hardware and software innovation to run more queries for the same cost with operational ease and lower TCO. Discover how Interactive tables and warehouses eliminate manual tuning for sub-second, high-concurrency analytics, and how Snowpipe Streaming and CDC simplify production-ready pipelines — all with the ease and zero operational overhead of a fully managed platform.

speakers.bio: Christine Avanessians is the Director of Product Management at Snowflake, where she leads the strategy for the core data warehouse/compute, FinOps, organization management, and billing platforms. A veteran of the cloud industry, Christine has spent her career scaling demanding compute environments. Before joining Snowflake, she managed the core infrastructure powering the Netflix experience and spent a decade at Microsoft, driving innovation across Azure’s compute and confidential computing portfolios, as well as Dynamics enterprise applications.

title: What's New: Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Agents for Real Business Impact
abstract: AI is only as powerful as the context it understands. In this session, discover what’s new in Snowflake Intelligence, the enterprise intelligence agent built to turn all your knowledge into trusted business decisions. Learn how organizations are moving beyond dashboards to uncover the critical “why,” grounded in governed enterprise data and shared business definitions. Discover how Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Agents work together to move teams beyond static insights, enabling natural language intelligence, trusted reasoning and actionable workflows that help organizations analyze, act and drive outcomes with confidence.

speakers.bio: Arun Agarwal is currently leading the AI product marketing initiatives at Snowflake. title: What's New: Unify Your Lakehouse with Snowflake Simple Interoperability
abstract: Pipeline sprawl and unnecessary data movement are the hidden taxes to your AI innovation. Discover how Snowflake’s platform empowers you to work from a single governed copy of data, wherever it lives. Built on native support for Apache Polaris™ and Apache Iceberg™, this session explores how to power new use cases with Iceberg V3 support and virtually eliminate storage complexity with Snowflake Storage, all without vendor lock-in. We'll also cover strategies to reduce governance and security risks by building a metadata-driven universal governance model to fuel human and agentic intelligence with access to governed, live data.

title: When AI Runs the Journey: How MoneyLion Simplified Marketing at Scale
abstract:
AI isn’t a tool: It’s an opportunity to reinvent business models, team roles and relationships with customers. In this session, hear from leaders at MoneyLion who have transformed their business with AI. They’ll share:

  • How they’ve replaced thousands of fragmented journeys and competing product priorities with customer-first marketing via AI Decisioning on Snowflake
  • How marketing, analytics, engineering and data science teams are collaborating and redefining their roles as AI allows them to delegate manual tasks
  • The pragmatic playbook they’ve developed to test and adopt impactful AI within Snowflake and the larger martech ecosystem

speakers.bio:
Peter Grimm leads Marketing Data and Technology Platforms at MoneyLion, responsible for activating Snowflake data to power acquisition, retention, and analytics use cases. He partners with Marketing, Product, Data Engineering and Business Intelligence teams to modernize MoneyLion’s customer data foundation – driving effective personalization at scale and implementing rigorous measurement frameworks to quantify true incrementality.  

At Snowflake Summit, Peter will share MoneyLion’s experience using Hightouch AI Decisioning on Snowflake and practical lessons learned while evolving personalization and measurement in a composable, warehouse-first martech stack.

title: Wingman: Delivering Governed, Agentic AI for Business Leaders on Snowflake
abstract: Business leaders need answers, not dashboards. Wingman is a Snowflake-native, agentic AI built for retail and CPG teams that delivers governed, multi-step reasoning across trusted enterprise data. Powered by Snowflake Intelligence, Cortex Analyst, Cortex Search and semantic views, Wingman uses multiple domain-specific agents to translate natural language into explainable insights — while enforcing role-based access and data controls. In this session, we walk through the product architecture, agent orchestration model and lessons learned from piloting Wingman, including reducing ad-hoc requests, accelerating analyst turnaround and driving adoption in under eight weeks.

speakers.bio: Sameer Ailawadi is a BI & Analytics Architect with over 15 years of experience designing and delivering enterprise data platforms that power large-scale analytics and business insights. He has led enterprise BI and modern data initiatives across global organizations including PepsiCo, Ulta Beauty, Walgreens, and Red Bull. His work spans traditional BI with SAP Business Intelligence to modern cloud data architectures on Snowflake. Recently, he has been focused on enabling AI-driven analytics by leveraging capabilities such as Snowflake Cortex to bring LLM-powered insights directly into enterprise data platforms, helping organizations unlock faster, smarter decision-making at scale.

Neil Vincent is the Manager of Enterprise Data & Analytics at Red Bull North America, where he focuses on bringing analytics and AI capabilities closer to business teams. Working alongside the platform architecture led by Sameer, Neil drives the business adoption of initiatives like Wingman, helping translate complex data capabilities into practical tools for decision-making. His work centers on connecting data platforms, analytics programs, and business teams to ensure insights are accessible and actionable across the organization.
