title: Write Once, Enforce Everywhere: Interoperable Governance with Iceberg
abstract:
Interoperable governance means enforcing consistent row- and column-level security across all engines without fragmenting policies. How do you achieve this when engines differ? This talk presents two approaches via Apache Iceberg:

  • Catalog-enforced FGAC using scan planning to return policy-enforced files
  • ReadRestrictions standardization where catalogs return policy expressions engines interpreted for deeper integration

You'll learn catalog-side architecture, including scan planning and policy evaluation. You'll also learn about two paths: transparent enforcement vs ReadRestrictions standardization — and trade-offs around interoperable governance.

speakers.bio: Vishwa Lakkundi leads the Open Analytics, Catalog Platform and Catalog Integration Platform teams at Snowflake, driving the strategy for  open data standards. Before joining Snowflake, Vishwa spent over thirteen years at Apple leading engineering for Apple’s Data Lakehouse and Data Orchestration platforms. He has extensive experience building elastic, fully managed Lakehouse platforms, AI infrastructures and large-scale distributed systems.

PMC @ Apache Iceberg and Committer @ Apache Polaris
presently working in OSS team @ Snowflake
past worked @ AWS, on integrating Apache Iceberg to AWS Redshift, DataFirehose, EMR

title: Your AI Strategy Has a Context Problem. Orchestration Solves It.
abstract:
Every enterprise has an AI strategy. Few have the foundation to deliver on it. Most treat context as something defined upfront and consumed downstream. Semantic layers define metrics. Ontologies model relationships. Neither captures what actually ran.

Enterprise context is not static. It is evidence that accumulates continuously: which pipelines ran, what changed upstream, what humans corrected. Orchestration is the only layer where that evidence accumulates naturally, not as declared truth, but as a continuous record of what actually happened. That is what production AI runs on. This talk outlines the architectural pattern that makes it possible.

speakers.bio: Steven Hillion has been leading engineering and analytics teams for twenty years. He is currently Head of Data at Astronomer, the New York-based company behind Apache Airflow, the popular open-source data orchestration platform. Previously, he founded Alpine Data, a collaborative data science platform later acquired by TIBCO. Before that he built a global data science team at Pivotal and developed a suite of open-source and enterprise software in machine learning. Earlier, he led engineering at several successful start-ups. Originally from Guernsey, Steven now lives in San Francisco. He holds a Ph.D. in Mathematics from UC Berkeley, and read mathematics at Oxford University.

title: Your Data Platform Is Now Your App Platform: 300% ROI with Snowflake, Sigma, dbt
abstract: Building customer applications used to require buying and maintaining an entire application stack — cost, complexity and technical debt included. Lumelight, a national employee benefits firm, skipped all of that. Working with Coastal, Lumelight used Snowflake, Sigma and dbt to turn its own claims and reporting data into two revenue-generating applications, delivering 300% ROI at 60%-70% margins. In this session, see how they built it and how you can turn your own data into a product your customers will pay for.

title: Zero-ETL Citizen Development on Snowflake: Apps with Postgres and SPCS
abstract: Business teams need to build applications faster while keeping their data ready for analytics and AI. This session presents a modern application stack on Snowflake that connects applications and data on a single platform. It explores how Snowflake Postgres and Snowpark Container Services enable new application patterns, and how open-source ecosystems and multiple technical communities — PostgreSQL, low-code OSS and the Snowflake community — can collaborate to accelerate innovation. The session highlights Snowflake’s evolution beyond analytics into a unified platform for applications, data and community-driven development.

speakers.bio: Kosuke Kida is a Snowflake Data Superhero and a long-time contributor to the PostgreSQL community, serving as Chair of the Japan PostgreSQL User Group. He focuses on bridging application development, open-source ecosystems, and modern data platforms, helping communities explore how Snowflake can evolve beyond analytics into a unified platform for applications and data.

title: Zero Latency Analytics on Demand: Scaling BI Expertise with Agentic AI
abstract: Whatnot is the fastest-growing online marketplace ever. It's a live shopping platform where users spend 80 to 90 minutes per day, and sellers host over 20 million minutes of live content every week. Data and BI teams find themselves buried in ad-hoc reporting requests: "Can you check this query?" "What happened with this seller?" Every answer pulled them away from strategic partnership with business leaders. Whatnot will show how the team escaped this "help desk" trap by building an agentic Slackbot powered by Snowflake Cortex Analyst and Semantic Views. The company reduced time-to-answer to sub-minute latency and cut analyst distractions to a minimum, replacing manual triage with governed AI.

speakers.bio: Stephen Bailey is Engineering Manager for Data Platforms at Whatnot, where he leads the platforms that power intelligence, trust, and decision-making across one of the world’s fastest-growing livestream shopping marketplaces. Operating at the intersection of product, infrastructure, and data, he specializes in translating rapid business growth into systems that scale. He is especially drawn to ambitious technical problems, high-leverage platforms, and environments that move unreasonably fast.

title: Zero PII, Full Insights: Cortex AI Transforms Insurance Claims Processing
abstract: How do you unlock analytics from claims data packed with sensitive PII? Global Excel Management leverages Cortex and Horizon Catalog's AI_REDACT to automatically detect and redact personal information at scale. Join us to see our real-world architecture, chunking strategies for long documents, and how we combined AI_REDACT with other Cortex functions to build a compliant, insight-rich claims pipeline. Learn practical strategies for chunking long documents, handling edge cases and building a privacy-first data pipeline that satisfies regulators while unlocking AI-driven insights.

speakers.bio: Walid Elguebaly leads Data, Analytics & AI at Global Excel Management, where he drives AI and modern data platforms to transform insurance operations. With a Master’s in Machine Learning and experience across global organizations including Merck and GSK, he bridges deep technical expertise with strategic business outcomes.
